Small_molecule_information: 3,5,7-trihydroxy-2-(3,4,5-trihydroxyphenyl)chromen-4-one, also referred to as Myricetin, is a natural flavonol compound with the formula C15H10O8, and is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anticancer, and neuroprotective effects that binds to CAG repeat RNA, inhibiting toxic protein aggregation in Huntingtons disease. Its structure is a flavonol backbone with hydroxyl groups on the A ring (5 and 7) and C ring (3), and crucially, three hydroxyl groups on the B ring (3, 4, and 5 positions, a pyrogallol moiety), and its polyphenolic structure enables metal chelation and free radical scavenging. Myricetin is widely found in fruits, vegetables, berries, wine, and tea. Functionally, possessing six hydroxyl groups, particularly the pyrogallol structure on the B ring, Myricetin is recognized as one of the most powerful natural antioxidants known, capable of efficiently scavenging various free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative stress, and it also exhibits significant anti-inflammatory, antiviral, antitumor, and cardiovascular protective effects, often by modulating signaling pathways and enzyme activities, in addition to its anticancer and neuroprotective properties linked to its binding with CAG repeat RNA and inhibition of toxic protein aggregation in Huntingtons disease. Due to its potent antioxidant capacity, it is extensively studied and applied in nutritional supplements and natural drug development.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 3-imidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-1-ium-2-yl-7-piperazin-4-ium-1-ylchromen-2-one, also referred to as C29, is an unsubstituted dicationic coumarin derivative with the molecular formula C20H19N4O22, and it is a non-planar coumarin analog with a piperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system (note: the compound carries dicationic charges overall while C29 is described to carry a 1 charge in single-moiety characterization). Similar to 3-(7-chloroimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-1-ium-2-yl)-7-piperazin-4-ium-1-ylchromen-2-one, it carries fixed positive charges on both the piperazin-4-ium at coumarins position 7 and the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-1-ium at position 1 of the ring connected at position 3. This dicationic structure, devoid of halogen substitution, retains the maximum planar conjugated surface while ensuring extremely high water solubility and strong electrostatic interaction with negatively charged biomolecules, and it can recognize the single-G bulge of RNA1, with this binding driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand. The dicationic nature and planar aromatic system are classic strategies for designing high-affinity nucleic acid fluorescent probes, enabling them to efficiently penetrate cell membranes and specifically bind to nucleic acids. Therefore, this compound is most likely to be used as a live-cell nucleic acid dye, or as a potential anti-cancer/antiviral agent acting as a non-intercalating or intercalating nucleic acid binder.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: bis(2-acetamidoacetic acid);4-[2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)iminohydrazinyl]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Diminazine, is a complex salt molecule consisting of a large cationic formamidine hydrazonebased diamidine species and two 2-acetamidoacetic acid anions, and the ,-diamidine core connected through a biphenyl and hydrazone bridge corresponds to the class of aromatic diamidine compounds used as antiprotozoal agents that exert activity by binding to the minor groove of DNA, with such cationic diamidines commonly displaying antiprotozoal, antifungal, antiviral, anticancer, or broad-spectrum antimicrobial properties.

RNA information: The structure of MAPT pre-mRNA contains a hairpin structure located at the junction of exon 10 and intron, which is crucial for splicing regulation. Under normal circumstances, the hairpin structure is stable and helps maintain the correct splicing ratio of exon 10. However, certain mutations (such as C+14U) can disrupt the stability of hairpins, leading to excessive inclusion of exon 10 and subsequently the production of 4R-tau protein, which is associated with the neurodegenerative disease FTDP-17. The article points out that small molecule compounds can promote the excretion of exon 10 by binding to and stabilizing the mutated hairpin structure, thereby restoring the normal proportion of tau protein and having potential therapeutic effects.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[(6-amino-1-methylquinolin-1-ium-4-yl)amino]-N-[4-[(1-methylpyridin-1-ium-4-yl)amino]phenyl]benzamide, also referred to as the compound that can bind to DDPAC MAPT RNA, is a complex asymmetric dicationic molecule linking a quinolinium and a pyridinium quaternary salt via an anilino-benzamide bridge; this compound has weak affinity for DDPAC MAPT RNA and only shows a slight reduction in luciferase expression at relatively high concentrations, indicating it has a certain ability to promote the exclusion of exon 10 but with much lower activity than Compound 1 and 2 and relatively weak function, while due to its dicationic nature and multiple planar aromatic rings, it is extensively studied as a DNA-binding dye or fluorescent probe, functionally, these types of molecules are known to effectively bind into the minor groove of DNA in the cell nucleus, making them useful for fluorescence microscopy and flow cytometry to label genetic material, and its strong nucleic acid affinity also suggests potential antiproliferative activity in anticancer drug research.

RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-[3,5-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-hydroxyphenyl]phenol, also referred to as 3,3,5,5-tetrakis(2-aminoethyl) biphenyl with 4,4-diol groups (Compound 2), is a polyamine-substituted tetrasubstituted biphenol structure featuring four 2-aminoethyl groups and two phenolic hydroxyls, which is synthesized via sequential palladium-catalyzed Suzuki couplings and nitrile reduction, binds to HIV-1 RRE Bh with low affinity (ITC-measured), has no specificity for Bh over GGh, inhibits Bh-Rev interaction via a different mechanism and shows weak anti-HIV activity; this multiple amine/hydroxyl structure confers high charge and hydrophilicity at physiological pH, and since polyamines are known in biology (e.g., spermidine, spermine) for their ability to bind DNA/RNA and regulate cell proliferation, this compound, with its multivalent positive charge, is researched in the real world as a potential polyamine metabolism regulator or a DNA/RNA binding agent, with potential use in anticancer therapy (by interfering with polyamine synthesis) or as a non-viral gene delivery vector due to its ability to bind to negatively charged nucleic acids for gene therapy research.

RNA information: The bacterial riboflavin (FMN) riboswitch is located in the 5' untranslated region (5' UTR) of mRNA and consists of an aptamer domain and an expression platform. It can specifically bind to FMN and regulate the expression of the downstream key riboflavin synthesis gene ribB through conformational changes. When FMN is sufficient, the riboswitch forms a terminator/sequestering loop to inhibit gene expression; when FMN is deficient, it switches to an antiterminator/anti-sequestering loop, allowing gene transcription and translation to achieve negative feedback regulation. This riboswitch exists exclusively in bacteria and has no homologous structure in mammals, making it a highly selective target for new antibacterial drugs (such as ribocil).
Small_molecule_information: 2-[1-[[2-(methylamino)pyrimidin-5-yl]methyl]piperidin-3-yl]-4-thiophen-2-yl-1H-pyrimidin-6-one, also referred to as Ribocil, is a synthetic small molecule with an overall U-shaped rigid skeleton, this complex molecule has a substituted pyrimidin-6-one core, linked at the 2-position to a substituted piperidine ring, which is further connected to a 2-(methylamino)pyrimidinyl-methyl group, the 4-position is substituted with a thiophenyl group, its left 6-thienyl-pyrimidinone ring can intercalate into the stacking plane of the RNA double helix, while the right methylamino-piperidinyl group forms weak hydrogen bonds with key guanines, such multi-heterocyclic and complex structures are characteristic of many small-molecule drugs, this compound can highly specifically bind to the aptamer pocket of the bacterial FMN riboswitch, mimicking the conformation of the natural ligand FMN and blocking the binding of FMN to RNA, thereby relieving the negative feedback inhibition of the riboswitch on the riboflavin synthesis gene ribB, leading to impaired bacterial growth due to riboflavin depletion, and importantly, its activity can be completely reversed by exogenous riboflavin, meanwhile this compound is often designed to target specific protein kinases, by inhibiting key kinases, it may find applications in the treatment of cancer, inflammation, or autoimmune diseases.

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 1-methyl-4-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-2-[2-(3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl)ethenyl]quinolin-1-ium, also referred to as Compound 19, is a quinoline quaternary ammonium salt bearing a permanent positive charge, which is a 1-methylquinolinium derivative synthesized via general procedure B with a 3,4,5-trimethoxystyryl group at C2 and a 4-methylpiperazin-1-yl moiety at C4 of the quinoline ring; its core is the 1-methyl-4-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)quinolin-1-ium cation, linked by a vinylene bridge to a 3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl group, it binds ADAM10 mRNAs QGRS with moderate affinity but weak translational up-regulation, the trimethoxystyryl group enables partial RNA interaction yet is less potent than analogs with carbazolyl/indolyl groups, failing to robustly enhance ADAM10 expression or reduce A production, and this trimethoxyphenyl moiety is a classic pharmacophore for many antitubulin drugs (like colchicine), responsible for binding to tubulin protein, therefore, this compound may be designed to combine the nucleic acid binding or membrane disruption properties of the quaternary ammonium salt with the tubulin inhibition activity of the trimethoxyphenyl group, and in reality, such molecules could be investigated as dual-mechanism anticancer drugs, aiming to enhance cytotoxicity by simultaneously interfering with nuclear and cytoskeletal processes.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: [9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6-(diethylamino)xanthen-3-ylidene]-diethylazanium, also referred to as Rhodamine, is a xanthene derivative, typically classified as a Rhodamine-type fluorescent dye. The structure is based on a xanthene ring, with the molecular formula C28H31N2O3. This compound exhibits a planar conjugated system and is known for its exceptionally high fluorescence intensity and photostability. In biological function, this compound and its analogs (like Rhodamine B) are widely used as fluorescent tracers in biological and medical research, employed for labeling proteins, antibodies, or for cell counting and sorting in flow cytometry, and it is also a fluorescent dye used for staining and imaging in biological research that labels cellular structures while its interaction with nucleic acids (DNA/RNA) is nonspecific and primarily for visualization purposes.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-benzoyl-9-bromo-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 14, is a four-ring heterocycle based on the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one scaffold, the molecule is substituted with a phenyl group at 4, a bromo atom at 9, and a benzoyl group at 2, compared to some of its analogues, this compound lacks the para-fluoro atom on the benzoyl group at position 2, this defluorination is a common optimization step in drug discovery used to finely tune the molecules electronic distribution and lipophilicity, due to its inherent planarity and multiple aromatic rings, the structure of this compound suggests it is a potential DNA intercalator or small molecule inhibitor, in research, similar large planar heterocyclic ketones are often explored for their therapeutic potential against proliferative diseases, achieved through high-affinity binding to key intracellular targets, and this compound which contains a bromine atom showed high binding affinity to r(CAG)exp RNA, was effective in biophysical assays and reduced polyQ aggregation in cells.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]-N-[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]ethylamino]-2-oxoethyl]amino]-2-oxoethyl]amino]-2-oxoethyl]amino]-2-oxoethyl]amino]-2-oxoethyl]amino]-2-oxoethyl]amino]-2-oxoethyl]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-6G, is an exceptionally large and structurally complex bicephalic molecule, featuring two benzimidazole-piperazine active cores connected by an ultra-long polypeptide linker consisting of seven glycinamide units, while 2H-6G itself is an -peptide derivative with a six-glycine spacer; this extreme linker length is the defining feature of the compound, dramatically increasing its molecular flexibility, and similar to the performance of 2H-6G which showed reduced potency compared to shorter oligoglycine analogues as overly extended linkers diminish effective binding by reducing optimal positioning, this also provides evidence for the limitations of -peptide spacing beyond four glycines and led to 2H-6G not being developed further, with no additional features emphasized for it; the benzimidazole units of the bicephalic molecule are known for their fluorescent and nucleic acid binding potential, while the piperazine groups enhance their basicity and hydrophilicity, and in medicinal chemistry or biomaterials, such ultra-long bicephalic molecules are often designed as macroscopic biological probes or self-assembling molecules to study complex molecular recognition events, for instance, in biosensors or drug delivery systems.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: Trisodium;5-oxo-1-(4-sulfonatophenyl)-4-[(4-sulfonatophenyl)diazenyl]-4H-pyrazole-3-carboxylate, also referred to as Tartrazine (and known as the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110)), is the chemical name for the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110), a synthetic azo dye with the formula C16H10N2Na3O7[(2R,3S,4S,5R,6R)-2-[(2R,3S,4S,5S,6S)-2-[(1R,2S)-3-[[(2R,3S,4S)-5-[[(2S,3R)-1-[2-[4-[4-[3-[4-[[2-[[2-[[2-[acetyl(propyl)amino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-prop-2-ynylamino]acetyl]amino]butylamino]propylcarbamoyl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]ethylamino]-3-hydroxy-1-oxobutan-2-yl]amino]-3-hydroxy-4-methyl-5-oxopentan-2-yl]amino]-2-[[6-amino-2-[(1S)-3-amino-1-[[(2S)-2,3-diamino-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]-5-methylpyrimidine-4-carbonyl]amino]-1-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)-3-oxopropoxy]-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-3-yl]oxy-3,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-4-yl]carbamate, and Tartrazine is a synthetic yellow azo dye used as a food coloring agent while primarily an industrial colorant, its structure features a pyrazolone core linked by an azo bond (NN) to two benzene rings, both substituted with sodium sulfonate groups, and exists as a trisodium salt, the azo structure imparts a vibrant color, in real-world use, Sunset Yellow is widely employed as an orange-yellow coloring agent in foods, beverages, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics, such as orange juice, sodas, candies, and ice cream, to enhance visual appeal, as a common food colorant, its use is strictly regulated globally to ensure safety within prescribed limits, despite some controversy regarding its potential to affect behavior in sensitive individuals, particularly children.

RNA information: (5'CNG/3'CNG) denotes repetitive RNA sequences (N = any nucleotide) commonly in 5'/3' UTRs. Structurally, it forms stable hairpin or G-quadruplex structures. Functionally, it regulates post-transcriptional gene expression. Pathogenically, expanded subtypes (e.g., CUG/CAG repeats) cause microsatellite expansion disorders like myotonic dystrophy type 1, sequestering RNA-binding proteins (e.g., MBNL1) to disrupt alternative splicing, underpinning disease pathogenesis.
Small_molecule_information: methyl 7-hydroxy-6-(hydroxymethylidene)-4,9-dioxo-10H-phenazine-1-carboxylate, also referred to as lomofungin, is a derivative of phenazine, a core structure found in natural pigments with anthraquinone-like features, substituted with hydroxyl, keto, and methyl carboxylate groups; it is a natural antimicrobial agent produced by Streptomyces lomondensis that undergoes spontaneous dimerization in DMSO to form dilomofungin, and in real activity, phenazine compounds are well-known for their redox activity, enabling them to generate reactive oxygen species, this compound has potential antimicrobial activity as certain phenazine natural products are potent antibiotics, while it has also been identified as a potent inhibitor of MBNL1-CUG RNA binding, binds selectively to RNA hairpins containing CUG repeats particularly those with UU mismatches and disrupts the interaction between MBNL1 protein and pathogenic CUGexp RNA, in cellular models of myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) it partially restores MBNL1-dependent alternative splicing and increases the diffuse nucleoplasmic localization of MBNL1 indicating functional reversal of RNA toxicity, though its dimeric form dilomofungin, while more potent in vitro, unexpectedly stabilizes CUGexp RNA and leads to its accumulation in nuclear foci highlighting a potential limitation in the therapeutic use of high-affinity RNA ligands, and these findings suggest that it represents a promising scaffold for developing small-molecule therapies against DM1 provided that off-target effects on RNA turnover are carefully managed.

RNA information: The HIV-1 frameshift-stimulating stem-loop (FSS) RNA is a highly conserved structured element located within the gag-pol transcript that regulates production of the essential Gag-Pol polyprotein through a −1 ribosomal frameshift. It consists of a stable stem-loop positioned downstream of a slippery sequence that induces ribosomal pausing and promotes frameshifting in about 5–10% of translation events. This process allows translation to continue beyond the gag stop codon, producing Gag-Pol proteins such as protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase that are required for infectious virion assembly. Altering FSS activity disrupts the Gag to Gag-Pol ratio, substantially reducing viral replication and infectivity.
Small_molecule_information: 3-[[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-[(E,2S,7S)-8-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-azaniumylpropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]carbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-2,7-bis[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)amino]-8-oxooct-4-enoyl]pyrrolidine-2-carbonyl]amino]-3-phenylpropanoyl]amino]propylazanium, also referred to as Compound 5E, is a highly complex, poly-cationic peptidomimetic that shares the benzo[g]quinoline-expanded scaffold of 4Z and yields similarly high affinity for HIV-1 FSS RNA, while its selectivity profile is slightly weaker than 4Zshowing less discrimination between RNA and DNAit retains good specificity over unrelated viral FSS elements, can penetrate cells efficiently, shows no cytotoxicity at relevant concentrations, stimulates 1 frameshifting to a notable extent in reporter assays, reduces HIV infectivity significantly in viral assays and has corresponding effective concentration performance, confirming it as an effective FSS-targeted antiviral lead, and it is based on the (E)-oct-4-enediamide scaffold, featuring phenylalanine residues and the bulky 2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl group, with its termini linked to the 3-azaniumylpropyl groups (quaternized/protonated) that impart strong poly-cationic properties, and this structure which combines poly-cationic centers, high aromaticity, and the hydrophobicity of phenylalanine is ideally suited as a high-selectivity DNA minor groove binder or as an analog of cell-penetrating peptides, holding potential for development as a potent anti-cancer or antiviral agent.

RNA information: The NRQ is a 18-nucleotide RNA oligomer corresponding to the wild-type, guanine-rich sequence (5′-GGGAGGGGCGGGUCUGGG-3′) located in the 5′-UTR of the human NRAS proto-oncogene mRNA. This sequence possesses the intrinsic ability to fold into a stable, parallel RNA G-quadruplex structure, which has been previously established to act as a translational repressor by inhibiting the initiation of NRAS protein synthesis. In this study, it serves as the primary target structure for the novel MSQ derivatives. Its confirmed G-quadruplex formation, characterized by a specific CD spectrum with a positive peak at 262 nm that is enhanced in the presence of potassium ions, and its functional role make it essential for evaluating the binding affinity and specificity of potential ligands designed to stabilize this structure and further repress translation.
Small_molecule_information: N,N-diethyl-N-[2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-10H-indolo[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]ethane-1,2-diamine, also referred to as the derivative with an N1,N1-diethylethane-1,2-diamine side chain that binds to NRQ, is an indoloquinoline derivative similar to N,N-diethyl-N-[2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-10H-indolo[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]propane-1,3-diamine, its structure is based on the indolo[3,2-b]quinoline scaffold, with an ethyldiamine side chain at the 11-position, and the (4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl group at the 2-position (molecular formula C30H32N4S), this compound exhibits a planar conjugated system, making it an effective DNA intercalator and Topoisomerase inhibitor, and it can bind to NRQ while maintaining strong anti-proliferative effects in A375 cells, positioning it as a potential anti-cancer or antiparasitic drug.

RNA information: 5'CUG/3'CUG is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it folds into an A-form hairpin with characteristic UU mismatches, stabilized by base-pairing. Functionally, it sequesters Muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein, disrupting pre-mRNA alternative splicing, which is the core gain-of-function mechanism underlying DM1 pathogenesis.
Small_molecule_information: 7-hydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-8-[(2S,3R,4R,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-2-yl]chromen-4-one, also referred to as Genistin (another isoflavone glycoside in this category is Puerarin, which is found in kudzu root), is a glycoside derivative of Genistein (7-hydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)chromen-4-one), known as Genistin, which is an isoflavone glycoside with the formula C21H20O9; Puerarin exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective effects, improves blood circulation and is studied for cardiovascular and metabolic diseases, while Genistins structure features the Genistein backbone with a glucose molecule (or glucoside moiety, the oxan-2-yl part) attached via a C-C bond at the C8 position, with the specific stereochemistry of the sugar being characteristic of its natural form, and it is the most abundant isoflavone compound found in soybeans, where it predominantly exists in this glycosylated form; in biology, most natural flavonoids exist as glycosides, which typically means they have higher water solubility but lower inherent bioactivity, requiring hydrolysis by gut microbes or intracellular -glucosidases to release the more active aglycone form, Genistein (7-hydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)chromen-4-one), thus, Genistin is often considered a prodrug form of Genistein, sharing similar health benefits such as phytoestrogenic, antioxidant, and potential anticancer activities, but with a different pharmacokinetic profile.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: N-[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]-2-[[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]carbamoylamino]acetamide, also referred to as Compound H9 (a bis-benzimidazole derivative of Hoechst 33258), is a molecule with a linear acetamide backbone and a central urea group, with both ends substituted by a phenyl ring bearing a 4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl (imidazolinyl) group, the imidazolinyl group is strongly basic and is almost fully protonated at physiological pH, making this compound a classic example of a polycationic molecule, the central urea (N(H)CON(H)) and amide (CON(H)) linkages provide multiple hydrogen bond donors and acceptors, due to its length, rigidity, and strong cationic nature, this molecule is designed as a nucleic acid binding agent, intended to interact tightly with the phosphate backbone of DNA or RNA via electrostatic forces, and possibly through intercalation between base pairs, such compounds are frequently investigated as broad-spectrum antimicrobial agents (especially antifungal and antiprotozoal) or as gene expression modulators, while Compound H9 retains the DNA-binding benzimidazole core of Hoechst 33258 but is elongated with a hydrophobic p-chlorophenyl tail that improves its shape complementarity to the major groove of the 5CUG/3GUC hairpin repeat, can dissociate MBNL1 from r(CUG)exp RNA (the pathogenic RNA causing myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)) with greater potency than Hoechst 33258 though it is less selective than H1 as it binds with similar affinity to RNAs containing 5CUG/3GCC or 5CGG/3GGC motifs and to the human A-site, and in DM1 cell models it can partially shift a bichromatic MBNL1 exon 5 reporter toward the normal exon-skipped DsRed signal while showing cytotoxicity at higher doses, exemplifying how computational shape similarity can evolve a DNA-binding dye into a chemical probe for DM1 RNA that may serve as a stepping-stone toward in-vivo-compatible DM1 therapeutics.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]-N-[3-[[3-[[3-[2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]ethylamino]-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-3A, is the dimeric molecule with the longest spacer in this series, featuring a tetra-amide chain which provides the greatest spatial flexibility and molecular reach, while 2H-3A employs three -alanine units between recognition elements, showed similar potency to other -peptides and did not surpass the best -peptide constructs, with its related potency values remaining in a range indicating limited advantage of this design, having no detailed description of cell-based effects and only being part of comparative analysis rather than a development focus; this extremely long linker of the dimeric molecule allows the two bis-benzimidazole binding units to interact with DNA over a greater distance, potentially binding to different regions or exhibiting a more flexible binding mode, and in practice, such a large molecule is valuable as a molecular tool for investigating long-range DNA-drug interactions or could be designed as a multivalent ligand to enhance binding efficiency to targets, with its primary application remaining in the development of potent anticancer agents with novel mechanisms of action.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 4,5,6,7-tetrachloro-3,6-dihydroxyspiro[2-benzofuran-3,9-xanthene]-1-one, also referred to as Tetrachlorofluorescein, is a spiro compound, joining a benzofuranone ring (or phthalic anhydride moiety) to a xanthene scaffold via a common carbon atom (the spiro carbon), forming a spiro[2-benzofuran-3,9-xanthene]-1-one structure, essentially a derivative of Rhodamine or Fluorescein, and this fluorescent dye derived from fluorescein is also oFTAn used as a tracer in biochemical assays. This molecule belongs to the family of well-known organic fluorescent dyes, characterized by a large conjugated system that allows the molecule to absorb light at a specific wavelength and emit characteristic fluorescence, and it lacks specific RNA/DNA binding properties but serves as a marker in staining applications. In 4,5,6,7-tetrachloro-3,6-dihydroxyspiro[2-benzofuran-3,9-xanthene]-1-one, the xanthene moiety bears 3,6-dihydroxy groups, which facilitate the formation of the emissive quinoid structure, while the 4,5,6,7-tetrachloro substituents on the benzofuranone ring can significantly modify its fluorescence properties, such as increasing quantum yield or causing a red-shift in the emission wavelength. In real-world applications, these compounds are indispensable tools in biomedical research and diagnostics, widely used as fluorescent probes for cell labeling, protein tracking, immunofluorescence staining, and DNA sequencing, and also act as a tracer in biochemical assays and marker in staining applications.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: Trisodium;5-oxo-1-(4-sulfonatophenyl)-4-[(4-sulfonatophenyl)diazenyl]-4H-pyrazole-3-carboxylate, also referred to as Tartrazine (and known as the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110)), is the chemical name for the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110), a synthetic azo dye with the formula C16H10N2Na3O7[(2R,3S,4S,5R,6R)-2-[(2R,3S,4S,5S,6S)-2-[(1R,2S)-3-[[(2R,3S,4S)-5-[[(2S,3R)-1-[2-[4-[4-[3-[4-[[2-[[2-[[2-[acetyl(propyl)amino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-prop-2-ynylamino]acetyl]amino]butylamino]propylcarbamoyl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]ethylamino]-3-hydroxy-1-oxobutan-2-yl]amino]-3-hydroxy-4-methyl-5-oxopentan-2-yl]amino]-2-[[6-amino-2-[(1S)-3-amino-1-[[(2S)-2,3-diamino-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]-5-methylpyrimidine-4-carbonyl]amino]-1-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)-3-oxopropoxy]-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-3-yl]oxy-3,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-4-yl]carbamate, and Tartrazine is a synthetic yellow azo dye used as a food coloring agent while primarily an industrial colorant, its structure features a pyrazolone core linked by an azo bond (NN) to two benzene rings, both substituted with sodium sulfonate groups, and exists as a trisodium salt, the azo structure imparts a vibrant color, in real-world use, Sunset Yellow is widely employed as an orange-yellow coloring agent in foods, beverages, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics, such as orange juice, sodas, candies, and ice cream, to enhance visual appeal, as a common food colorant, its use is strictly regulated globally to ensure safety within prescribed limits, despite some controversy regarding its potential to affect behavior in sensitive individuals, particularly children.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: Benzoic acid is the simplest aromatic carboxylic acid with the formula C7H6O2. Its structure consists of a benzene ring directly attached to a single carboxyl group (COOH), which is the key structural feature of this simple aromatic carboxylic acid. Benzoic acid occurs naturally in many plants, such as gum benzoin and berries. The most significant real-world application of Benzoic acid is its use as a food preservative, commonly added to food, beverages, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. It primarily functions by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms, especially yeasts and molds, through the reduction of intracellular pH and interference with cellular enzyme systems. Its sodium salt, Sodium benzoate, is often preferred for its better water solubility. Additionally, Benzoic acid is an important precursor for the synthesis of other organic chemicals, including benzoyl chloride and benzoate esters. Relevant data shows that benzoic acid does not have a low Kd value and does not induce gene expression, indicating that it may not be an active ligand for 108yjdF RNA.

RNA information: (CUG)12 is an RNA model of expanded pathogenic CUG repeats in myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1). Structurally, it forms a stable A-form hairpin with UU mismatches in its double-stranded region, stabilized by base-pairing and phosphate backbone interactions. Functionally, it high-affinity sequesters MBNL1 protein, disrupting alternative splicing, causing ribonuclear foci accumulation and pre-mRNA mis-splicing (e.g., insulin receptor exon 11 exclusion), via a toxic gain-of-function mechanism, making it a DM1 therapeutic target.
Small_molecule_information: [[4-[azaniumylidene-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-1-ium-2-yl)amino]butylamino]methyl]phenyl]-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-1-ium-2-yl)amino]butylamino]methylidene]azanium, also referred to as Bisamidinium 3, is a structurally complex molecule characterized by a high positive charge, indicated by the presence of multiple imidamide and triazine groups, specifically the terms triazin-1-ium and azanium, suggesting the molecule is highly protonated under physiological pH, carrying multiple positive charges, and it possesses a bisamidinium moiety and triaminotriazine core, engineered to enter cells via the polyamine transport system; this structural patternmultiple strongly basic, planar heterocycles connected by flexible chainsis typical of fluorescent dyes and DNA/RNA binders, and the compound is a CUG-targeting ligand that selectively binds toxic CUGexp RNA with no detectable affinity for dCTG, can disperse MBNL1-CUGexp nuclear foci in DM1 model cells and partially rescue splicing defects, while unlike analog 9, it lacks RNase-like cleavage activity and CTGexp transcription inhibition, failing to reduce CUGexp levels or reverse DM1 phenotypes in Drosophila models; the numerous amino and imino/imidamide structures allow for strong electrostatic interactions with the negatively charged nucleic acid backbone and extensive hydrogen bonding, and in research, this compound is highly likely to be used as a biological probe, such as a nuclear stain or nucleic acid detection agent, with its biological activity centered on antimicrobial or anti-tumor applications through interference with nucleic acid function.

RNA information: NRQ mutant is a G-base mutant of NRQ that prevents the formation of the G4 structure.
Small_molecule_information: N,N-dimethyl-N-[2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-10H-indolo[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]propane-1,3-diamine, also referred to as the compound featuring an indolo[3,2-b]quinoline core and an N1,N1-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine side chain, is a neutral indolo[3,2-b]quinoline derivative with a propane-1,3-diamine side chain; this compound exhibited specific binding affinity for the NRQ and displayed potent anti-proliferative activity across various cancer cell lines, and the chain length and basicity of its propane-1,3-diamine side chain are critical parameters studied in drug design to optimize binding to specific targets, potentially including the DNA minor groove or particular protein kinase pockets for anticancer activity.

Small_molecule_information: N-[(E,2S)-1,8-bis[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]-methylcarbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-7-[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)-methylamino]-1,8-dioxooct-4-en-2-yl]-2-ethyl-N-methylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carboxamide, also referred to as Compound 4E (the tetramethylated analog), is a complex N-methylated poly-cationic peptidomimetic based on the (E)-oct-4-en-2-yl scaffold, which stands out for its combination of high affinity, strong selectivity, and superior antiviral potency, binds HIV-1 FSS RNA with extremely strong affinity and demonstrates notable selectivity over off-target nucleic acids, and crucially shows the greatest membrane permeability of the series due to increased hydrophobicity and exhibits the strongest inhibition of HIV infectivity in pseudotyped assays, not only reducing the production of infectious virions but also impairing intracellular viral replication to become the most biologically effective compound reported, while the molecule has undergone N-methylation at multiple amide nitrogens within the peptide backbone and the terminal carbamoyl groups, and N-methylation is a critical medicinal chemistry strategy used to increase metabolic stability (resistance to peptidase degradation), reduce hydrogen bonding capacity, and enhance membrane permeability, aiming to improve oral bioavailability, and the molecule retains its poly-cationic and highly hydrophobic nature, serving as a candidate for development as a more orally bioavailable kinase inhibitor or anti-cancer agent.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₀ is an RNA fragment with 10 consecutive CUG repeats, structurally forming a stable hairpin with 10 5′CUG/3′GUC internal loops, U·U mismatches, and optional GC clamps (5′/3′ ends) for enhanced stability. Functionally, it lacks toxicity of pathogenic long (CUG)ₙ  but binds MBNL1 with high affinity, interacts specifically with lomofungin  and dilomofungin . It serves as an in vitro tool for studying MBNL1-(CUG) interaction mechanisms and screening DM1 therapeutic small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: 8-(9-carboxy-4-formyl-1,3,6-trihydroxyphenazin-2-yl)-6-formyl-4,7,9-trihydroxyphenazine-1-carboxylic acid, also referred to as Dilomofungin, is an extremely complex dimeric molecule, consisting of two highly substituted phenazine units linked by a carbon-carbon bond, which is a spontaneous dimer of lomofungin formed in DMSO and structurally features a carboncarbon bond between two lomofungin monomers. The phenazine scaffold is a heterocyclic system made of two benzene rings fused with two nitrogen atoms, and many natural phenazine derivatives (like pyocyanin) are pigments produced by microorganisms with potent antibiotic and biological redox activities. This compound possesses multiple functional groups, including hydroxyl, carboxyl, and formyl groups, which lend the molecule multiple hydrogen bond donor/acceptor capabilities and high water solubility, and it exhibits higher potency than lomofungin in inhibiting MBNL1-CUG RNA binding in vitro, while also having strong binding affinity and the ability to partially rescue mis-splicing in DM1 cell models, though it has the unintended effect of stabilizing toxic CUG repeat RNA, leading to its accumulation in nuclear foci and reduced transcript turnover, which limits its therapeutic potential for myotonic dystrophy type 1. The phenazine rings establish it as a potent redox-active molecule capable of mediating electron transfer in biological systems. In reality, such structurally complex phenazine derivatives are often explored as novel antibiotics or anticancer agents, where the mechanism of action might involve DNA intercalation or the generation of reactive oxygen species to induce apoptosis. This molecule could also serve as a chelating agent or redox indicator in biochemical analysis.

Its confirmed G-quadruplex formation, characterized by a specific CD spectrum with a positive peak at 262 nm that is enhanced in the presence of potassium ions, and its functional role make it essential for evaluating the binding affinity and specificity of potential ligands designed to stabilize this structure and further repress translation.
Small_molecule_information: N-[2-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)ethyl]-2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-amine, also referred to as the key hit compound with a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core, the p-(methylthio)styryl group at the 2-position, and a 2-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)ethyl)amino side chain at the 11-position, features the short ethylamine-4-methylpiperazine side chain; this compound displayed the strongest binding affinity among all relevant compounds and potent anti-proliferative activity across all cancer cell lines, though it did not repress NRAS translation like 4a-10, suggesting a different mechanism or binding mode, and its short-chain piperazine is a classic motif in highly potent DNA intercalators and topoisomerase inhibitors, with the short ethylamine spacer being crucial for establishing the optimal electrostatic interaction distance between the positively charged terminus and the DNA phosphate backbone, making it an important structure for highly affine nucleic acid-targeting anticancer drug development.

RNA information: 5′GAU/3′C_A: This GAU/C_A motif forms a bulged loop detected in the RNA motif screen. While identified as a potential ligand-binding site, the study did not provide extensive validation of its direct role in HCV RNA or associated antiviral outcomes.
Small_molecule_information: 1-N,3-N,5-N-tris[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxamide, also referred to as Compound 8, is a molecule with a trimeric structure, built upon a benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxamide core, which is a rigid, three-armed scaffold with C3h symmetry. The end of each arm is substituted with a phenyl group bearing a 4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl (imidazolinyl) group. Consequently, the entire molecule contains a total of three imidazolinyl groups, which are strong bases that can be protonated at physiological pH, making it a potent tricationic system. This rigid, symmetrical, polycationic architecture is highly suitable for acting as a high-affinity DNA binding agent and also displays strong selectivity for RNA internal loops, especially motifs containing 5UUA/3A_U, 5GCU/3C_A, and 5UCUAU/3ACCA, with its recognition pattern highlighting tolerance for mismatched and bulged bases within short hairpins; the molecule effectively binds HCV 3 UTR stemloops I and II, consistent with motif enrichment analyses, and cellular studies revealed potent inhibition of HCV replication, with ASO competition confirming direct binding to viral RNA motifs. Its three-armed structure may allow it to interact simultaneously with multiple sites on the DNA helix, such as two minor groove sites and a phosphate backbone site. Compounds of this type are frequently designed in medicinal chemistry as anticancer, antitrypanosomal agents, or as gene transfection aids, and these results position this molecule as a promising lead for selective RNA-targeted antiviral development.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 1-[(2R,3R,4S,5R)-3,4-dihydroxy-5-(phosphonooxymethyl)oxolan-2-yl]pyridin-1-ium-3-carboxylate is a zwitterionic form of Nicotinic Acid Mononucleotide (NaMN), also referred to as Nicotinic acid mononucleotide (NAMN), which is a precursor to Nicotinamide Mononucleotide (NMN), with the formula C11H14NO10P, it is a non-azaaromatic nucleotide that lacks planar azaaromatic features and binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, its core structure is composed of a nicotinic acid moiety (pyridine ring attached to the sugar at N1 and a carboxylate at 3) linked to a ribose phosphate group (a D-ribose ring with a phosphate group at the 5 position, which is consistent with the structure of a nicotinic acid base, a ribose sugar, and one phosphate group linked to the riboses 5-carbon), this molecule is a vital metabolic intermediate found in all living cells, playing a critical role in cellular energy metabolism, functionally, NaMN (also NAMN) is a key intermediate in NAD biosynthesis as well as a step in the synthesis of Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide (NAD), cells convert it to nicotinic acid adenine dinucleotide (NAAD) first, then to NAD, supporting essential redox reactions, and NAD is a necessary coenzyme for hundreds of enzymatic reactions crucial for cell health, energy production, and DNA repair, in recent years, molecules of this class have gained significant attention for their potential in anti-aging research, as they are considered potential agents for boosting cellular NAD levels, improving metabolic function, and potentially slowing down the aging process.

RNA information: The r(CCUG)8 RNA forms an A-form structure with consecutive CU mismatches, sequestering MBNL1 protein, leading to splicing defects and DM2 disease symptoms.
Small_molecule_information: 1-N,4-N-bis[4-(2,4,6-triaminopyrimidin-5-yl)butyl]benzene-1,4-dicarboximidamide, also referred to as Bisamidinium 8, is built on a highly symmetrical benzene-1,4-dicarboximidamide core, where the imidamide groups are strongly basic and capable of bearing a positive charge; the para-substituted core is linked via two four-carbon butane flexible chains to the terminal 2,4,6-triaminopyrimidine rings, and the use of a longer butane chain compared to a propylene chain increases the overall flexibility of the molecule and modifies the effective distance between the two terminal triaminopyrimidine rings, which is critical for achieving optimal spatial alignment for binding to biological targets like nucleic acids; with multiple strongly basic, planar aromatic heterocycles, this compound is classified as a potential DNA/RNA binder, capable of disrupting nucleic acid function through multiple interactions (electrostatic, hydrogen bonding, stacking), and it also boasts the highest potency in its series, can efficiently disrupt the MBNL1-r(CCUG)8 interaction, dissolve disease foci in DM2 model cells while maintaining very low toxicity, highlighting its potential as a therapeutic agent, while such compounds are commonly screened and developed as antiparasitic drugs, particularly against parasitic organisms with distinct nucleic acid metabolism.

RNA information: RNA 2 (Drosha 515) is an RNA fragment that mimics the Drosha processing site in pri-miR-515, with its core structural feature being the presence of the Drosha processing site motif unique to pri-miR-515. Functionally, it serves as an in vitro research tool for detecting the binding properties between small molecules and RNA: the monomeric Compound 1 can bind to it, while the dimeric Compound 2 has no binding ability to it. Through such interaction analyses, it provides key data support for screening selective small molecules that target pri-miR-515.

RNA information: The duplex sequence is a designed RNA composed of complementary CUG and CAG repeats that form a stable double helix. Structurally, it lacks the mismatches present in expanded repeats, instead adopting a continuous A-form conformation that eliminates the irregular binding pockets exploited by proteins or ligands in single-stranded hairpins. Functionally, the duplex acts as a control to demonstrate whether ligands bind specifically to mismatched hairpin structures or indiscriminately to generic RNA helices. Its inclusion highlights the importance of structural context in ligand recognition and helps identify molecules with true selectivity for pathogenic RNA conformations.
Small_molecule_information: (E,2S,7S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,7-bis[[(2S)-1-(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)pyrrolidine-2-carbonyl]amino]oct-4-enediamide, also referred to as DCC 5 (2012), is the E-isomer counterpart of compound 4, differing only in the configuration of the olefin bridge, and this geometric change alters the positioning of the benzo[g]quinoline group relative to the peptide scaffold, leading to slightly different recognition properties, while it retains overall high binding capability, though its cooperativity and selectivity are reduced compared to the Z-isomer, and structurally, it highlights how subtle stereochemical differences strongly affect RNA recognition, and biologically, it is still active in cellular models and displaces pathogenic complexes, demonstrating that both isomers contribute valuable insights into structureactivity relationships for RNA-binding molecules; it is a highly complex, multi-cationic peptidomimetic structure, its core is the (E)-oct-4-enediamide scaffold, with side chains derived from proline, 2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl, and lysine, the molecule contains multiple primary amino groups, rendering it poly-cationic at physiological pH, the 2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline is a bulky, highly hydrophobic aromatic group, this combination of poly-cationic character, multiple chiral centers, and large hydrophobic groups makes it suitable as an anti-tumor agent, a DNA/RNA binding agent, or a novel antibiotic, and the (E)-alkene provides a specific, rigid conformation.

RNA information: (5'CNG/3'CNG) denotes repetitive RNA sequences (N = any nucleotide) predominantly located in 5'/3' UTRs. Structurally, it forms stable hairpin or G-quadruplex structures via specific base pairing. Functionally, it regulates post-transcriptional gene expression. Pathogenically, expanded subtypes (e.g., CUG/CAG repeats) cause microsatellite expansion disorders like myotonic dystrophy type 1, sequestering RNA-binding proteins (e.g., MBNL1) to disrupt alternative splicing and drive disease.
Small_molecule_information: (2E,4E)-5-(1,3-benzodioxol-5-yl)-1-piperidin-1-ylpenta-2,4-dien-1-one, also referred to as Piperine (a major bioactive alkaloid and phytochemical compound derived from black pepper (Piper nigrum) that is responsible for the pungent taste of pepper and has key characteristics including low water solubility and poor bioavailability), is a compound that is either a natural product derivative or a structural analogue, typically categorized with compounds related to Piperine. Its structure is characterized by a 1,3-benzodioxol-5-yl group (a signature motif derived from Piperine and common in natural products), linked via a conjugated diene bridge (composed of two double bonds) to a piperidin-1-yl-ketone (amide) moiety. This structure is highly planar and possesses an extended conjugated system, often resulting in a colored solid with potential unique fluorescence properties. Piperine and its derivatives exhibit various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, analgesic effects, and are most renowned for their bioavailability enhancing properties by inhibiting certain metabolizing enzymes like CYP3A4, with Piperine itself pharmacologically renowned for its enhancer effect, significantly increasing the absorption and efficacy of various drugs and nutrients by inhibiting digestive enzymes and drug-metabolizing enzymes like cytochrome P450, and also possessing intrinsic anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and potential neuroprotective properties; the specific piperidine substitution in (2E,4E)-5-(1,3-benzodioxol-5-yl)-1-piperidin-1-ylpenta-2,4-dien-1-one increases its lipophilicity, facilitating its passage across cell membranes, and it is often studied as a potential active component of natural product-based drugs or as an adjuvant to enhance the absorption of other pharmaceuticals.

RNA information: RNA1 contains the 1x1 nucleotide UU internal loop motif that is present within the Drosha processing site of pri-miR-96. It serves as the specific binding target for compound 1, with a Kd of 1,300 nM, but does not bind compound 2.
Small_molecule_information: N-(3-azidopropyl)-4-[2,6-ditert-butyl-4-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as Compound 1, is a mono-benzimidazole Click Chemistry probe with tert-butyl and azide groups, which is a benzimidazole-based RNA-binding module previously identified by the Informa platform to avidly bind a 1x1 nucleotide UU internal loop within the Drosha processing site of the pri-miR-96 hairpin precursor, inhibits miR-96 biogenesis and induces apoptosis in breast cancer cells with activity that limits its in vivo utility, shows no detectable binding to DNA, and its real-world activity is dedicated to biological orthogonal labeling experiments for target validation and mechanism elucidation.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-5,7-dihydroxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Luteolin (a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects that exhibits antioxidant activity and may influence gene expression), is a naturally occurring flavone compound (distinguished from flavonols like 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-3,5,7-trihydroxychromen-4-one by the lack of a hydroxyl group at C3), with the formula C15H10O6. Its structure features hydroxyl groups on the A ring (5 and 7) and a catechol moiety (3,4-dihydroxyphenyl) on the B ring. Luteolin is widely found in celery, parsley, broccoli, cabbage, and various medicinal herbs. In real-world biological activity, Luteolin demonstrates powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an important active component in traditional herbal medicine and modern supplements. It operates through multiple mechanisms, including free radical scavenging, inhibition of various inflammatory mediators, and modulation of immune cell function. Furthermore, it is extensively studied for its neuroprotective, anti-allergic, and potential anticancer properties, such as by inhibiting tumor cell proliferation and inducing apoptosis.

RNA information: (5'CCG/3'GCC)x6: This RNA contains CCG repeats, which are linked to fragile X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome (FXTAS). It is used as another control to demonstrate that the small molecules do not bind significantly to other trinucleotide repeat RNAs, further supporting their specificity for CAG repeats.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-(4-methoxybenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 3, shares the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one core with 3-amino-9-bromo-2-(4-methoxybenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one but lacks the bromine atom, and Compound 3 emerged as a lead compound with high affinity and selectivity for r(CAG)exp RNA, its large aromatic and amino-substituted structure suggests high lipophilicity and the potential to interact strongly with biological targets, it is likely investigated as an antiproliferative agent targeting intracellular enzymes or signaling pathways, Compound 3 significantly reduced polyQ aggregation in cellular models, showed low cytotoxicity, and was cell-permeable, localizing primarily in the cytoplasm. RNA information: r(CUG)2 is an RNA motif found in the expanded CUG repeat RNA (r(CUG)exp) that causes myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1). It forms a stable stem-loop structure with two CUG repeats and can specifically bind to muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein, leading to its sequestration and the resulting misregulation of alternative splicing. This RNA-protein interaction contributes to the molecular pathology of DM1, and small molecules targeting r(CUG)2 can disrupt the complex and restore splicing defects in disease models.

due to the cis conformation, making it a critical tool for studying the impact of molecular conformation on biological activity.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (3Z)-3-hydroxyimino-2-phenylchromen-4-one, also referred to as CP9 or an oximinoflavone derivative, has the formula C15H11NO3. Its structure is a derivative of flavone (2-phenylchromen-4-one) featuring a chromone (benzopyranone) core with an oxime group and a phenyl ring, where an oxime group (CNOH) is attached at the 3 position, and the oxime bond has a (Z) geometric isomer configuration. This class of compounds is often obtained through chemical synthesis or structural modification of natural flavonoids. In pharmaceutical chemistry research, oxime derivatives of flavonoids are often explored for metal ion chelation or as enzyme inhibitors. This specific molecule and its analogues are studied for their potential antioxidant and antitumor activities, for instance, by inhibiting certain tyrosine kinases or acting as neuroprotective agents. Such oxime-containing molecules are also sometimes used in drug chemistry to improve lipophilicity or alter metabolic stability, though the presence of the oxime in this compound likely contributed to a very high Kd2 value, indicating poor or non-specific binding and disqualifying it as a candidate.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: N-(17-methoxy-5,7-dioxa-13-azoniapentacyclo[11.8.0.0{2,10}.0{4,8}.0{15,20}]henicosa-1(13),2,4(8),9,14,16,18,20-octaen-16-yl)-N,N-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine chloride This molecule couples the complex cationic scaffold with an N,N-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine side chain. The propane-1,3-diamine backbone is a common structural motif found in natural polyamines (e.g., spermine, spermidine precursors), lending the molecule dicationic tendencies. Structurally similar compounds are often designed as ion channel blockers or GPCR ligands, particularly targeting histamine or serotonin receptors. The combination of the tertiary amine terminus and the quaternary ammonium core suggests potential for modulating intracellular pH or interfering with transmembrane signaling. Its activity might be focused on the treatment of CNS disorders or as an antipsychotic drug lead.

RNA information: The r(CAG)₁₀ sequence serves as a comparative motif to test ligand selectivity against repeat RNAs with A–A mismatches instead of U–U mismatches. Structurally, it forms A-form helical RNA with alternating positive and negative electrostatic stripes similar to CUG repeats, leading to comparable overall architectures. Functionally, r(CAG)₁₀ is not associated with myotonic dystrophy but provides a stringent control for assessing molecular recognition. Because many proteins and synthetic ligands show similar affinity toward both CUG and CAG repeats, this sequence demonstrates the challenges of engineering true sequence selectivity in RNA-targeted small-molecule design.

RNA information: RNAC is a control RNA hairpin that does not contain the specific internal loop targets for compounds 1 or 2. It features a single-nucleotide change compared to RNA1, which eliminates binding, confirming that the compounds' interactions with other RNAs are specific to the intended motifs.

(Z,2S,7S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,7-bis[[(2S)-1-(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)pyrrolidine-2-carbonyl]amino]oct-4-enediamide will be significantly altered due to the cis conformation, making it a critical tool for studying the impact of molecular conformation on biological activity.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[5-(4-carbamimidoylphenoxy)pentoxy]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Compound 24 (EBAB, Pentamidine), is an amidine linked by an alkoxy chain, where two amidine groups are connected by a relatively long pentyloxy ether chain, this molecule is an aromatic diamidine with benzamidine pharmacophore and features a 1,2-ethane/1,5-bis(4-amidinophenoxy)pentane scaffold and bisamidinium groups, its bis-amidine ether structure is highly significant in medicinal chemistry, exemplified by the well-known antiprotozoal drug Pentamidine which acts by binding to parasite DNA, it can also bind r(CUG)exps UU loops/5CUG/3GUC hairpins, displace MBNL1, correct DM1 splicing defects, act as an antibiotic adjuvant against multidrug-resistant bacteria, inhibit Class I intron self-splicing, and has low toxicity, the structural features of the molecule make it a candidate for a potential antiprotozoal or antifungal drug particularly against diseases like Pneumocystis pneumonia, and its activity is closely related to the length and flexibility of the linker chain, while also showing promise for DM1 and drug-resistant infections.

Small_molecule_information: 3,5-dichloro-4-phenylmethoxybenzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Compound P7, is a compound based on the benzamidine scaffold, substituted with two chlorine atoms and a benzyloxy group on the benzene ring, with the formula C14H12Cl2N2O; it is a dichloro-substituted mono-imidamide derivative, cationic due to the imidamide group, retains the twin benzamidine cations of pentamidine but is elongated with a nine-carbon methylene linker that maximizes its shape complementarity to the major groove of the 5CUG/3GUC hairpin repeat, the benzyloxy group enhances lipophilicity and steric bulk, is a pentamidine-derived small molecule optimized through 3-D shape-based similarity screening as a next-generation ligand for the toxic r(CUG)exp RNA that causes myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1), can dissociate MBNL1 from r(CUG)12 in a TR-FRET displacement assay and partially shifts a bichromatic MBNL1 exon 5 reporter toward the normal exon-skipped DsRed signal in DM1 cell models though cytotoxicity becomes evident at higher doses, is less selective than P1 as it binds with similar affinity to RNAs containing 5CCG/3GUC or 5CAG/3GCC motifs and to the human A-site, and such compounds are researched as novel antibacterial and antiparasitic agents, with activity often attributed to the specific binding and electrostatic interaction of the cationic structure with the negatively charged DNA of the microorganism, while Compound P7 itself exemplifies how computational shape similarity can rapidly evolve an antimicrobial template into a low-micromolar chemical probe for DM1 RNA that may serve as a stepping-stone toward in-vivo-compatible DM1 therapeutics.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: (3R,4S,5S,6R,7R,9R,11R,12R,13S,14R)-6-[(2S,3R,4S,6R)-4-(dimethylamino)-3-hydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxy-14-ethyl-7,12,13-trihydroxy-4-[(2R,4R,5S,6S)-5-hydroxy-4-methoxy-4,6-dimethyloxan-2-yl]oxy-3,5,7,9,11,13-hexamethyl-oxacyclotetradecane-2,10-dione, also referred to as Erythromycin, is an extremely complex macrolide structure, representing Erythromycin or its derivatives, and Erythromycin is a macrolide antibiotic with a 14-membered lactone ring core linked to two deoxy sugars (desosamine and cladinose), its structure features a large cyclic lactone scaffold with glycosidic attachments, enabling specific bacterial ribosome binding, macrolides are important clinical antibiotics that inhibit bacterial protein synthesis by binding to the 50S ribosomal subunit, functionally, it inhibits bacterial protein synthesis by targeting the 50S ribosomal subunit, blocking peptide bond formation, and exhibits broad activity against Gram-positive bacteria, being widely used to treat respiratory and skin infections while resisting certain drug-resistant strains.

RNA information: pre-miR-21 is a stem-loop non-coding RNA precursor with a distinct structure, containing G28-A29 (Dicer-TRBP cleavage site) in the stem and single-stranded bulges/apical loops (small-molecule binding pockets). It processes into oncogenic mature miR-21; ligands like 4a or bPGN  bind specifically, inhibiting Dicer processing, upregulating tumor suppressors (PDCD4, PTEN), and suppressing cancer cell proliferation. It is a druggable target for cancers such as colorectal cancer.
Small_molecule_information: N-(2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridin-6-ylmethyl)-N-methylaniline, also referred to as 4b, is a relatively simple amine heterocyclic compound, consisting of an N-methylaniline moiety (a key moiety for pre-miR-21 binding) linked to a 2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine group via a methylene bridge, and this 2-amino-dihydropyrrolopyridine scaffold-based small molecule is synthesized via a sustainable, modular ruthenium-catalyzed [222] cycloaddition route, which shows good pre-miR-21 selectivity vs. duplex DNA/tRNA but lacks Dicer cleavage inhibition activity likely due to the absence of crucial H-bond interactions from its aniline group; as a tertiary amine, it is easily protonated at physiological pH and exhibits moderate lipophilicity, and this combination of properties suggests good membrane permeability and potential for traversing the blood-brain barrier, while in real-world applications, it is often explored as a candidate for Monoamine Oxidase (MAO) inhibition or as a ligand for specific neurotransmitter receptors, making it relevant for the treatment of neurodegenerative diseases like Parkinsons or conditions like depression.

RNA information: The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).; The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).·
Small_molecule_information: 5-amino-2-[[5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoyl]amino]-5-oxopentanoic acidThis conjugate links the antineoplastic benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core with a peptide chain comprising Arginine and Glutamine. Glutamine is a neutral amino acid crucial for rapidly proliferating cells, including many cancer cells. This compound is potentially engineered to exploit the high metabolic demand for Glutamine in tumors, functioning as a targeted cytotoxic agent or biomarker. The Glutamine residue influences the molecules hydrogen bonding capacity and cellular uptake characteristics.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-3,5,7-trihydroxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Quercetin, is a natural flavonol compound and one of the most common and widely studied flavonoids in nature, with the formula C15H10O7. Its structure features hydroxyl groups on the A ring (5 and 7) and C ring (3), and a catechol moiety (two adjacent hydroxyl groups) on the B ring (3 and 4 positions). Quercetin is abundant in various plant-based foods, including onions, apples, tea, berries, and red wine. In real-world biological activity, Quercetin exhibits outstanding pharmacological properties: it is a powerful antioxidant and free-radical scavenger, effective at inhibiting lipid peroxidation, and also possesses anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties; it also acts as an effective anti-inflammatory agent by inhibiting histamine release, modulating immune cell function, and suppressing various inflammation-related enzymes (such as cyclooxygenase and lipoxygenase), and has been shown to interact with G-quadruplex DNA and may modulate RNA-related pathways. Furthermore, Quercetin is studied for its anti-allergic, cardioprotective, antiviral, and potential anticancer properties, and is widely used and researched as a dietary supplement or a component in natural medicine.
