Small_molecule_information: 2-(2-methylhydrazinyl)-1,7-dihydropurin-6-one, also referred to as G5, is a derivative of xanthine (1,7-dihydropurin-6-one) (and G5 is additionally a derivative of guanine with N-N-methyl (-N-NH-CH) introduced to its C2 position) substituted with a 2-methylhydrazinyl group at the 2-position, which can induce RNA to produce structural changes similar to natural guanine but has a weak inhibitory effect on the growth of Bacillus subtilis and only has moderate binding activity without significant antibacterial function; the hydrazine functional group is a characteristic moiety in several classes of drugs, in real activity, certain hydrazine compounds are known monoamine oxidase (MAO) inhibitors used to treat depression, therefore, this compound holds potential antidepressant or neurotransmitter-metabolism-modulating activity, and as a purine analog, it may also exhibit antimetabolite properties.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: Quinoline, also referred to as an azaaromatic compound with a planar hydrophobic benzo[b]pyridine scaffold that interacts non-specifically with the yjdF motif RNA and shows minimal activation of riboswitch-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, is one of the most important nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocycles formed by the fusion of a benzene ring with a pyridine ring with the molecular formula, and it is a colorless hygroscopic liquid with a strong odor whose skeleton forms the core of many natural products such as cinchona alkaloids and synthetic drugs such as chloroquine and quinolone antibiotics, with quinoline derivatives widely used in the synthesis of dyes, pesticides, and pharmaceuticals and exhibiting diverse biological activities including antibacterial, antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer effects.

RNA information: This RNA serves as a defined model for the pathogenic expanded repeat in FXTAS. It folds into a stable intramolecular hairpin structure characterized by repeating 1x1 nucleotide GG internal loops. These loops adopt a specific syn-anti conformation that forms three hydrogen bonds, contributing to the hairpin's significant thermodynamic stability (ΔG°₃₇ ≈ -5.6 kcal/mol). This structured hairpin acts as a high-affinity binding platform, effectively sequestering proteins like DGCR8 and Sam68. The primary function of this RNA in the study is to demonstrate that its stable self-structure poses a major energetic barrier for duplex formation with complementary oligonucleotides, a key finding that highlights the challenge of targeting it with antisense approaches.
Small_molecule_information: N-[3-[4-[[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[(2-amino-2-oxoethyl)-[[1-[3-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]propyl]triazol-4-yl]methyl]amino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]methyl]triazol-1-yl]propyl]-4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-6, is an extremely ultra-long chain bis-head benzimidazole molecule, featuring an exceptionally long linker arm with eight repeating polyamide units, and 2H-6 extends the linker to six propylamine spacers, offering slightly greater separation than 2H-5, this represents one of the longest flexible spacers in the series, this extreme length is designed to explore the upper limit of flexible linker arms on the activity of bis-head molecules, despite the increased flexibility, it remains one of the most potent analogs, indicating excellent selectivity, under physiological conditions, the molecule is likely to adopt a highly flexible random coil conformation, and this flexibility may allow it to adapt quickly to different binding sites in a dynamic environment or be used to non-specifically aggregate large intracellular molecules, although its affinity is somewhat weaker than 2H-5, the minimal difference suggests that it can still align effectively with RNA repeat spacing, in functional assays, 2H-6 restores splicing defects comparably to 2H-5, underscoring that optimized spacing near five or six linkers provides the best structural match for targeting r(CGG)exp hairpins, and it could potentially serve as a high-capacity molecular scaffold or a probe for studying biomacromolecular dynamics in real-world applications.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[(7-chloroquinolin-4-yl)amino]-6-(diethylaminomethyl)phenol;dihydrochloride, also referred to as Quinacrine Di HCl, is a quinoline-phenol heterocyclic compound presented as the dihydrochloride salt, which is an antimalarial and antiprotozoal drug with DNA intercalation properties and has been repurposed for cancer and prion diseases due to its ability to inhibit protein aggregation and nucleic acid interactions, its structure featuring a 4-aminoquinoline backbone with a 7-chloro substitution is chemically analogous to classic antimalarial drugs like chloroquine, the presence of both a phenolic hydroxyl group and a diethylaminomethyl group on the phenyl ring provides multiple sites for interaction and protonation, quinolines are a broad class of compounds known for antimalarial, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, due to its salt form the molecule is highly protonated enhancing its water solubility and the functional groups suggest potential for metal ion chelation, in reality this compound is likely explored as a novel antimalarial candidate to combat drug resistance or possibly as an anticancer agent due to its potential to interfere with metal homeostasis or kinase activity.

RNA information: r(CUG)109 is a pathogenic RNA motif composed of 109 CUG repeats that folds into a stable, elongated hairpin structure containing periodic 1×1 UU internal loops. It functions as a toxic RNA by specifically binding to and sequestering muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein, thereby disrupting its normal splicing regulation and contributing to the molecular pathology of myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1). Small molecules that selectively bind r(CUG)109 can displace MBNL1, restore aberrant splicing patterns, and alleviate disease-associated phenotypes in cellular models .
Small_molecule_information: N-[4-[(2-amino-2-oxoethyl)-[(2S)-2-[methyl-[(2S)-2-[methyl-[(2S)-2-[methyl-[(2S)-2-[methyl-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoyl]amino]propanoyl]amino]propanoyl]amino]propanoyl]amino]propanoyl]amino]butyl]-N-methyl-4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-K4NMeS, is a massive N-methylated oligopeptide-heterocycle conjugate, which is a rationally designed, modular small molecule that functions as a potent and selective inhibitor of the toxic r(CUG)exp RNA; its structure features two benzimidazole RNA-binding modules (H motifs) displayed on an N-methyl peptide backbone, with the structural core being a five-residue N-methylated (2S)-alanine chain where N-methylation is incorporated to improve metabolic stability against peptidases and enhance cell membrane penetration, and this oligopeptide backbone is connected via amide bonds to a complex bis-benzimidazole unit, a structural motif well-known as a high-affinity DNA minor groove binder, while the design of 2H-K4NMeS enables the molecule to bind multivalently to two adjacent 1x1 UU internal loops within the r(CUG)exp hairpin; functionally, it acts through non-covalent binding to displace the sequestered MBNL1 protein from the pathogenic RNA, and in DM1 patient-derived cells, it effectively ameliorates disease-associated phenotypes including correcting MBNL1-dependent pre-mRNA splicing defects, reducing the number of r(CUG)exp nuclear foci and improving the impaired nucleocytoplasmic transport of transcripts containing the expanded repeat, and the molecule exhibits improved metabolic stability compared to previous analogues and serves as the foundational core for developing advanced compounds like covalent binders, cleavers, and components for on-site probe synthesis, and the compound is designed to utilize the oligopeptide as a high-penetration vector to deliver the high-affinity DNA-binding unit into cells, holding significant potential in the development of anti-tumor therapeutics, gene silencing agents, or diagnostic probes that interfere with gene expression or replication.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: acridine-3,6-diamine, also referred to as Proflavine (commonly known as 3,6-Diaminoacridine), is commonly known as 3,6-Diaminoacridine, a representative acridine dye, structured as a tricyclic acridine core substituted by amino groups at positions 3 and 6, molecular formula C13H11N3, and Proflavine is nitrogen-heterocyclic aromatic compound that can specifically bind to the binding pocket of Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA, the acridine scaffold is characterized by extreme planarity and conjugation, in terms of real-world activity, acridine compounds are indispensable tools and drugs in biology and medicine, its most famous application is as a fluorescent stain for nucleic acids; it intercalates into the DNA and RNA double helix, emitting different colors of fluorescence depending on the nucleic acid type and aggregation state, widely used for cell cycle analysis and nucleic acid quantification, as a pharmaceutical agent, acridine dyes, due to their potent DNA intercalation and disruption effects, are developed as broad-spectrum disinfectants/antiseptics (e.g., acriflavine) and cytotoxic anticancer drugs, Proflavine can induce significant structural changes in the RNA, has a high binding affinity for 108yjdF RNA and can activate gene expression regulated by 108yjdF RNA, these characteristics indicate that Proflavine is an important tool for studying the functional mechanism of 108yjdF RNA. NADNADPH

RNA information: (5'CNG/3'CNG) denotes repetitive RNA sequences (N = any nucleotide) predominantly located in 5'/3' UTRs. Structurally, it forms stable hairpin or G-quadruplex structures via specific base pairing. Functionally, it regulates post-transcriptional gene expression. Pathogenically, expanded subtypes (e.g., CUG/CAG repeats) cause microsatellite expansion disorders like myotonic dystrophy type 1, sequestering RNA-binding proteins (e.g., MBNL1) to disrupt alternative splicing and drive disease.
Small_molecule_information: 8-(9-carboxy-4-formyl-1,3,6-trihydroxyphenazin-2-yl)-6-formyl-4,7,9-trihydroxyphenazine-1-carboxylic acid, also referred to as Dilomofungin, is an extremely complex dimeric molecule, consisting of two highly substituted phenazine units linked by a carbon-carbon bond, which is a spontaneous dimer of lomofungin formed in DMSO and structurally features a carboncarbon bond between two lomofungin monomers. The phenazine scaffold is a heterocyclic system made of two benzene rings fused with two nitrogen atoms, and many natural phenazine derivatives (like pyocyanin) are pigments produced by microorganisms with potent antibiotic and biological redox activities. This compound possesses multiple functional groups, including hydroxyl, carboxyl, and formyl groups, which lend the molecule multiple hydrogen bond donor/acceptor capabilities and high water solubility, and it exhibits higher potency than lomofungin in inhibiting MBNL1-CUG RNA binding in vitro, while also having strong binding affinity and the ability to partially rescue mis-splicing in DM1 cell models, though it has the unintended effect of stabilizing toxic CUG repeat RNA, leading to its accumulation in nuclear foci and reduced transcript turnover, which limits its therapeutic potential for myotonic dystrophy type 1. The phenazine rings establish it as a potent redox-active molecule capable of mediating electron transfer in biological systems. In reality, such structurally complex phenazine derivatives are often explored as novel antibiotics or anticancer agents, where the mechanism of action might involve DNA intercalation or the generation of reactive oxygen species to induce apoptosis. This molecule could also serve as a chelating agent or redox indicator in biochemical analysis.

Small_molecule_information: (2R,3S,4S,5R,6R)-2-(aminomethyl)-6-[(1R,2R,3S,4R,6S)-4,6-diamino-3-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-4-amino-3,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-2-yl]oxy-2-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxyoxane-3,4,5-triol, also referred to as Kanamycin, is a non-azaaromatic aminoglycoside antibiotic, with its chemical name clearly indicating its oligosaccharide structure, which is derived from aminocyclitol and aminosugar units linked by glycosidic bonds, specifically composed of three linked amino sugar ringskanosamine, 6-amino-6-deoxy-D-glucose, and 2-deoxy-D-streptamineconnected via such bonds, and it lacks planar azaaromatic features and binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis; this molecular backbone contains multiple primary and secondary amine groups, providing a high positive charge density in aqueous solution, which enables it to efficiently target the negatively charged bacterial cell membrane and ribosomal RNA, and its core biological activity stems from inhibiting protein synthesis by binding to the bacterial 30S ribosomal subunit, blocking protein synthesis and ultimately causing bacterial cell death, serving as an important treatment for sensitive bacterial infections; similar to other aminoglycosides, it represents an important class of anti-infective agents, but due to the potential for bacterial aminoglycoside modifying enzymes to recognize and inactivate the amino groups, research is continually directed towards the study of resistance mechanisms and the development of novel derivatives of this compound.

Small_molecule_information: 7H-purin-2-amine, also referred to as 2-aminopine2AP, is a fluorescently labeled purine analogue and a fluorescent derivative of adenine with the 2-position hydrogen replaced by an amino group, which is a nitrogen-heterocyclic aromatic compound, often used to study the ligand binding characteristics of RNA molecules, especially riboswitches. The structure of 2AP is similar to that of adenine and guanine, and it can be embedded in the pbuE ribose switch like adenine, which makes it suitable as a substitute for these bases to study the interaction between RNA and ligands and can be used as a probe to reveal the dynamic-thermodynamic control mechanism of the ribose switch. Functionally, 2AP is used as a probe to detect and quantify the binding affinity of ribose switches for purine ligands. When 2AP binds to the aptamer region of the ribose switch, it causes a significant weakening of 2AP fluorescence. This is because 2AP is completely surrounded by RNA in the ligand binding pocket of the ribose switch. By monitoring the changes in 2AP fluorescence, researchers can infer the affinity of the ribose switch for ligand binding, as well as the changes in the ribose switch structure before and after ligand binding.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-7-[(3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl]-1H-quinolin-2-one, also referred to as SMSM73, is a non-planar coumarin analog bearing a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and an imidazopyridine-thiazole DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge, which recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, as confirmed by fluorescence-polarization assays and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand.

Small_molecule_information: 2-(7-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-1-ium-6-yl)-6-[(3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl]-1,3-benzothiazole, also referred to as SMSM40, is based on the 1,3-benzothiazole core, an aromatic heterocycle known for its intrinsic fluorescence and applications in oncology and as probes for Alzheimers -amyloid protein, and SMSM40 is a non-planar coumarin analog with a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge. This compound is a dicationic salt: the 7-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-1-ium group at C2 is quaternized at N1, and the (3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl group at C6 is also quaternized, and SMSM40 recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand. This highly aromatic and dicationic structure makes 2-(7-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-1-ium-6-yl)-6-[(3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl]-1,3-benzothiazole highly suitable as a high-affinity DNA/RNA binding agent. Given its benzothiazole cores similarity to Alzheimers probes, it may also hold potential for the diagnosis or treatment of amyloidosis.

Small_molecule_information: 7-(3-aza-8-azoniabicyclo[3.2.1]octan-3-yl)-3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)chromen-2-one, also referred to as SMSM23, is a non-planar coumarin derivative, with the substituent at position 7 being the 3-aza-8-azoniabicyclo[3.2.1]octan-3-yl group, which has a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system, bearing a 1 charge and binding the single-G bulge of RNA via minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, validated by FP and SAR comparison, and showing 10-fold selectivity over GA-rich RNA, ranking it among the most potent and selective ligands; this is a bridged-ring structure formed by connecting a six-membered ring and a seven-membered ring across two common carbon atoms, possessing extremely high rigidity and a highly defined three-dimensional conformation, which is essential for the precise fitting of the drug molecule into the active pocket of an enzyme or receptor, and the quaternary ammonium positive charge provides enhanced water solubility and electrostatic interaction capabilities, the entire molecular structure is indicative of a molecule designed for high selectivity and affinity binding, and based on the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine group and the coumarin core, its potential activities include inhibiting tumor cell proliferation or acting as a ligand for specific G-protein coupled receptors (GPCRs).

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: (2R)-6-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]-2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoyl-propylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]amino]hexanamide, also referred to as 2H-K4, is an extremely complex, macro-oligomeric structure featuring a core repetitive polyalanine peptide chain linked to two massive terminal groups, each containing a bis-benzimidazole moiety with a 4-methylpiperazinyl substitution, and 2H-K4 is the lysine-derived scaffold lacking tertiary amide substitution, this structure is typically designed as a macromolecular DNA minor groove binder or topoisomerase inhibitor in drug chemistry, its large size, numerous aromatic rings, and multiple basic nitrogen atoms suggest a high affinity for nucleic acids, making it a promising candidate for an highly potent anti-tumor agent aimed at disrupting DNA replication and repair in cancer cells, though challenges related to cell permeability and pharmacokinetics are inherent to molecules of this complexity, and although 2H-K4 indicated some potency, its cellular uptake was poor, and toxicity was observed at higher concentrations, these drawbacks limited its suitability for therapeutic use, the compound served as a control to show the importance of tertiary amide substitution for balancing activity and safety.

Small_molecule_information: [(2R,3S,4R)-5-(6-aminopurin-9-yl)-3,4-dihydroxyoxolan-2-yl]methoxy-hydroxyphosphoryl] [hydroxy(phosphonooxy)phosphoryl] hydrogen phosphate, also referred to as AP4 (adenosine 5-tetraphosphate), is an Adenosine (6-aminopurin) nucleoside derivative linked to a linear chain of four phosphate groups via the C5 position, making it an analog of Adenosine 5-Tetraphosphate (AP4); this compound, sourced from Jena Bioscience, was tested as a candidate ligand for yjdF motif RNAs, showing low binding affinity to the RNA with a dissociation constant and failing to activate riboswitch-mediated gene expression, and due to its weak binding and lack of functional activity, it was not highlighted in the main text of the relevant study, only documented in the supplemental Table S1. ATP is the main energy currency of the cell, and this non-natural tetraphosphate structure is likely engineered as a potent inhibitor or non-hydrolyzable substrate analog of ATPases, kinases, or polymerases, often utilized in enzyme kinetics studies and investigated for potential applications as a novel anticancer drug.

RNA information: The HIV-1 frameshift-stimulating stem-loop (FSS) RNA is a highly conserved structured element located within the gag-pol transcript that regulates production of the essential Gag-Pol polyprotein through a −1 ribosomal frameshift. It consists of a stable stem-loop positioned downstream of a slippery sequence that induces ribosomal pausing and promotes frameshifting in about 5–10% of translation events. This process allows translation to continue beyond the gag stop codon, producing Gag-Pol proteins such as protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase that are required for infectious virion assembly. Altering FSS activity disrupts the Gag to Gag-Pol ratio, substantially reducing viral replication and infectivity.
Small_molecule_information: N-[(2S)-1-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-amino-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]-methylcarbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-4-[4-[(2S)-3-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-amino-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]-methylcarbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-2-[(2-ethylquinoline-3-carbonyl)-methylamino]-3-oxopropyl]triazol-1-yl]-1-oxobutan-2-yl]-2-ethyl-N-methylquinoline-3-carboxamide, also referred to as Compound 8, is a complex, linear peptidic analog that was synthesized to test the contribution of charged groups to HIV-1 FSS RNA recognition, featuring a more hydrophobic structure lacking basic amines, which led to a drastic reduction in binding, highlighted the necessity of charged groups for stable RNA interaction, and showed poor aqueous solubility that further limited its biological utility; in functional assays, this compound showed no significant antiviral activity, and its weak affinity compared to compounds with cationic substituents confirmed that electrostatic interactions play a central role in recognition of the HIV-1 FSS, thus serving as a negative control validating this principle; its structure features two (2S)-2-(N-methyl-(2S)-2-(N-methyl-N-phenylalaninamide)pyrrolidine-1-carbonyl)butanoyl units linked by a 1,4-disubstituted 1,2,3-triazole ring, with both termini acylated by a 2-ethylquinoline-3-carbonyl group, thus incorporating an aromatic quinoline heterocycle; the molecule contains multiple chiral centers (S configuration) and amide bonds, adopts a non-planar, flexible conformation; the quinoline core provides the ability to participate in aromatic stacking and hydrophobic interactions, while the triazole and amide groups can act as hydrogen bond donors and acceptors; in terms of biological function, peptides and their analogs generally have the potential to act as receptor ligands, enzyme inhibitors, or signaling molecules, and quinoline compounds are broadly known for diverse activities including antibacterial, antifungal, antimalarial, anthelmintic, spasmolytic, cardiotonic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic effects.

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 9-ethyl-3-[2-[4-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)quinolin-2-yl]ethenyl]carbazole, also referred to as Compound 31 (a green-yellow 1-methylquinolinium derivative with a 2-(2-(9-ethyl-9H-carbazol-3-yl)vinyl) group at the quinoline 2-position and a 4-methylpiperidin-1-yl group at the 4-position), is a carbazole-quinoline fluorophore, distinct from its analogues solely by the substitution at the quinoline 4-position, where a 4-methylpiperazine group replaces a piperidine ring; SPR assay shows Compound 31s affinity for A10-RNA-Wt is weaker than that of Compound 24, it was not tested in the dual-luciferase reporter assay so its effect on ADAM10 translation remains unknown, and no explicit cytotoxicity data (IC values) for it is provided in the tested compound tables, while the incorporation of the 4-methylpiperazine group, a common medicinal chemistry strategy, aims to subtly modulate the molecules lipophilicity, basicity, and pharmacokinetics, often to improve aqueous solubility or metabolic stability, and in practice, this compound retains the core function as a fluorescent probe and DNA binder, but its specific fluorescent properties, cell permeability, and potential biological activities, such as its efficacy as an anticancer agent, are likely optimized or altered compared to similar structures in its series.

RNA information: The NRQ is a 18-nucleotide RNA oligomer corresponding to the wild-type, guanine-rich sequence (5′-GGGAGGGGCGGGUCUGGG-3′) located in the 5′-UTR of the human NRAS proto-oncogene mRNA. This sequence possesses the intrinsic ability to fold into a stable, parallel RNA G-quadruplex structure, which has been previously established to act as a translational repressor by inhibiting the initiation of NRAS protein synthesis. In this study, it serves as the primary target structure for the novel MSQ derivatives. Its confirmed G-quadruplex formation, characterized by a specific CD spectrum with a positive peak at 262 nm that is enhanced in the presence of potassium ions, and its functional role make it essential for evaluating the binding affinity and specificity of potential ligands designed to stabilize this structure and further repress translation.
Small_molecule_information: 5-methyl-2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-N-(2-pyrrolidin-1-ylethyl)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-amine iodide, also referred to as the N5-methylated quaternary salt analogue of 4a-10, is a quaternary ammonium salt built upon a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core, and this compound is the N5-methylated quaternary salt analogue of 4a-10, similar to other large planar cationic molecules, it is expected to exhibit strong DNA binding/intercalation and potential topoisomerase inhibition, making it a target for anticancer drug development, the N5-methylated analogue of 4a-10 showed notable affinity for the NRQ, consistent with other methylated derivatives, it showed poor selectivity, it exhibited notable activity in A375 cells.

RNA information: AU is a fully paired RNA . It shows no binding to compound 2 . Used as a control in affinity/selectivity assays for 2, highlighting 2’s specificity for r(CUG)₁₂ over fully paired RNAs to support its role targeting r(CUG)exp in DM1.
Small_molecule_information: 1,8-diamino-3,6-dipyrrolidin-1-yl-2,7-naphthyridine-4-carbonitrile, also referred to as Compound 1 (2) (N-(3-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)propyl)-2-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)quinolin-4-amine), is a compound featuring a highly substituted 2,7-naphthyridine scaffold with multiple amino and pyrrolidine substituents, and a cyano group at position 4, with the formula C19H23N7, and Compound 1 (2), identified via Inforna, selectively binds miR-544 precursors Dicer site and r(CUG)exp U loops, naphthyridine cores are often investigated as fluorescent dyes and DNA binders, the high polarity and multiple hydrogen bond sites suggest good water solubility and potential biological activity, it is likely designed as a DNA minor groove binder, aiming to interfere with replication and transcription processes through non-covalent binding, thus having prospects in antimicrobial and anticancer research, it inhibits miR-544 biogenesis, derepresses mTOR, disrupts ATMmTOR axis (induces TNBC apoptosis to a notable extent, with significant efficacy vs antagomirs), displaces MBNL1, improves DM1 splicing/translation, and disperses foci, a single i.p. injection of a low dose abolishes MDA-MB-231 tumor growth (low toxicity), acting as an RNA-directed probe for TNBC and DM1. RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 4-amino-2-hydroxybenzoic acid, also referred to as 4-Aminosalicylic acid or p-Aminosalicylic acid (PAS), is a salicylate drug and salicylic acid derivative characterized by an amino group at position 4, a hydroxyl group, and a carboxyl group attached to a benzene ring, with the molecular formula C7H7NO3; it has a strong binding affinity for 108yjdF RNA but does not induce gene expression, suggesting that it may not trigger relevant biological function, and in terms of real-world activity, it is a historically significant antituberculosis drug that played a pivotal role in the early treatment of the disease, currently still used as a second-line agent for treating multidrug-resistant tuberculosis (MDR-TB), its mechanism of action involves acting as a competitive inhibitor of p-Aminobenzoic acid (PABA), thereby disrupting the function of dihydropteroate synthase in Mycobacterium tuberculosis, which ultimately blocks the synthesis of essential folic acid and inhibits bacterial growth, and due to its salicylic acid structure, it also possesses some anti-inflammatory properties.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC)x6: This is a six-repeat control RNA with perfect Watson-Crick base pairing. It is used to evaluate the specificity of compound binding and shows minimal structural perturbation upon ligand interaction, confirming that small molecules do not significantly bind to regular duplex RNA.; The (5'CAG/3'GUC)x6 sequence serves as the extended control duplex. Its repetitive yet perfectly paired structure lacks the distinctive features of disease-repeat RNA, allowing researchers to conclusively prove that compound efficacy is due to specific recognition of the pathological mismatch motif and not general RNA binding.
Small_molecule_information: 4-benzylsulfanyl-6-fluorocinnoline, also referred to as CP6, is a compound based on the cinnoline scaffold, substituted with a benzylsulfanyl group at position 4 and a fluorine atom at position 6, with the formula C15H11FN2S, and CP6 is characterized by a naphthalene fused with a nitrogen-rich heterocycle, featuring a fluorine atom and a benzyl group attached via a thioether linkage, cinnoline derivatives are often explored as kinase inhibitors and antitumor agents, the sulfide group (S) is a common bioisostere in medicinal chemistry, this molecule is likely investigated as a potential tyrosine kinase inhibitor (e.g., targeting EGFR), aiming to disrupt growth factor receptor signaling pathways to inhibit cancer cell growth, or as a potential treatment for cardiovascular diseases, this design resulted in excellent specificity for the AA-mismatched RNA, a significant stabilizing effect on the duplex, and potent anti-aggregation activity, making it a top lead.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoyl]amino]-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic acidThis conjugate features the core linked to Arginine and Tyrosine. Tyrosine is an aromatic amino acid with a phenolic hydroxyl group that can undergo phosphorylation by protein kinases, a critical regulatory mechanism. The compound is potentially designed as a tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) or a signaling pathway modulator, retaining the cores antineoplastic potential. It may also serve as a dual-fluorescence probe due to the intrinsic fluorescence of the quinoline core and the Tyrosine residue.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 24-methyl-5,7,18,20-tetraoxa-24-azoniahexacyclo[11.11.0.02,10.04,8.014,22.017,21]tetracosa-1(24),2,4(8),9,11,13,15,17(21),22-nonaene, also referred to as Sanguinarine, is a highly complex, polycyclic cationic compound, which can be chemically identified as the N-methyl derivative of a macrocyclic tetraoxaza-heteroatom system, and its molecular structure is dominated by its extensive ring fusion as indicated by its IUPAC name, and Sanguinarine is nitrogen-heterocyclic aromatic compound that can specifically bind to the binding pocket of Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA, inducing significant structural changes in the RNA, Sanguinarine is also a benzophenanthridine alkaloid with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and pro-apoptotic effects, it targets multiple cellular pathways, including NF-B, and is studied for anticancer applications despite potential toxicity concerns, the compounds formula, C21H16NO4, reveals a structure containing 21 carbon atoms, 4 oxygen atoms, and a single nitrogen atom that carries a permanent positive charge (azonia) due to methylation, the core hexacyclic structure suggests a rigid, cage-like framework featuring two major 12-membered rings bridged by various fused smaller rings (implied by the [11.11.0.02,10.04,8.014,22.017,21] nomenclature), leading to a highly planar and stiff molecular geometry, the presence of four oxygen atoms and nine double bonds (nonaene) indicates extensive unsaturation, and the presence of oxygen atoms at positions 5, 7, 18, and 20 suggests they are likely incorporated into ether or furan-like structures, strongly suggesting this molecule is an analog of macrocyclic dyes or natural products with rigid, fluorescent properties, potentially related to porphyrins or cryptands in shape, although Sanguinarine has a high binding affinity for 108yjdF RNA, it fails to activate gene expression regulated by 108yjdF RNA, possibly due to its toxicity to cells, nevertheless, Sanguinarine, as one of the potential ligands, provides important clues for studying the functional mechanism of this riboswitch, due to its fixed positive charge and large, planar aromatic system, this compound is highly likely to function as an intercalating agent for nucleic acids, making it a potential DNA/RNA binding fluorescent probe for biological imaging, or a promising scaffold for anti-cancer or antimicrobial drug development, where its rigidity could confer high target specificity.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: acridin-9-amine is a famous DNA intercalator. The molecule exerts its effect by inserting itself between the base pairs of the DNA double helix. Historically, it has been used as a topical antiseptic and antibiotic. In molecular biology, it is an important fluorescent dye and mutagen, used to study DNA structure, replication, and repair, and is also applied in mutagenesis studies; it binds nucleic acids, disrupting replication and transcription, and is explored for anticancer potential. Its derivatives are used in antimalarial and antirheumatic treatment.

RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 1-[4-(4-chlorophenyl)sulfanyl-3,5,6-trifluoro-2-pyridinyl]piperazin-4-ium, also referred to as Compound 4k (a 2-aminomethylene-dihydropyrrolo[3,2]-pyridine derivative), is a complex quaternary ammonium salt featuring a trifluoropyridine, piperazine, and a chlorophenyl sulfide moiety, with the formula C15H13ClF3N3S (cationic part), while Compound 4k is synthesized via ruthenium-catalyzed [222] cycloaddition, amine substitution, and Boc deprotection, binds pre-miR-21 with moderate affinity and shows good selectivity over duplex DNA/tRNA, lacks Dicer cleavage inhibition activity likely due to its methoxy-substituted aniline moiety failing to block the pre-miR-21 cleavage site (G28-A29), and its scaffold retains RNA-binding potential but requires aromatic/fluorinated modifications for enhanced bioactivity against oncogenic miR-21 biogenesis; this complex structure (of 1-[4-(4-chlorophenyl)sulfanyl-3,5,6-trifluoro-2-pyridinyl]piperazin-4-ium) is often designed to leverage the pharmacophore properties of piperazine and the metabolic stability of the trifluoropyridine scaffold, the quaternary ammonium group imparts a cationic character, potentially making it an ion channel modulator or a cell-permeable probe, and pyridine derivatives (including its related Compound 4k) have potential applications in cardiovascular, metabolic, and CNS drug development.

RNA information: 5′UCCUU/3′AUUA: This motif forms an extended internal loop with multiple unpaired bases. It was included among RNA motifs identified in the 2DCS screen but showed less prominent binding compared to shorter asymmetric loops. While its detailed involvement in viral RNA function was not established, its structural irregularity represents a potential recognition site for certain small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[3-[[2,5-bis[[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]carbamoyl]phenyl]carbamoylamino]phenyl]carbamoylamino]-1-N,4-N-bis[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]benzene-1,4-dicarboxamide, also referred to as Compound 7, is an extremely large, highly branched polycationic compound, and it can selectively bind internal loop motifs such as 5UUA/3A_U and 5UGU/3G_A with submicromolar affinity, with structural studies revealing that its binding is driven by recognition of asymmetric bulges within RNA stems, it can directly interact with HCV 3 UTR stemloops I and II by targeting motifs present in viral RNA, functional assays have demonstrated that it inhibits HCV replication in cell culture without affecting host ribosomal RNAs, and RNase H/ASO mapping has confirmed its intracellular engagement of the predicted binding sites; its intricate scaffold contains multiple phenyl rings, amide bonds, and urea groups, terminated by several 4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl substituents, structural analysis indicates that this molecule bears at least six imidazolinyl groups, making it a potent hexacationic system, this structural design is an extreme example used to construct supramolecular nucleic acid binders, where the molecular length, rigidity, and multiple charges enable high-affinity interactions with multiple sites on the DNA helix, including the minor groove, major groove, or phosphate backbone, and such compounds are often investigated as non-viral gene delivery vectors (by charge-neutralizing DNA), highly effective anticancer agents, or for use as DNA structural probes, aiming for extremely high binding stability and cytotoxicity.

Small_molecule_information: tetrasodium;[(2R,3R,4R,5R)-2-(6-aminopurin-9-yl)-5-[[[[(2R,3S,4R,5R)-5-(3-carbamoyl-4H-pyridin-1-yl)-3,4-dihydroxyoxolan-2-yl]methoxy-oxidophosphoryl]oxy-oxidophosphoryl]oxymethyl]-4-hydroxyoxolan-3-yl] phosphate, also referred to as -Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide phosphate, reduced (NADPH), is a non-azaaromatic coenzyme that contains an adenine nucleoside (adenosine phosphate) and a nicotinamide nucleoside linked by a pyrophosphate bond, with its structure being a variant of Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide (NAD) or its phosphorylated form NADPH/NADH; it has a structure of two nucleotides (nicotinamide mononucleotide and adenine mononucleotide) linked by phosphate groups in its reduced form, lacks planar azaaromatic features, binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, and its core function is donating electrons in anabolic reactions and antioxidant defense (unrelated to azaaromatic sensing), while NAD itself is an essential coenzyme in all living cells that participates in hundreds of redox reactions, making the molecule a critical metabolic intermediate used in the study of redox balance, energy metabolism, and enzymology.

RNA information: OI-guaA refers to a guanine riboswitch from Oceanobacillus iheyensis. The structural features of this ribose switch include a specific RNA secondary structure, which is usually composed of three stem-ring regions (P1, P2 and P3), and these regions are organized around a three-way junction point, forming the guanine binding site. The function of OI-guaA is to specifically recognize and bind guanine through its aptamer region, thereby regulating gene expression. When guanine binds to the aptamer region, it triggers a rearrangement of RNA structure. This structural change may lead to the termination or continuation of transcription, thereby controlling the expression of downstream genes. This regulatory mechanism of the OI-guaA ribose switch enables it to respond rapidly when the intracellular guanine concentration changes, thereby playing a key role in the metabolic regulation and gene expression of bacteria.
Small_molecule_information: 7H-purin-2-amine, also referred to as 2-aminopine2AP, is a fluorescently labeled purine analogue and a fluorescent derivative of adenine with the 2-position hydrogen replaced by an amino group, which is a nitrogen-heterocyclic aromatic compound, often used to study the ligand binding characteristics of RNA molecules, especially riboswitches. The structure of 2AP is similar to that of adenine and guanine, and it can be embedded in the pbuE ribose switch like adenine, which makes it suitable as a substitute for these bases to study the interaction between RNA and ligands and can be used as a probe to reveal the dynamic-thermodynamic control mechanism of the ribose switch. Functionally, 2AP is used as a probe to detect and quantify the binding affinity of ribose switches for purine ligands. When 2AP binds to the aptamer region of the ribose switch, it causes a significant weakening of 2AP fluorescence. This is because 2AP is completely surrounded by RNA in the ligand binding pocket of the ribose switch. By monitoring the changes in 2AP fluorescence, researchers can infer the affinity of the ribose switch for ligand binding, as well as the changes in the ribose switch structure before and after ligand binding.

RNA information: 5'CNG/3'CNG refers to repetitive RNA sequences (N = any nucleotide), commonly located in 5'/3' UTRs. Structurally, it forms stable hairpin or G-quadruplex structures. Functionally, it regulates post-transcriptional gene expression. Pathogenically, expanded subtypes (e.g., CUG/CAG repeats) cause microsatellite expansion disorders like myotonic dystrophy type 1, sequestering RNA-binding proteins (e.g., MBNL1) to disrupt alternative splicing.
Small_molecule_information: (2E,4E)-5-(1,3-benzodioxol-5-yl)-1-piperidin-1-ylpenta-2,4-dien-1-one, also referred to as Piperine (a major bioactive alkaloid and phytochemical compound derived from black pepper (Piper nigrum) that is responsible for the pungent taste of pepper and has key characteristics including low water solubility and poor bioavailability), is a compound that is either a natural product derivative or a structural analogue, typically categorized with compounds related to Piperine. Its structure is characterized by a 1,3-benzodioxol-5-yl group (a signature motif derived from Piperine and common in natural products), linked via a conjugated diene bridge (composed of two double bonds) to a piperidin-1-yl-ketone (amide) moiety. This structure is highly planar and possesses an extended conjugated system, often resulting in a colored solid with potential unique fluorescence properties. Piperine and its derivatives exhibit various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, analgesic effects, and are most renowned for their bioavailability enhancing properties by inhibiting certain metabolizing enzymes like CYP3A4, with Piperine itself pharmacologically renowned for its enhancer effect, significantly increasing the absorption and efficacy of various drugs and nutrients by inhibiting digestive enzymes and drug-metabolizing enzymes like cytochrome P450, and also possessing intrinsic anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and potential neuroprotective properties; the specific piperidine substitution in (2E,4E)-5-(1,3-benzodioxol-5-yl)-1-piperidin-1-ylpenta-2,4-dien-1-one increases its lipophilicity, facilitating its passage across cell membranes, and it is often studied as a potential active component of natural product-based drugs or as an adjuvant to enhance the absorption of other pharmaceuticals.

RNA information: The r(CUG)₆ oligonucleotide is a key in vitro model used to study the pathogenic CUG repeat expansion. Its repetitive sequence forms a stable stem-loop structure characterized by canonical GC base pairs and non-canonical, dynamic UU mismatches. This unique structural motif is the primary binding site for both the Muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein and the designed small molecule ligands. The RNA was synthetically labeled with a TAMRA fluorophore at its 5' end for binding assays. In these assays, ligand binding to the UU mismatches induces a structural change that quenches the TAMRA fluorescence, allowing the direct measurement of binding affinity (K_D), thus establishing r(CUG)₆ as a critical tool for quantifying ligand-RNA interactions.
Small_molecule_information: 9-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)amino]butylamino]-N-[2-[2-[2-[2-[[9-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)amino]butylamino]acridine-4-carbonyl]amino]ethylamino]ethylamino]ethylamino]ethyl]acridine-4-carboxamide, also referred to as Acridine 10, is an acridine-triazine dimer with acridine substituted at position 4, and the linker is a longer polyethylamino chain containing four ethylamino units (an oligoamino linker), molecular formula C60H67N21O2, and this potent dimeric ligand demonstrated strong binding and inhibition with a notable Tm, a much lower KD than the monomer, and a significant bivalent effect. In terms of real-world activity, its core function is as an ultra-potent DNA intercalator/cross-linker and topoisomerase inhibitor. Compared to 9-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)amino]butylamino]-N-[2-[2-[2-[[9-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)amino]butylamino]acridine-4-carboxamide, the increase in linker chain length means the molecule carries more positive charges, which generally further enhances its non-specific electrostatic affinity for DNA and may alter the span across the DNA double helix, affecting DNA cross-linking efficiency and topoisomerase toxicity. The synthesis and study of such compounds aim to systematically explore the influence of linker structure on the activity and pharmacokinetics of multi-target drugs, to develop more effective anticancer therapies.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 3-phenyl-3,4-dihydroisochromen-1-one, also referred to as CP11, is an isocoumarin derivative, and CP11 is a chromanone (dihydrobenzopyranone) derivative with a phenyl ring whose saturated chromone core exhibited a single, modest-affinity binding mode, lacking the desired specificity for the target RNA structure, the isocoumarin scaffold, fused from a benzene ring and a six-membered lactone ring (dihydro-pyranone), is prevalent in natural products, especially fungal and plant metabolites, these naturally occurring isocoumarin derivatives often exhibit diverse biological activities, including antibacterial, antifungal, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, 3-phenyl-3,4-dihydroisochromen-1-ones structure includes a phenyl group at the 3-position of the lactone ring, which increases the molecules lipophilicity and steric complexity, in reality, compounds with this structure serve as basic building blocks for the synthesis of various natural product analogues and pharmaceutical precursors, where researchers explore and optimize their potential pharmacological activity through further chemical modifications (e.g., introducing other functional groups on the phenyl or isocoumarin rings). RNA

RNA information: 5'CGG/3'GGC is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to fragile X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome (FXTAS), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it forms a hairpin with a 1×1 internal loop, closed by CG pairs, adopting non-canonical folding. Functionally, it contributes to FXTAS pathogenesis, likely by sequestering cellular proteins and disrupting normal splicing. As a disease-relevant RNA, it serves as a target for screening therapeutic small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-5,7-dihydroxy-3-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-[[(2R,3R,4R,5R,6S)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxymethyl]oxan-2-yl]oxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Rutin, is a naturally occurring flavonoid glycoside found in plants, known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasoprotective properties; Rutin is widely used as a dietary supplement and medicinal agent due to its potent antioxidant and free-radical scavenging activities, it stabilizes blood vessels, reduces capillary fragility, may protect against oxidative stress-related diseases, clinically it is known for its Vitamin P activity, primarily used to strengthen capillary walls, reduce capillary permeability, and treat conditions like varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and capillary fragility, it also exhibits anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties, and its poor water solubility limits bioavailability, but derivatives are explored for enhanced delivery.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 165ribD is a riboswitch that can specifically bind to FMN (flavin mononucleotide). It maintains the homeostasis of FMN and riboflavin in bacterial cells by regulating gene expression.
Small_molecule_information: 5-[8-(dimethylamino)-7-methyl-2,4-dioxobenzo[g]pteridin-10-yl]pentyl dihydrogen phosphate, also referred to as BRX830 which is a synthetic analog of FMN (flavin mononucleotide), is a phosphate-containing pteridine derivative whose structural design aims to optimize binding ability with the FMN riboswitch; its structure features the 8-(dimethylamino)-7-methylbenzo[g]pteridine-2,4-dione core, with the terminal of the pentyl side chain attached at N10-position being phosphorylated, and the core structure of BRX830 retains an isoalloxazine ring system similar to that of FMN but removes the ribityl chain present in natural FMN, replacing it with a simple alkyl chain, a design intended to reduce dependence on the phosphate group while improving the molecules passive membrane permeability, thereby enhancing its properties as a potential antibacterial agent; the molecular formula is CHNOP; this compound is similar to [5-[8-(dimethylamino)-7-methyl-2,4-dioxobenzo[g]pteridin-10-yl]-2,3,4-trihydroxypentyl] dihydrogen phosphate but lacks hydroxyl groups on the chain, making it moderately hydrophilic, and the phosphate group enhances water solubility; in terms of biological function, it can be used as an intracellular flavin-like molecular probe or a substrate for studying phosphatase activity, and functionally, BRX830 exhibits binding ability similar to FMN, as it can bind to the FMN riboswitch and induce conformational changes, thereby regulating gene expression, and through chemical probe techniques (such as SHAPE analysis) and in vitro transcription termination assays, BRX830 has been shown to effectively bind to the riboswitch and partially mimic the function of FMN, furthermore, the discovery of BRX830 provides an important structural basis and functional guidance for the further optimization of FMN analogs, especially in exploring how chemical modifications can enhance antibacterial activity and selectivity.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-(4-fluorobenzoyl)-9-methyl-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 7, incorporates a fluorine atom on the benzoyl ring which improved its binding properties, showed good affinity for CAG RNA and was used to study the effect of halogen substituents; this is a complex pentacyclic fused heterocycle based on the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one scaffold, a fused chromone and pyridine system, featuring an amino group at the 3-position, a phenyl group at the 4-position, a methyl group at the 9-position, and a 4-fluorobenzoyl group at the 2-position, and chromone structures are found in natural products and various anti-inflammatory and antiallergic drugs, while this rigid, multi-ring, highly substituted structure suggests potential as a kinase or phosphodiesterase (PDE) inhibitor candidate, potentially for anti-cancer or anti-inflammatory applications, and the 4-fluoro substitution on the benzoyl group is a common modification to improve metabolic stability and lipophilicity.

RNA information: r(AUUCU)11: This RNA is an 11-repeat unit that models the pathogenic expansion found in the intron of the ATXN10 mRNA in SCA10. Its secondary structure is characterized by periodically repeating 5'AU/3'UA base pair steps, creating an extended, repetitive binding platform. It is the primary disease-relevant target in the study, and its key functional feature is its ability to be recognized with high affinity and positive cooperativity by the designed dimeric compound 2AU-2. Binding to this RNA disrupts its interaction with proteins like hnRNP K, which is the fundamental pathogenic mechanism. This disruption, demonstrated in cellular models, leads to the amelioration of disease phenotypes, including reduced nuclear foci and improved mitochondrial function.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[5-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)furan-2-yl]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Compound 2 (DB75, furamidine), is a bis-imidamide compound where the two benzamidine groups are connected by a furan ring, with the formula C18H16N4O, and it is also a bis-cationic diphenylfuran derivative with a furamidine chromophore and smaller amidine end-groups; the introduction of the furan ring imparts some flexibility to the molecule, allowing for conformational adjustments during DNA binding, and this compound can selectively bind AU base pairs with positive cooperativity, targeting r(AUUCU) repeats and protecting AU sites from RNase cleavage, can bind HIV-1 TAR RNAs GCUCU motif via shape-driven recognition, displace Tat, block trans-activation, stabilize TAR, and has anti-PCP activity/DNA minor-groove binding, with no cytotoxicity below 30 M and serving as a TAR-targeting benchmark; these heterocyclic diamidines are a focus in the development of antiparasitic drugs, leveraging their strong DNA binding ability to effectively inhibit the growth of protozoa like Trypanosoma, and also show potential for antifungal and anticancer therapy.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: naphthalen-2-ol, also referred to as 2-Naphthol (commonly known as -Naphthol), is a crucial fundamental aromatic compound, and 2-Naphthol is a simple aromatic compound characterized by a hydroxyl group attached to a naphthalene ring, it consists of a naphthalene scaffold fused from two benzene rings with a hydroxyl group attached at the 2-position, -Naphthol is a weakly acidic white solid with a characteristic phenolic odor, which is extensively used in the chemical industry, in reality, the primary use of -Naphthol is as a key intermediate in the synthesis of various azo dyes, which are widely used for coloring textiles, paper, and leather, additionally, it is used as a raw material for synthesizing antioxidants, pesticides, preservatives, and other pharmaceuticals and fragrances, due to its structural similarity to natural phenolic substances, it and its derivatives also possess some antimicrobial and antifungal activity, the figure shows that 2-naphthol has a certain binding affinity but does not induce gene expression, indicating that it may not be an active ligand for 108yjdF RNA. -RNA

RNA information: Aptamer AC17-4, a 43-nucleotide compact core motif derived from SELEX-derived R10-6, binds ASP2905/ASP7967 with high affinity . Key residues in its three-way junction are critical for binding; loops L2/L4 tolerate substitution. It enables aptazyme/exon-skipping riboswitches, activating gene expression  in mammalian cells and regulating hEPO in mice via oral ASP7967.
Small_molecule_information: 2-N,4-N-bis(4-fluorophenyl)-6-N-(pyrimidin-2-ylmethyl)-1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triamine, also referred to as ASP7967, which is structurally analogous to ASP2905 but with an extra fluorine in the pendant phenyl group, is a compound featuring a triazine scaffold substituted with two 4-fluorophenyl and one pyrimidin-2-ylmethyl groups, with the formula C24H19F2N7, this compound can bind aptamer AC17-4 tightly, can activate AC17-4-based riboswitches in HEK293 cells as a ligand, can regulate hEPO expression in mice via AAV8 vectors when orally administered, and shows good liver distribution and low cytotoxicity in cells, meanwhile triazine derivatives are widely researched as kinase inhibitors and receptor antagonists, the presence of fluorine atoms enhances lipophilicity and metabolic stability, this complex structure suggests a multi-target inhibition potential and it is likely studied as an anticancer agent (e.g., through inhibition of the PI3K/Akt pathway) or as a broad-spectrum antimicrobial agent.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-5,7-dihydroxy-3-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-[[(2R,3R,4R,5R,6S)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxymethyl]oxan-2-yl]oxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Rutin, is a naturally occurring flavonoid glycoside found in plants, known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasoprotective properties; Rutin is widely used as a dietary supplement and medicinal agent due to its potent antioxidant and free-radical scavenging activities, it stabilizes blood vessels, reduces capillary fragility, may protect against oxidative stress-related diseases, clinically it is known for its Vitamin P activity, primarily used to strengthen capillary walls, reduce capillary permeability, and treat conditions like varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and capillary fragility, it also exhibits anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties, and its poor water solubility limits bioavailability, but derivatives are explored for enhanced delivery.

Small_molecule_information: N,N-dimethylquinoline-6-amine, also referred to as N,N-dimethylquinolin-6-amine, is a simple quinoline derivative that is a planar azaaromatic compound with a benzo[b]pyridine scaffold (one benzene ring fused to one pyridine ring, containing one nitrogen atom) and an N,N-dimethylamino group at the 6-position, which binds to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) via non-specific interactions but with lower affinity than potent ligands like chelerythrine and fails to effectively activate riboswitch-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, serving as a tool to study riboswitch ligand structure-activity relationships for azaaromatic sensing; the quinoline core is ubiquitous in pharmaceuticals, and this molecule serves as a synthetic intermediate or a template for screening antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory activities.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a short, genetically conserved stem–bulge–loop hairpin found at the 5′ end of every viral transcript.  Functional constructs range from minimal 29-nt fragments (containing the essential bulge and loop) up to the canonical 52–59-nt forms seen in full-length genomes; in all cases the active fold is the same: a double-stranded stem interrupted by a three-base pyrimidine bulge (commonly UCU) and capped by a six-nucleotide loop.  This bulge-and-loop motif first captures an arginine-rich segment of the viral Tat protein, inducing a local conformational switch that allows Tat to recruit the host Cyclin T1–CDK9 (P-TEFb) complex.  The resultant Tat–TAR–P-TEFb assembly converts RNA polymerase II from abortive to highly processive elongation, boosting viral gene expression >100-fold, and the same RNA structure also facilitates strand-transfer during reverse transcription.  Because the bulge–loop signature is indispensable and sequence-invariant across strains, TAR acts as a natural “riboswitch-like” sensor that tunes the balance between transcription and genome replication, offering a structurally small yet functionally critical target for therapeutic intervention.
Small_molecule_information: actinium;2-amino-5-carbamimidamido-N-[3-[2-(methylamino)-1,3-thiazol-4-yl]phenyl]pentanamide, also referred to as N-(2-N-Acetylamino-4-(3-aminophenyl)thiazole)-argininamide (2b), is a compound composed of a radioactive actinium (Ac) atom and a complex organic ligand, and N-(2-N-Acetylamino-4-(3-aminophenyl)thiazole)-argininamide (2b) is a synthetic nucleobaseamino acid conjugate engineered to mimic and out-compete the natural Tat peptide for binding to the HIV-1 TAR RNA stembulgeloop, the organic ligand is a derivative based on arginine (containing a guanidine group) linked via an amide bond to an aniline substituted with a methylamino thiazole ring, it replaces the labile ribose-phosphate backbone with a rigid thiazolearyl core that presents an arginine side-chain optimized for electrostatic recognition of the TAR bulge, the core function of the molecule is its role as a precursor for Targeted Alpha Therapy (TAT): the organic ligand is designed as a high-affinity chelating agent to stably bind the alpha-particle emitter Actinium-225, the ligands targeting capability (e.g., high affinity for a tumor-specific receptor) ensures that Actinium-225 is delivered highly specifically to tumor cells, enabling the precise and efficient alpha-radiation killing of the tumor, in vitro, 2b binds TAR with high affinity, triggers no global RNA conformational change, but shows reduced selectivity in the presence of excess tRNA, in HIV-infected MAGIC-5B reporter cells, 2b fails to suppress viral replication, correlating with its low specificity, these properties indicate that 2b is a potent but non-specific TAR ligand, underscoring the need for balanced charge and shape to achieve antiviral activity. RNA

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 1-methyl-2-[2-(3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl)ethenyl]quinolin-1-ium, also referred to as Compound 11, is a green-yellow 1-methylquinolinium derivative and a Cyanine Dye analog (monocationic), which has a 3,4,5-trimethoxystyryl group at the 2-position of the quinoline ring, no substitution at the 4-position, and its phenyl ring substituent is the well-known 3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl group; the trimethoxyphenyl scaffold (also known as the combretastatin scaffold) is present in many natural products with anti-microtubule activity (such as colchicine), and this compound combines the fluorescent and intercalating properties of the cyanine dye with the anti-tumor potential of the trimethoxyphenyl group, additionally it has certain affinity for A10-RNA-Wt (ADAM10 mRNAs G-quadruplex sequence) as shown by SPR assay, was not tested in the dual-luciferase reporter assay so its effect on ADAM10 translation is unknown, and exhibits cytotoxicity; in practical applications, this dual-functional structure is often explored as a novel anticancer lead compound that can both track cells via fluorescence and exert cytotoxic effects by interfering with microtubules or acting as an intercalating agent.
