Small_molecule_information: (2S,3S,4R,5R,6R)-5-amino-2-(aminomethyl)-6-[(2R,3S,4R,5S)-5-[(1R,2R,3S,5R,6S)-3,5-diamino-2-[(2S,3R,4R,5S,6R)-3-amino-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-2-yl]oxy-6-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxy-4-hydroxy-2-(hydroxymethyl)oxolan-3-yl]oxyoxane-3,4-diol;sulfuric acid, also referred to as Paromomycin sulphate, is an aminoglycoside antibiotic existing as a sulfate salt, and Paromomycin sulphate is an aminoglycoside antibiotic that binds to the 16S rRNA of bacterial ribosomes, causing misreading of mRNA and inhibiting protein synthesis and is used to treat parasitic and bacterial infections, its core structure, (2S,3S,4R,5R,6R)-5-amino-2-(aminomethyl)-6-[(2R,3S,4R,5S)-5-[(1R,2R,3S,5R,6S)-3,5-diamino-2-[(2S,3R,4R,5S,6R)-3-amino-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-2-yl]oxy-6-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxy-4-hydroxy-2-(hydroxymethyl)oxolan-3-yl]oxyoxane-3,4-diol, is similar to (2R,3S,4R,5R,6R)-5-amino-2-(aminomethyl)-6-[(1R,2R,3S,4R,6S)-4,6-diamino-2-[(2S,3R,4S,5R)-4-[(2R,3R,4R,5S,6S)-3-amino-6-(aminomethyl)-4,5-dihydroxyoxan-2-yl]oxy-3-hydroxy-5-(hydroxymethyl)oxolan-2-yl]oxy-3-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxyoxane-3,4-diol, consisting of multiple aminosugar units linked via glycosidic bonds, featuring numerous amino and hydroxyl groups, the presence of multiple amino groups makes it strongly polycationic, which is the structural basis for its biological activity, allowing it to specifically bind to the bacterial ribosome and disrupt protein synthesis, its formulation as a sulfate salt is typical for this class of drugs, enhancing its water solubility and stability for clinical applications such as injection or solution preparation, this molecule is highly likely to be a clinically relevant antibiotic, such as a derivative of streptomycin, kanamycin, or tobramycin, employed as a broad-spectrum bactericidal agent primarily against aerobic Gram-negative infections, with caution required due to the risk of ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 4-amino-1-[(2R,5S)-2-(hydroxymethyl)-1,3-oxathiolan-5-yl]pyrimidin-2-one, also referred to as Lamivudine (3TC) or its enantiomer, is a synthetic cytidine nucleoside analog where the 3 oxygen atom of the ribose ring is replaced by a sulfur atom, forming a 1,3-oxathiolane sugar ring, molecular formula C8H11N3O3S; it is a nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor (NRTI) that incorporates into viral DNA, terminating its synthesis, and in terms of real-world activity, Lamivudine is an extremely important antiviral drug, classified as a Nucleoside Reverse Transcriptase Inhibitor (NRTI), which is widely used clinically for the treatment of Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) infection and Hepatitis B Virus (HBV) infection, with its mechanism of action involving being phosphorylated intracellularly to its active triphosphate form, which is then incorporated into the viral DNA chain, causing chain termination and effectively inhibiting the activity of the HIV reverse transcriptase and HBV polymerase.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(2-chlorophenyl)quinolin-3-amine, also referred to as CP18, is a quinolinamine derivative (and an aminoquinoline derivative with a 2-chlorophenyl group) substituted with an ortho-chlorophenyl group at position 2, its amino group could enhance solubility and binding, but the compound demonstrated only moderate affinity and was outperformed by other leads in the screen; the quinoline scaffold is the core structure of many important drugs, exhibiting antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, antineoplastic, and antiarrhythmic activities, and this specific 2-aryl-3-aminoquinoline structure has been studied as a tyrosine kinase inhibitor or an antitumor agent.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a conserved stem-bulge-loop hairpin at the 5′ end of all HIV-1 transcripts, existing as 29–59-nt fragments. Its structure features a double-stranded stem, a 3-base pyrimidine bulge (e.g., UCU), and a 6-nt loop. Functionally, it binds Tat’s arginine-rich segment, recruits P-TEFb, boosts RNA Pol II processivity (viral gene expression >100-fold), aids reverse transcription, and is a key therapeutic target for HIV infections.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-N,6-dimethyl-N-[3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl]thieno[2,3-b]pyridine-2-carboxamide, also referred to as Compound 9, is a thienopyridine carboxamide derivative with an N-methylated amide group, modified from initial active analogs targeting HIV-1 RNAs, which binds HIV-1 TAR RNA and RRE RNA key for viral replication, targeting HIV-1 infection by interacting with these regulatory RNAs and showing moderate affinity while lacking further functional validation for anti-HIV-1 activity, and its core is the thieno[2,3-b]pyridine scaffold, a fused thiophene and pyridine system, substituted at the 3-position with an amino group and at the 6-position with a methyl group, with the 2-position linked via an N-substituted carboxamide to a methyl group and a 3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl group, the thienopyridine framework being a significant pharmacophore found in antineoplastic and CNS drugs, the trifluoromethyl (CF3) group being highly lipophilic and electron-withdrawing, often introduced to enhance metabolic stability and membrane permeability, and the N-substituted amide being characteristic of many kinase inhibitors and GPCR antagonists, making this molecule a strong candidate for an enzyme-targeting drug, such as a PDE inhibitor or a kinase inhibitor with anti-inflammatory/anti-cancer activity.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[[6-amino-2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]hexanoyl]amino]-5-(diaminomethylideneamino)pentanoic acid, this compound is a complex conjugate of a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core with amino acids. This structural class is known for DNA/RNA binding and topoisomerase inhibition, developed as broad-spectrum anticancer agents. Real-world applications may involve their use as novel anti-tumor leads.

This structure is a targeted drug design: the benzofuroquinoline acts as the cytotoxic warhead, and the alkaline peptide acts as a targeting vehicle to enhance cellular uptake and nuclear localization in anticancer drug development.

Small_molecule_information: 4-(2-naphthalen-1-ylethyl)pyridine, also referred to as CP16, is a coupled aromatic compound containing pyridine and naphthalene moieties, where the naphthalene ring (two fused benzene rings) is linked to the 4-position of the pyridine ring via an ethyl chain, and this flexible alkyl linkage together with the separated aromatic systems is consistent with its general, non-specific interaction with RNA; both the pyridine and naphthalene rings are hydrophobic and planar structures, which help the molecule interact with lipid membranes or hydrophobic pockets, and such structures are often used to design drugs with the ability to interact with membranes or receptors, while the basic nitrogen atom in the pyridine ring can be ionized, influencing its distribution and activity in vivo

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide, also referred to as the saturated analog of DAPI (whose full name is 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide), is a compound featuring a dihydroindole scaffold and bis-imidamide groups, with the formula C16H16N4, while DAPI is a fluorescent stain binding AT-rich DNA regions, widely used in microscopy for nuclear labeling and is cell-impermeant unless membranes are compromised, making it useful for viability assays and chromosome visualization; this compound is the saturated analog of 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide, the saturation of the indole ring removes aromaticity and significantly increases the flexibility and changes the 3D shape of the molecule, this structural change dramatically influences its binding mode and affinity for the DNA minor groove, and this compound is investigated to explore the impact of molecular rigidity/flexibility on the activity of DNA-binding drugs, showing potential for anticancer and anti-infective activity.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[3-(2-aminoethyl)-5-[4-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-2-methoxyphenyl]-2-phenylmethoxyphenyl]ethanamine, also referred to as Compound 5a (a 3,5,2,6-tetrakis(2-aminoethyl) p-terphenyl compound with a single methoxy group at the 2 position of the central benzene ring), is a derivative of 2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-[4-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-2-methoxyphenyl]phenol, where the phenol is substituted with a benzyloxy group, and Compound 5a is synthesized via sequential palladium-catalyzed Suzuki coupling reactions and was characterized by methods such as Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) and Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR); the benzyloxy group is an aromatic side chain that increases lipophilicity, and Compound 5a functionally can bind to the hairpin RNA (Bh) of the HIV-1 RRE subdomain IIB, has limited selectivity for Bh over DNA, exhibits a high half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC) in inhibiting the interaction between Bh and Rev, and shows no antiviral activity in cellular assays; as a tetracationic polyamine, its primary mode of action remains strong interaction with negatively charged biomolecules (nucleic acids, membranes), and in practical applications, this benzyloxy-substituted polyamine is often used in Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) studies to evaluate the effect of simple aromatic lipophilic side chains on the polyamine cores cytotoxicity, transfection efficiency, and selectivity, to find the optimal drug design.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: (2S,3S,4R,5R,6R)-5-amino-2-(aminomethyl)-6-[(2R,3S,4R,5S)-5-[(1R,2R,3S,5R,6S)-3,5-diamino-2-[(2S,3R,4R,5S,6R)-3-amino-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-2-yl]oxy-6-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxy-4-hydroxy-2-(hydroxymethyl)oxolan-3-yl]oxyoxane-3,4-diol;sulfuric acid, also referred to as Paromomycin sulphate, is an aminoglycoside antibiotic existing as a sulfate salt, and Paromomycin sulphate is an aminoglycoside antibiotic that binds to the 16S rRNA of bacterial ribosomes, causing misreading of mRNA and inhibiting protein synthesis and is used to treat parasitic and bacterial infections, its core structure, (2S,3S,4R,5R,6R)-5-amino-2-(aminomethyl)-6-[(2R,3S,4R,5S)-5-[(1R,2R,3S,5R,6S)-3,5-diamino-2-[(2S,3R,4R,5S,6R)-3-amino-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-2-yl]oxy-6-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxy-4-hydroxy-2-(hydroxymethyl)oxolan-3-yl]oxyoxane-3,4-diol, is similar to (2R,3S,4R,5R,6R)-5-amino-2-(aminomethyl)-6-[(1R,2R,3S,4R,6S)-4,6-diamino-2-[(2S,3R,4S,5R)-4-[(2R,3R,4R,5S,6S)-3-amino-6-(aminomethyl)-4,5-dihydroxyoxan-2-yl]oxy-3-hydroxy-5-(hydroxymethyl)oxolan-2-yl]oxy-3-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxyoxane-3,4-diol, consisting of multiple aminosugar units linked via glycosidic bonds, featuring numerous amino and hydroxyl groups, the presence of multiple amino groups makes it strongly polycationic, which is the structural basis for its biological activity, allowing it to specifically bind to the bacterial ribosome and disrupt protein synthesis, its formulation as a sulfate salt is typical for this class of drugs, enhancing its water solubility and stability for clinical applications such as injection or solution preparation, this molecule is highly likely to be a clinically relevant antibiotic, such as a derivative of streptomycin, kanamycin, or tobramycin, employed as a broad-spectrum bactericidal agent primarily against aerobic Gram-negative infections, with caution required due to the risk of ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity.

Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-3,5,7-trihydroxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Quercetin, is a natural flavonol compound and one of the most common and widely studied flavonoids in nature, with the formula C15H10O7. Its structure features hydroxyl groups on the A ring (5 and 7) and C ring (3), and a catechol moiety (two adjacent hydroxyl groups) on the B ring (3 and 4 positions). Quercetin is abundant in various plant-based foods, including onions, apples, tea, berries, and red wine. In real-world biological activity, Quercetin exhibits outstanding pharmacological properties: it is a powerful antioxidant and free-radical scavenger, effective at inhibiting lipid peroxidation, and also possesses anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties; it also acts as an effective anti-inflammatory agent by inhibiting histamine release, modulating immune cell function, and suppressing various inflammation-related enzymes (such as cyclooxygenase and lipoxygenase), and has been shown to interact with G-quadruplex DNA and may modulate RNA-related pathways. Furthermore, Quercetin is studied for its anti-allergic, cardioprotective, antiviral, and potential anticancer properties, and is widely used and researched as a dietary supplement or a component in natural medicine.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[(5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl)amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoic acid is the benzofuroquinoline core conjugated via glycine to arginine. This structure is a typical cell-penetrating drug precursor used in anticancer and antiviral research to enhance cellular uptake and nuclear targeting capabilities.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[[5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoyl]amino]propanoic acid is a peptide derivative featuring a complex benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline structural fragment, with arginine, glycine, and alanine fragments serving as linkers: one end of the molecule is attached to the polycyclic aromatic heterocycle benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline, which has a 1-methylpiperidinyl group attached via a side chain at position 7, while the other end is linked to arginine and alanine, with a calculated molecular formula of C39H49N9O7. This molecule integrates the quinoline derivative, which possesses potential antitumor activity, with the peptide chain that can enhance solubility/targeting, suggesting research potential as a novel targeted drug.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[3-(2-aminoethyl)-5-[4-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-2,6-dimethylphenyl]-2-phenylmethoxyphenyl]ethanamine, also referred to as Compound 6a (a 3,5,2,6-tetrakis(2-aminoethyl) terphenyl with two methyl groups at the 2 and 6 positions of the central benzene), is a giant tetracationic polyamine structure where the phenol is substituted with a benzyloxy group, enhancing the molecules lipophilicity and metabolic stability, which is synthesized via sequential palladium-catalyzed Suzuki couplings, can bind to HIV-1 RRE Bh with ITC-detected high-affinity binding, inhibits Bh-Rev interaction and HIV-1 replication in cell assays thus showing antiviral activity, and as a super-large polyamine molecule, its primary mode of action remains high-charge driven nucleic acid or membrane interaction, functionally, this highly complex and highly lipophilic polyamine structure may be considered a multifunctional scaffold in medicinal chemistry, used to develop lead compounds with unique cell-penetrating ability and specific intracellular localization to target complex intracellular pathways.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: [[5-(2-amino-6-oxo-1H-purin-9-yl)-3,4-dihydroxyoxolan-2-yl]methoxy-hydroxyphosphoryl] [3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-2-yl] hydrogen phosphate, also referred to as guanosine 5-diphospho-D-mannose (GDP-mannose), is a complex diphosphate sugar analog built on the Guanosine nucleoside skeleton that lacks planar azaaromatic features, structured with a guanosine 5-diphosphate (GDP) moiety covalently linked to D-mannose (its core structure is also equivalent to Guanosine 5-monophosphate (GMP) connected through a pyrophosphate bond to a hexose derivative (an oxan-2-yl ring, likely a glucose or mannose analog)); this compound binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, while in nature, as a member of nucleoside diphosphate sugars (e.g., UDP-glucose), it serves as a key donor of mannose residues in biosynthesis, supporting the production of glycoproteins, glycolipids, and bacterial cell wall components and is primarily researched for its potential to interfere with cellular glycometabolism and signaling, which may act as a substrate or inhibitor of glycosyltransferases, suggesting potential applications in anticancer or antiparasitic research by disrupting cell surface glycan structures.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a short, genetically conserved stem–bulge–loop hairpin found at the 5′ end of every viral transcript.  Functional constructs range from minimal 29-nt fragments (containing the essential bulge and loop) up to the canonical 52–59-nt forms seen in full-length genomes; in all cases the active fold is the same: a double-stranded stem interrupted by a three-base pyrimidine bulge (commonly UCU) and capped by a six-nucleotide loop.  This bulge-and-loop motif first captures an arginine-rich segment of the viral Tat protein, inducing a local conformational switch that allows Tat to recruit the host Cyclin T1–CDK9 (P-TEFb) complex.  The resultant Tat–TAR–P-TEFb assembly converts RNA polymerase II from abortive to highly processive elongation, boosting viral gene expression >100-fold, and the same RNA structure also facilitates strand-transfer during reverse transcription.  Because the bulge–loop signature is indispensable and sequence-invariant across strains, TAR acts as a natural “riboswitch-like” sensor that tunes the balance between transcription and genome replication, offering a structurally small yet functionally critical target for therapeutic intervention.
Small_molecule_information: N-[4-[2,5-dioxo-1-[4-(trifluoromethoxy)phenyl]pyrrolidin-3-yl]phenyl]-2,2,2-trifluoroacetamide (also referred to as Maybridge 15091, i.e., 5-bromo-indole-2-carboxylic acid, with the molecular formula C9H6BrNO2) is a complex organic compound with the formula C19H14F6N2O4, its structure contains a pyrrolidine-2,5-dione (succinimide) core substituted with complex phenyl groups at N1 and C3, the C3 phenyl group is attached to a trifluoroacetamide moiety, and the N1 phenyl group carries a trifluoromethoxy group, while Maybridge 15091 is a compact, halogenated heterocycle that has been repurposed as a minimalist, non-phosphorylated surrogate of the ribityl phosphate tail of flavin mononucleotide (FMN) for targeting the HIV-1 TAR RNA bulge, it gains enhanced -stacking and halogen-bond capacity by replacing the anionic ribose phosphate with a hydrophobic 5-bromo-indole-2-carboxylate core while still presenting the carboxylate as a hydrogen-bond anchor, it can disrupt the TatTARcyclin T1 complex, dose-dependently inhibit Tat-driven LTR trans-activation without affecting a heterologous Rev-RRE control, and induce chemical-shift perturbations exclusively in the 5 bulge of TAR to realize RNA-specific recognition analogous to FMN-riboswitch binding, providing a commercially available, low-molecular-weight scaffold for developing next-generation antivirals that target viral RNA instead of host proteins, this highly substituted amide and imide structure of N-[4-[2,5-dioxo-1-[4-(trifluoromethoxy)phenyl]pyrrolidin-3-yl]phenyl]-2,2,2-trifluoroacetamide is often found in drugs designed as selective hormone receptor modulators or enzyme inhibitors, for example, succinimide derivatives are characteristic of certain antiepileptic drugs, the inclusion of trifluoromethyl (CF3) groups is a common modification in drug design to increase lipophilicity, enhance passage across the blood-brain barrier, and improve metabolic stability, therefore, this compound is highly likely to be a drug discovery candidate with a specific target, perhaps for central nervous system disorders or as an analogue of non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAID).

Small_molecule_information: 2-[[4-[(2-amino-5-methyl-4-oxo-3,6,7,8-tetrahydropteridin-6-yl)methylamino]benzoyl]amino]pentanedioic acid, also referred to as 5-methyl THF (5-methyltetrahydrofolate), is a non-azaaromatic folate derivative with a pteridine core (reduced, lacking planarity), a p-aminobenzoyl group, a glutamate chain, and a methyl group at the 5-position, which binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis and whose key role is as a methyl donor in biological methylation reactions unrelated to azaaromatic sensing, this compound is an analog of Folic Acid, structurally related to the crucial anticancer drug Methotrexate (a DHFR inhibitor), and is likely a metabolite or a potential antimetabolite anti-tumor agent.

RNA information: (5'CNG/3'CNG) denotes repetitive RNA sequences (N = any nucleotide) commonly in 5'/3' UTRs. Structurally, it forms stable hairpin or G-quadruplex structures. Functionally, it regulates post-transcriptional gene expression. Pathogenically, expanded subtypes (e.g., CUG/CAG repeats) cause microsatellite expansion disorders like myotonic dystrophy type 1, sequestering RNA-binding proteins (e.g., MBNL1) to disrupt alternative splicing, underpinning disease pathogenesis.
Small_molecule_information: N-[4-[6-(furan-2-carbonylamino)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenyl]furan-2-carboxamide, also referred to as Benzimidazole 16 (2015), is a complex aromatic heterocyclic compound centered around a benzimidazole core, a planar heterocycle formed by the fusion of a benzene and an imidazole ring, and Benzimidazole 16 (2015) is a 2-phenyl-benzimidazole-4-carboxamide derivative engineered to selectively bind the toxic r(CUG)109 RNA expansion that causes myotonic dystrophy type 1, the benzimidazole is substituted at position 2 with a 4-aminophenyl-furan-2-carboxamide side chain, and at position 6 with a furan-2-carbonylamino side chain, the entire molecule is composed of multiple planar aromatic rings (benzimidazole, phenyl, and two furan rings) linked by amide bonds, creating an extended, rigid, and highly conjugated system, the amide and benzimidazole moieties provide multiple hydrogen bond donors and acceptors, this scaffold is often designed as a protein kinase inhibitor or receptor ligand, benzimidazole compounds are a privileged scaffold in medicinal chemistry due to their diverse biological activities, frequently exhibiting antiparasitic, antiviral, and anticancer properties, the activity of this specific compound is likely related to its potential as a molecular probe or as a lead compound in the search for new drugs, by mimicking the recognition surface of MBNL1, it engages the periodic UU internal loops of the CUG hairpin with notable potency in TR-FRET displacement, displaces MBNL1 from ribonuclear foci, and restores correct alternative splicing of the cTNT mini-gene in DM1 cell models without altering DMPK mRNA abundance or affecting off-target RNAs such as human A-site rRNA or r(GGCC)4, its drug-like physicochemical profilemoderate molecular weight (352 Da), cLogP 3.9, topological polar surface area 111 , and negligible cytotoxicity up to 100 Mdemonstrates that uncharged, benzimidazole-based small molecules can selectively drug structured, repetitive RNA motifs and rescue disease-relevant splicing defects.
