Small_molecule_information: 1-[4-(4-amino-6,7-dimethoxyquinazolin-2-yl)piperazin-1-yl]-3-hydroxypropan-1-one, also referred to as Compound 9 (a DPQ (compound 1) derivative with modifications on the piperazines secondary amine where R2-Hydroxyethyl and synthesized via one-step coupling), is a medicinal compound with a quinazoline-piperazine core, which serves as a typical -adrenoceptor antagonist scaffold. The piperazine ring is linked to a short, polar 3-hydroxypropanoyl side chain containing a hydroxyl (-OH) group. The introduction of the hydroxyl group is a common strategy in medicinal chemistry to increase the molecules hydrophilicity and aqueous solubility, and it may also serve as a site for metabolism. The structure suggests its design goal is to maintain the pharmacological activity of the quinazoline while optimizing the drugs physicochemical properties, and it binds the influenza A RNA promoter similarly to compound 1. In reality, it may be studied as a water-soluble -adrenoceptor antagonist for the treatment of hypertension or benign prostatic hyperplasia.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 9-[(4aR,6R,7R,7aS)-7-hydroxy-2-oxido-2-oxo-4a,6,7,7a-tetrahydro-4H-furo[3,2-d][1,3,2]dioxaphosphinin-6-yl]-1H-purin-6-one, also referred to as cIMP, is a highly stereospecific compound whose chemical name indicates a purine base (1H-purin-6-one, a tautomer of hypoxanthine or guanine) linked to a complex sugar analogue; structurally, cIMP has an inosine base, a ribose ring, and a 3,5-cyclic monophosphate ester linkage, typical of cyclic nucleotides, while the attached cyclic structure of this compound is a fused furan/dioxaphosphinane system bearing a phosphonate group, containing multiple chiral centers (4aR,6R,7R,7aS). This structure mimics the form of natural nucleosides or nucleotides, which are cornerstones of energy metabolism, signaling, and genetic material replication in biological systems; functionally, as a cyclic nucleotide analog, it may potentially regulate cyclic nucleotide-mediated signaling (e.g., via interacting with related enzymes/ion channels), but no roles like riboswitch binding or bacterial gene regulation are mentioned in the document. Consequently, nucleoside analogues like 9-[(4aR,6R,7R,7aS)-7-hydroxy-2-oxido-2-oxo-4a,6,7,7a-tetrahydro-4H-furo[3,2-d][1,3,2]dioxaphosphinin-6-yl]-1H-purin-6-one (also referred to as cIMP) are typically designed to interfere with intracellular nucleic acid synthesis or function. Its potential real-world activities include use as an antiviral drug (by inhibiting viral reverse transcriptase or polymerase to block viral replication) or an anticancer agent (by being incorporated into DNA/RNA chains or inhibiting key enzymes to disrupt cell proliferation). The stereochemical structure is paramount for its recognition by biological targets and is a critical factor in drug design.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-7-(4-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl)pyrido[1,2-a]pyrimidin-4-one, also referred to as C5, is a heterocyclic compound built upon a pyrido[1,2-a]pyrimidin-4-one core scaffold, which is a non-planar coumarin analog with a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge; it is substituted at position 2 with an 8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridine group and at position 7 with an N-methylated piperazin-4-ium cation, recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand, the pyrido-pyrimidine scaffold forms the basis for numerous drugs with reported activities in the central nervous system and oncology, the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine group is a key active pharmacophore often designed to engage with kinase active sites, the N-methylated piperazinium ring is a positively charged hydrophilic group aimed at increasing water solubility and/or enhancing affinity for anionic targets, and the molecules planar geometry and extensive conjugation facilitate efficient stacking interactions with biomolecular targets, from a structural design perspective, this compound is a candidate for developing targeted therapeutics against neurological disorders or as a potent tyrosine kinase inhibitor in cancer treatment.

RNA information: G4-EBNA1 is a stable G-quadruplex in the EBNA1 mRNA GAr region. It binds nucleolin (NCL), inhibiting translation and reducing antigen presentation to enable immune evasion. Its formation is length-dependent. Ligands like PhenDC3, PyDH2, and PhenDH2 disrupt NCL binding, restoring EBNA1 expression and antigen presentation. The structure prefers U-shaped ligands that stack on G-tetrads. Targeting G4-EBNA1 exposes infected cells to immune response, offering a therapeutic strategy against EBV.
Small_molecule_information: 2-N,7-N-bis[(E)-(1-benzylquinolin-1-ium-4-yl)methylideneamino]-1,8-naphthyridine-2,7-dicarboxamide dibromide, also referred to as NaphDH3, is a dicationic molecule with a 1,8-naphthyridine-2,7-dicarboxamide core, and NaphDH3 features a central naphthyridine core with 1-benzylquinolinium lateral groups and had good G4 stabilization and moderate FID affinity but no biological activity, the 1,8-naphthyridine scaffold is formed by the fusion of two pyridine rings in an ortho arrangement, resulting in two close-proximity nitrogen atoms, which gives this core potential for metal coordination, the side chains are the quaternized quinoline moieties attached via the 4-position of the quinoline, this compound combines dicationic character, a planar aromatic scaffold, and potential metal coordination sites, compared to the pyridine- or pyrimidine-based cores, the rigidity and shape of the naphthyridine core provide a more distinct affinity profile for DNA minor groove binding and may influence its sequence selectivity with nucleic acids, molecules like this are important in anticancer drug research because their activity may not only stem from DNA binding but also be enhanced by forming metal complexes that can cause DNA damage, for example, through the generation of reactive oxygen species. RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]-N-[2-[[(2R)-2-[[(2R)-2-[[(2R)-2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoyl-propylamino]propanoyl]-propylamino]propanoyl]-propylamino]propanoyl]amino]ethyl]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-3NPr, is a polymeric peptidomimetic linked to an ethylenediamine backbone, containing three repeating D-alanine-amide units as the linker arm, and 2H-3NPr was one of the stronger members of the NPr series, which displayed effective disruption of the r(CUG)MBNL1 complex, though in cellular assays its activity was only moderate and did not match lysine-based scaffolds with better stability, demonstrating that an intermediate spacer length confers stronger in vitro potency but other chemical properties limited its therapeutic potential; this design of the polymeric peptidomimetic, featuring a reduced number of repeating units compared to the four-unit analogues, is specifically intended for the systematic study of the effect of linker length on the binding mode and affinity of the bis-benzimidazole molecule to DNA, and this medium-length linker is crucial for precisely optimizing the molecules coverage and spatial fit within the target site, such as the DNA minor groove, while as a highly cationic DNA binder, its potential real-world applications are focused on antitumor and antimicrobial drug development.

RNA information: 5'CUG/3'CUG is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it folds into an A-form hairpin with characteristic UU mismatches, stabilized by base-pairing. Functionally, it sequesters Muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein, disrupting pre-mRNA alternative splicing, which is the core gain-of-function mechanism underlying DM1 pathogenesis.
Small_molecule_information: 1,2,3,4-tetrahydroacridin-9-amine, also referred to as 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydroacridine-9-amine, is a hydrogenated acridine derivative with the acridine core, where the benzene ring is fused with a dihydropyridine ring, and the 1,2,3,4 positions are hydrogenated, with an amino group at position 9, molecular formula C13H16N2; the compounds core is a partially saturated tricyclic acridine structure with some lipophilicity, and the amino group imparts basicity. In terms of real-world activity, such acridine derivatives, due to their unique planar tricyclic structure and amino side chain, are often studied as DNA intercalators, showing potential in anticancer and antibacterial fields; specifically, this compound shares structural similarities with the well-known Alzheimers drug tacrine (an acetylcholinesterase inhibitor), which makes it a derivative of tacrine with potential acetylcholinesterase inhibitory activity, suggesting its potential for Central Nervous System activity as a candidate for acetylcholinesterase (AChE) inhibitors in neurodegenerative diseases including Alzheimers disease treatment, and it may also possess topoisomerase inhibitory activity, making it valuable in antitumor drug development.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 17-methoxy-16-(6-pyridin-1-ium-1-ylhexoxy)-5,7-dioxa-13-azoniapentacyclo[11.8.0.0{2,10}.0{4,8}.0{15,20}]henicosa-1(13),2,4(8),9,14,16,18,20-octaene This is a prototypical bis-quaternary ammonium compound, linking the rigid core cation and a N-alkyl pyridinium cation via a long, flexible hexoxy chain. In practice, this structural class is renowned for its potent neuromuscular blocking activity. The long six-carbon chain separates the two cationic heads, allowing them to ideally bridge the two binding sites on the nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR), resulting in effective muscle paralysis. It is researched for potential use as an adjunct anesthetic during surgery for muscle relaxation.

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 2,6-dimethoxy-4-[2-(1-methyl-4-piperidin-1-ylquinolin-1-ium-2-yl)ethenyl]phenol, also referred to as Compound 3, is a green-yellow 1-methylquinolinium derivative and conjugated compound with a quinoline quaternary ammonium salt core, with a 4-hydroxy-3,5-dimethoxystyryl group at the quinoline 2-position and a piperidine group at the 4-position, the quinoline ring is N-methylated to form a permanent positive charge, substituted at the 4-position with a piperidinyl group, and at the 2-position linked via a vinylene bridge to a phenyl ring bearing 2,6-dimethoxy and 4-hydroxy (phenol) substitutions, SPR assay shows introducing piperidine has no significant effect on its affinity for A10-RNA-Wt vs unsubstituted analogs and it wasnt tested in dual-luciferase assays so its effect on ADAM10 translation is unreported, this structure is a large cationic fluorophore with an extended conjugated system and potential pH sensitivity due to the phenolic hydroxyl group, in real-world applications, such compounds are often used as biological probes for fluorescence imaging in live cells, particularly for selective staining of organelles or nucleic acids, its cationic nature and hydrophobicity suggest potential antimicrobial or anticancer activity through interference with cell membranes or nucleic acid metabolism.

RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 1,4-dihydroxy-5,8-bis[2-(2-hydroxyethylamino)ethylamino]anthracene-9,10-dione, also referred to as Mitoxantrone, is a synthetic anthraquinone derivative with the formula C22H28N4O6, originally developed as a DNA-reactive antineoplastic and immunosuppressive agent, its core structure is the anthraquinone (anthracene-9,10-dione) backbone, substituted with hydroxyl groups at positions 1 and 4, and two hydroxyethylamino-ethylamino side chains at positions 5 and 8, it is a cytotoxic agent that primarily acts by intercalating into double-stranded DNA (inserting between the base pairs of the DNA double helix) and forming stable cross-links with topoisomerase II, thereby blocking DNA unwinding and RNA synthesis, disrupting DNA replication and repair, leading to cancer cell death, this planar anthraquinone core also confers non-selective affinity for structured RNA motifs including the HIV-1 Rev-response-element (RRE) subdomain IIB, in HIV-1 cellular assays it inhibits viral replication by acting primarily on pre-integration steps (integration/uncoating), while at higher near-cytotoxic doses it additionally suppresses LTR-driven transcription and NF-B activity most likely through generalized nucleic-acid intercalation, in real-world clinical use, Mitoxantrone is mainly employed in the treatment of various malignancies including Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML), non-Hodgkins lymphoma, and prostate cancer, it is also used to treat progressive multiple sclerosis, though its use is carefully monitored due to the potential for cardiotoxicity, and the compound exemplifies an anthraquinone scaffold that can be repurposed or optimized to yield dual-action antiviral leads targeting both viral DNA integration and RNA-regulatory elements.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-5,7-dihydroxy-3-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-[[(2R,3R,4R,5R,6S)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxymethyl]oxan-2-yl]oxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Rutin, is a naturally occurring flavonoid glycoside found in plants, known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasoprotective properties; Rutin is widely used as a dietary supplement and medicinal agent due to its potent antioxidant and free-radical scavenging activities, it stabilizes blood vessels, reduces capillary fragility, may protect against oxidative stress-related diseases, clinically it is known for its Vitamin P activity, primarily used to strengthen capillary walls, reduce capillary permeability, and treat conditions like varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and capillary fragility, it also exhibits anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties, and its poor water solubility limits bioavailability, but derivatives are explored for enhanced delivery.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2,3-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium, also referred to as Nitidine, is another methylated benzophenanthridine quaternary alkaloid, similar to 1,2-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium but with different methoxy substitution (2,3-positions), and as a benzophenanthridine alkaloid it also possesses antitumor and antimalarial activity, can inhibit topoisomerases and telomerase to disrupt DNA replication, and its fluorescent properties aid in cellular imaging studies; due to its planar, rigid, and positively charged structural features, its primary activity in practice is as a DNA intercalator and cytotoxic agent, exhibiting strong anti-tumor properties, minor changes in the substitution pattern (from 1,2- to 2,3-dimethoxy) are critical in drug design to modulate the molecules charge distribution and steric bulk, which can alter its interaction strength and selectivity for specific DNA sequences or Topoisomerase isozymes, and this makes it valuable in the structure-activity relationship studies of novel anticancer agents.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 7-(2-aza-7-azoniaspiro[3.5]nonan-2-yl)-3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridine-6-yl)chromen-2-one, also referred to as SMSM21, is a non-planar coumarin derivative where position 3 is substituted with the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine group, and position 7 carries a unique double-nitrogen spirocyclic moiety: the 2-aza-7-azoniaspiro[3.5]nonan-2-yl group, bearing a 1 charge, this spirocycle fuses a four-membered ring and a six-membered ring through a shared atom, imparting high rigidity and specific spatial arrangement, which is beneficial for achieving high target binding specificity, particularly for receptors or enzymes requiring highly specific ligand binding, the quaternary ammonium positive charge, located on the six-membered ring, enhances water solubility and reinforces electrostatic interactions with anionic biomolecules, it recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand, and the overall highly optimized structure suggests its potential as a highly selective ligand or inhibitor targeting specific receptors like GPCRs or kinases.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-9-bromo-2-(4-methoxybenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 15, is a highly substituted chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, and Compound 15 was identified as a top lead compound with very high affinity and exceptional selectivity for r(CAG)exp RNA, the chromene moiety is utilized in antiviral and antitumor drug design, the presence of a bromo atom, amino group, and benzoyl group suggests it may act as a potent enzyme inhibitor or receptor antagonist, potentially blocking key processes in cancer cell proliferation, its bromine substituent enhanced RNA binding through additional halogen bonding, it significantly reduced polyQ aggregates, was cell-permeable, and showed low toxicity. RNA information: The influenza A virus RNA promoter features a bent A-form helical structure with a dynamic (A–A)–U internal loop that serves as a specific binding site for the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) and nucleoprotein complex. This unique structural motif exhibits conformational flexibility that facilitates recognition by viral replication machinery while maintaining overall A-form geometry. The promoter's distinctive architecture and dynamic properties are essential for initiating viral RNA synthesis, making it a critical functional element in the influenza replication cycle and a promising target for antiviral therapeutic development; the influenza A virus RNA promoter is a highly conserved structural element located at the 3' and 5' ends of each viral RNA segment. Its structure, determined by NMR spectroscopy, adopts a panhandle-like conformation characterized by an A-form helix. A key structural feature is a unique (A-A)-U internal loop and a C-A mismatch pair. Furthermore, the promoter exhibits a significant bend of approximately 46 ± 10° near the viral RNA initiation site. Functionally, this specific architecture is specifically recognized by the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp), which binds to it to initiate both transcription and replication of the viral genome. The high conservation of its sequence across strains and its intolerance to mutations, which are detrimental to viral replication, establish the influenza RNA promoter as a novel and promising druggable target for antiviral development.; the influenza A virus RNA promoter is a highly conserved structural element located at the 3' and 5' ends of each viral RNA segment. Small_molecule_information: 1-[4-(4-amino-6,7-dimethoxyquinazolin-2-yl)piperazin-1-yl]propan-1-one, also referred to as Compound 6, is a compound containing quinazoline and piperazine structures, which is a derivative of compound 1 where the piperazine secondary amine is modified with a propionyl group (R Et); its structure is based on the quinazoline parent skeleton, substituted at C2 with a piperazinyl group, where the piperazine N1 is further substituted with a propionyl group, this structure is related to the Prazosin class of quinazolines, it binds to the influenza RNA promoter and exhibits antiviral activity with no cytotoxicity, resulting in a favorable IC/CC ratio, and in terms of biological function, quinazoline compounds generally exhibit diverse biological activities such as -receptor blockade (antihypertensive), antitumor, and related pharmacological effects.

RNA information: The expanded RNA repeat r(G₄C₂)ₑₓₚ, the genetic cause of c9ALS/FTD, dynamically folds between a G-quadruplex and a hairpin structure featuring 1x1 nucleotide GG internal loops. The hairpin form is the predominant and functionally relevant structure within cells, where it mediates disease pathogenesis by two key mechanisms: it sequesters RNA-binding proteins into nuclear foci, and it undergoes repeat-associated non-ATG (RAN) translation to generate neurotoxic dipeptide repeat proteins. This specific hairpin conformation, particularly its GG internal loops, is thus a critical therapeutic target for ameliorating toxicity, as its selective targeting inhibits both protein sequestration and RAN translation.
Small_molecule_information: 3,6-bis[(E)-2-(1-methylpyridin-1-ium-4-yl)ethenyl]-9H-carbazole diiodide, also referred to as BMVC/BMVC2/BMVC4, is a dicationic compound with a carbazole scaffold and two iodide ions, belonging to the class of styryl dye derivatives; this molecule, which is a series of carbazole derivatives and well-known fluorescent probes with high affinity for DNA/RNA G-quadruplex structures and served as representative G-quadruplex ligands in a related study (despite binding strongly to the G-quadruplex form of r(G4C2)exp in vitro but being poor inhibitors of RAN translation in cellular models), features two 1-methylpyridin-1-ium cationic groups connected to the carbazole core at positions 3 and 6 via ethenyl bridges, forming an extended conjugated system, and this structure provides excellent fluorescent properties, with its real-world applications mainly in fluorescent biological probes, DNA staining agents, and sensitizers for Photodynamic Therapy (PDT); as a high quantum yield fluorescent probe, it can be used for specific labeling of DNA or mitochondria in live cells, and in PDT, it absorbs light at specific wavelengths to generate Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS), thereby selectively killing cancer cells.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (7S,9S)-7-[(2R,4S,5R,6S)-4-amino-5-hydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxy-6,9,11-trihydroxy-9-(2-hydroxyacetyl)-4-methoxy-8,10-dihydro-7H-tetracene-5,12-dione, also referred to as Epirubicin, is the chemical name for, or a very close analogue of, the anthracycline antibiotic Epirubicin, which is an anthracycline chemotherapeutic agent that intercalates into DNA, inhibiting topoisomerase II and causing DNA strand breaks; Epirubicin is a widely used chemotherapy drug in clinical practice for treating various cancers such as breast cancer and ovarian cancer, and its mechanism involves DNA intercalation and Topoisomerase II inhibition.

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[2-(4-fluoro-3-methoxyphenyl)ethenyl]-1-methylquinolin-1-ium, also referred to as Compound 10, is a quinolinium quaternary salt, linked at the 2-position via a vinyl bridge to a fluorinated and methoxylated phenyl ring, and Compound 10 is a 1-methylquinolinium derivative with a 4-fluoro-3-methoxystyryl group at the 2-position of the quinoline ring, synthesized via general procedure B, this forms a large, conjugated cationic structure, in real activity, such conjugated quaternary salts are studied as potential fluorescent dyes for bioimaging, the cationic nature also lends itself to cytotoxicity and antimicrobial activity, likely through electrostatic interactions and intercalation with nucleic acids, it binds the G-quadruplex-forming sequence (QGRS) in ADAM10 mRNAs 5-UTR with moderate affinity and low interaction with mutant QGRS, however, it shows weak ability to up-regulate ADAM10 translation, lacking robust activity in dual-luciferase assays, likely due to suboptimal structural for RNA G-quadruplex modulation.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 4-(1H-benzimidazol-2-yl)benzenecarbothioamide, also referred to as 2-(4-Thiocarbamoylphenyl)benzimidazole, is a combination of benzimidazole and carbothioamide structures, and 2-(4-Thiocarbamoylphenyl)benzimidazole is a heterocyclic small molecule with a benzimidazole core linked to a thiocarbamoyl-substituted phenyl group, the benzimidazole core is often associated with antiparasitic, antifungal, antiviral, and antineoplastic activities, the carbothioamide group may enable the molecule to act as a metal chelator or enzyme inhibitor, the structure suggests the molecule may serve as a broad-spectrum antimicrobial agent, acting on cell division or specific enzyme systems, unlike myricetin, it lacks reported affinity for CAG-repeat RNAs in HD/SCAs but may interact with structured RNAs via benzimidazole intercalation and thiocarbamoyl H-bonding, it shows no validated activity against polyQ aggregation or mitochondrial dysfunction in HD models, though its benzimidazole motif could potentially target nucleic acid mismatches, making it a speculative scaffold for TRED-focused optimization with further biophysical validation needed. -

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: N,N-diethyl-N-(5-methyl-10H-indolo[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl)propane-1,3-diamine iodide is an indoloquinoline quaternary ammonium salt derivative. The structure features the indoloquinoline scaffold, with the nitrogen at the 5-position quaternized by a methyl group, and a diethylpropanediamine side chain attached at the 11-position, with iodide as the counterion (molecular formula C23H29N4I). This compound exhibits a planar conjugated system and a permanent positive charge, which enhances its affinity for DNA. In biological function, such quaternary ammonium salt derivatives typically demonstrate stronger cytotoxicity and more efficient DNA intercalation capabilities compared to their neutral counterparts, making them promising anti-tumor drug candidates.

RNA information: This RNA serves as a defined model for the pathogenic expanded repeat in FXTAS. It folds into a stable intramolecular hairpin structure characterized by repeating 1x1 nucleotide GG internal loops. These loops adopt a specific syn-anti conformation that forms three hydrogen bonds, contributing to the hairpin's significant thermodynamic stability (ΔG°₃₇ ≈ -5.6 kcal/mol). This structured hairpin acts as a high-affinity binding platform, effectively sequestering proteins like DGCR8 and Sam68. The primary function of this RNA in the study is to demonstrate that its stable self-structure poses a major energetic barrier for duplex formation with complementary oligonucleotides, a key finding that highlights the challenge of targeting it with antisense approaches.
Small_molecule_information: N-[3-[4-[[(2-amino-2-oxoethyl)-[2-[[2-[[1-[3-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]propyl]triazol-4-yl]methylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]amino]methyl]triazol-1-yl]propyl]-4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-1, is a massive and structurally complex bis-head benzimidazole derivative, while 2H-1 itself is a dimeric small molecule in which two Ht-N3 modules are joined by a single propylamine linker, establishing the shortest spacing in the 2H series; its core active unit is the bis-benzimidazole-piperazine structure, a classic DNA minor groove binder, and the 2H-1s arrangement allows simultaneous recognition of adjacent 5CGG/3GGC motifs but provides limited preorganization of the scaffold, which slightly enhances potency compared to Ht-N3, and it exhibits certain potency with moderate selectivity, despite improved activity relative to the monomer, its structural flexibility and suboptimal spacing reduce its efficiency compared to longer-spacer analogs such as 2H-5; the molecule utilizes a triazole ring as a rigid attachment point, connected by a relatively long polyamide chain (composed of two repeating units) to link the two benzimidazole active units, this bis-head design combined with a flexible spacer arm aims to enable the molecule to simultaneously bind two sites in the DNA minor groove, or act as a DNA cross-linker, thereby dramatically increasing binding affinity and cellular toxicity, this compound represents the design of multivalent ligands in medicinal chemistry, with potential application as an antitumor drug or a targeting vector in gene therapy, leveraging long chain flexibility to achieve efficient cross-linking.

RNA information: pre-miR-21 is a stem-loop non-coding RNA precursor with a distinct structure, containing G28-A29 (Dicer-TRBP cleavage site) in the stem and single-stranded bulges/apical loops (small-molecule binding pockets). It processes into oncogenic mature miR-21; ligands like 4a or bPGN  bind specifically, inhibiting Dicer processing, upregulating tumor suppressors (PDCD4, PTEN), and suppressing cancer cell proliferation. It is a druggable target for cancers such as colorectal cancer.
Small_molecule_information: 1-(2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridin-6-ylmethyl)benzimidazol-2-amine, also referred to as 4q, is composed of two heterocyclic systems: a 2-aminobenzimidazole and a 2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine, and is a 2-amino-dihydropyrrolopyridine-based RNA ligand synthesized via a modular route involving Ru-catalyzed [222] cycloaddition, nucleophilic substitution (with a heterocyclic nucleophile), and Boc deprotection. The benzimidazole scaffold is common in pharmaceuticals, including proton pump inhibitors and antiviral drugs. The pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine core contains a saturated, flexible amine fragment (the dihydro-pyrrole), which typically aids in aqueous solubility and receptor binding. The two heterocycles are connected by a methylene (-CH2-) linker. Classified as a small molecule polyheterocyclic amine, its structural features suggest it may be designed as a ligand for G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), particularly those for neurotransmitters like histamine or serotonin, while it also exhibits weak affinity for pre-miR-21, far lower than active analogs like 4a, and unlike potent inhibitors, it fails to effectively block Dicer-mediated pre-miR-21 processing, indicating its heterocyclic substitution does not support robust RNA binding or anticancer-related biogenesis inhibition. In research, such molecules are frequently pursued for Central Nervous System disorders and anti-inflammatory drugs.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: bis(2-acetamidoacetic acid);4-[2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)iminohydrazinyl]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Diminazine, is a complex salt molecule consisting of a large cationic formamidine hydrazonebased diamidine species and two 2-acetamidoacetic acid anions, and the ,-diamidine core connected through a biphenyl and hydrazone bridge corresponds to the class of aromatic diamidine compounds used as antiprotozoal agents that exert activity by binding to the minor groove of DNA, with such cationic diamidines commonly displaying antiprotozoal, antifungal, antiviral, anticancer, or broad-spectrum antimicrobial properties.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 7H-purine, also referred to as a planar hydrophobic bicyclic azaaromatic compound that interacts non-specifically with the yjdF motif RNA and shows minimal activation of riboswitch-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, is a bicyclic nitrogen-containing aromatic system formed by the fusion of a pyrimidine ring and an imidazole ring with the molecular formula CHN, and it represents one of the most essential structural frameworks in living organisms as the backbone of DNA and RNA bases such as adenine and guanine and of key coenzymes including ATP and NADH, with purine derivatives playing crucial roles in genetic information transfer, energy metabolism, and signal transduction and serving widely in medicinal chemistry as the basis for anticancer, antiviral, and immunosuppressive agents.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: (7S,9S)-7-[(2R,4S,5R,6S)-4-amino-5-hydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxy-6,9,11-trihydroxy-9-(2-hydroxyacetyl)-4-methoxy-8,10-dihydro-7H-tetracene-5,12-dione, also referred to as Epirubicin, is the chemical name for, or a very close analogue of, the anthracycline antibiotic Epirubicin, which is an anthracycline chemotherapeutic agent that intercalates into DNA, inhibiting topoisomerase II and causing DNA strand breaks; Epirubicin is a widely used chemotherapy drug in clinical practice for treating various cancers such as breast cancer and ovarian cancer, and its mechanism involves DNA intercalation and Topoisomerase II inhibition.

RNA information: The r(CGG)12 sequence is a CGG-repeat RNA motif derived from the FMR1 5′UTR that functions as a protein-binding ribo-regulator. In cells it folds into a hairpin displaying multiple 5′CGG/3′GGC internal loops; these loops act as tandem recognition sites for RNA-processing factors such as DGCR8, Sam68 and hnRNP A2/B1. By sequestering these proteins into nuclear foci, r(CGG)12 perturbs alternative pre-mRNA splicing and, in the absence of an AUG start codon, it also templates repeat-associated non-ATG (RAN) translation to produce toxic poly-glycine-containing peptides. Thus, r(CGG)12 behaves like a disease-linked riboswitch-like element whose “ligand” is the protein machinery it binds, and its homeostatic imbalance drives the molecular pathology of fragile-X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome (FXTAS).

In terms of biological function, pteridine compounds are well-known and attract attention in medicinal chemistry for their potential anticancer and diverse pharmacological features.

Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-benzoyl-9-bromo-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 14, is a four-ring heterocycle based on the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one scaffold, the molecule is substituted with a phenyl group at 4, a bromo atom at 9, and a benzoyl group at 2, compared to some of its analogues, this compound lacks the para-fluoro atom on the benzoyl group at position 2, this defluorination is a common optimization step in drug discovery used to finely tune the molecules electronic distribution and lipophilicity, due to its inherent planarity and multiple aromatic rings, the structure of this compound suggests it is a potential DNA intercalator or small molecule inhibitor, in research, similar large planar heterocyclic ketones are often explored for their therapeutic potential against proliferative diseases, achieved through high-affinity binding to key intracellular targets, and this compound which contains a bromine atom showed high binding affinity to r(CAG)exp RNA, was effective in biophysical assays and reduced polyQ aggregation in cells.

RNA information: (5'CNG/3'CNG) denotes repetitive RNA sequences (N = any nucleotide) predominantly located in 5'/3' UTRs. Structurally, it forms stable hairpin or G-quadruplex structures via specific base pairing. Functionally, it regulates post-transcriptional gene expression. Pathogenically, expanded subtypes (e.g., CUG/CAG repeats) cause microsatellite expansion disorders like myotonic dystrophy type 1, sequestering RNA-binding proteins (e.g., MBNL1) to disrupt alternative splicing and drive disease.
Small_molecule_information: methyl 7-hydroxy-6-(hydroxymethylidene)-4,9-dioxo-10H-phenazine-1-carboxylate, also referred to as lomofungin, is a derivative of phenazine, a core structure found in natural pigments with anthraquinone-like features, substituted with hydroxyl, keto, and methyl carboxylate groups; it is a natural antimicrobial agent produced by Streptomyces lomondensis that undergoes spontaneous dimerization in DMSO to form dilomofungin, and in real activity, phenazine compounds are well-known for their redox activity, enabling them to generate reactive oxygen species, this compound has potential antimicrobial activity as certain phenazine natural products are potent antibiotics, while it has also been identified as a potent inhibitor of MBNL1-CUG RNA binding, binds selectively to RNA hairpins containing CUG repeats particularly those with UU mismatches and disrupts the interaction between MBNL1 protein and pathogenic CUGexp RNA, in cellular models of myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) it partially restores MBNL1-dependent alternative splicing and increases the diffuse nucleoplasmic localization of MBNL1 indicating functional reversal of RNA toxicity, though its dimeric form dilomofungin, while more potent in vitro, unexpectedly stabilizes CUGexp RNA and leads to its accumulation in nuclear foci highlighting a potential limitation in the therapeutic use of high-affinity RNA ligands, and these findings suggest that it represents a promising scaffold for developing small-molecule therapies against DM1 provided that off-target effects on RNA turnover are carefully managed.

Small_molecule_information: (2S)-2-[[4-[(2-amino-4-oxo-5,6,7,8-tetrahydro-3H-pteridin-6-yl)methyl-formylamino]benzoyl]amino]pentanedioic acid, also referred to as 10-Formyl tetrahydrofolate (10-formyl THF), is a reduced folate derivative closely related to Tetrahydrofolate (THF) and the active one-carbon donor cofactors, and it is a non-azaaromatic folate derivative structured with a tetrahydrofolate (THF) core and a formyl group (-CHO) attached to its 10-position (instead of the 5-position in folinic acid) which lacks planar azaaromatic features, binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis; specifically, the N-5 formyl and N-10 substitution pattern makes its structure resemble N{10}-Formyltetrahydrofolate, and this molecule acts as a crucial cofactor for one-carbon unit metabolism, transferring formyl units specifically for the early steps of purine synthesis which is essential for building the purine rings in DNA and RNA while also supplying the formyl group required for the synthesis of C-2 and C-8 positions of the purine ring, and its research value in the real world lies in its use as a probe or inhibitor for folate metabolism-related enzymes, which is important for understanding cell proliferation and metabolic disorders such as megaloblastic anemia and cancer.

RNA information: 5′GAG/3′C_C: This motif represents a small asymmetric internal loop, characterized by a GAG sequence opposite two nucleotides with a bulge. It was found to bind RNA-targeted small molecules selectively in combinatorial screening. Although direct antiviral activity linked to this motif was not detailed, its enrichment in binding assays indicates its potential as a privileged RNA element for selective ligand recognition.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[3-[[2,5-bis[[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]carbamoyl]phenyl]carbamoylamino]phenyl]carbamoylamino]-1-N,4-N-bis[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]benzene-1,4-dicarboxamide, also referred to as Compound 7, is an extremely large, highly branched polycationic compound, and it can selectively bind internal loop motifs such as 5UUA/3A_U and 5UGU/3G_A with submicromolar affinity, with structural studies revealing that its binding is driven by recognition of asymmetric bulges within RNA stems, it can directly interact with HCV 3 UTR stemloops I and II by targeting motifs present in viral RNA, functional assays have demonstrated that it inhibits HCV replication in cell culture without affecting host ribosomal RNAs, and RNase H/ASO mapping has confirmed its intracellular engagement of the predicted binding sites; its intricate scaffold contains multiple phenyl rings, amide bonds, and urea groups, terminated by several 4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl substituents, structural analysis indicates that this molecule bears at least six imidazolinyl groups, making it a potent hexacationic system, this structural design is an extreme example used to construct supramolecular nucleic acid binders, where the molecular length, rigidity, and multiple charges enable high-affinity interactions with multiple sites on the DNA helix, including the minor groove, major groove, or phosphate backbone, and such compounds are often investigated as non-viral gene delivery vectors (by charge-neutralizing DNA), highly effective anticancer agents, or for use as DNA structural probes, aiming for extremely high binding stability and cytotoxicity.

RNA information: HIV-1 FS RNA is a highly conserved RNA stem-loop element that drives programmed −1 ribosomal frameshifting at the gag-pol junction. By transiently pausing translating ribosomes on the slippery sequence UUUUUUA, it enforces a precise ~20:1 Gag:Gag-Pol protein ratio that is essential for proper virion assembly and replication. Small-molecule ligands such as DB213/RG501 bind in the major groove of the stem-loop, thermally stabilize the RNA and increase frameshifting efficiency; this shift in the Gag/Gag-Pol ratio blocks particle formation, making the frameshift site an attractive, druggable RNA target for novel anti-HIV therapeutics.
Small_molecule_information: 1-N,4-N-bis[3-(dimethylamino)propyl]benzene-1,4-dicarboximidamide, also referred to as DB213 (1,4-bis-[N-(3-N,N-dimethylpropyl)amidino]benzene tetrahydrochloride), this compound is a benzodicarboximidamide derivative. The combination of the amidine structure and polyamine side chains often endows compounds with the ability to interact with DNA or RNA, and this compound is specifically an RNA-binding small molecule designed to target the HIV-1 frameshift site (FS) RNA stem-loop, which mimics the electrostatic and groove-binding properties of natural ligands but achieves enhanced stability and specificity through its bisamidinium-benzene core flanked by dimethylaminopropyl arms; in vitro, it binds in the major groove of the FS RNA across multiple base pairs, forms hydrogen bonds with phosphate oxygens, and elevates the RNA melting temperature, thereby increasing programmed 1 ribosomal frameshifting efficiency, a shift that disrupts the precise Gag:Gag-Pol ratio required for virion assembly and potently inhibits HIV-1 replication in both acute and chronic cell-culture models while exhibiting a favorable therapeutic index, and such compounds are frequently used in medicinal chemistry as potential antiparasitics, antifungals, or anti-tumor agents, and may also be explored for their inhibitory activity against serine proteases due to their amidine structure.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: acridin-9-amine is a famous DNA intercalator. The molecule exerts its effect by inserting itself between the base pairs of the DNA double helix. Historically, it has been used as a topical antiseptic and antibiotic. In molecular biology, it is an important fluorescent dye and mutagen, used to study DNA structure, replication, and repair, and is also applied in mutagenesis studies; it binds nucleic acids, disrupting replication and transcription, and is explored for anticancer potential. Its derivatives are used in antimalarial and antirheumatic treatment.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]-N-[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]ethyl]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-5G, is a very high molecular weight bicephalic compound, where the two benzimidazole active cores are separated by an extremely long linker composed of six repeating glycinamide units (while 2H-5G itself has a five-glycine linker) and maintained micromolar activity, though somewhat reduced relative to 2H-3G and 2H-4G, this ultra-long, flexible spacer provides the molecule with the ability to span large distances within a biological system and achieve multipoint binding, and the compound still demonstrated significant disruption of the RNAprotein complex but was not the most optimized variant, its performance suggests that excessive linker length can slightly compromise binding affinity, the benzimidazole and piperazine structures collaboratively provide cationic characteristics and aromatic stacking capabilities, making it an ideal scaffold for nucleic acid binding agents, additional biochemical or cellular data were not reported, and the molecule served primarily as part of the systematic length-dependence study, in practical applications, such giant molecules could be developed as highly specific multivalent ligands, for example, to study receptor clustering or as a component of gene transfection reagents by forming stable complexes with nucleic acids to facilitate cellular uptake.

RNA information: r(GGCC)4 is a short, double-stranded RNA motif composed of four tandem GGCC repeats that adopts a fully base-paired hairpin structure. It is commonly used in competition assays as a negative control because it lacks internal loops or single-stranded regions and therefore does not bind to RNA-targeted small molecules such as CB253, which selectively recognize the hairpin form of r(G4C2)exp RNA .
Small_molecule_information: 6-nitro-2-[3-(6-nitro-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl)propyl]-1H-benzimidazole, also referred to as Benzimidazole 1 (2015), is a symmetric or near-symmetric molecule composed of two benzimidazole units connected by a flexible three-carbon (propyl) chain, with each benzimidazole ring carrying a nitro group at the 6-position, and Benzimidazole 1 (2015) is a 2-phenyl-benzimidazole-4-carboxamide derivative optimized for selective recognition of the pathogenic r(CUG)109 RNA that causes myotonic dystrophy type 1, the benzimidazole scaffold, fused from a benzene ring and an imidazole ring, is a core structure in many pharmaceuticals (such as antifungals and anthelmintics), the two benzimidazole units are linked by a flexible alkyl chain, allowing the Small_molecule_information: 3-bromo-2-phenylquinoline, also referred to as CP12, is a brominated quinoline derivative with a phenyl substitution and is a bromine-containing aromatic heterocyclic compound. Its structure is based on the quinoline core: a benzene ring is fused with a pyridine ring to form the quinoline skeleton, with a phenyl group substituted at the 2-position and a bromine atom at the 3-position. The molecular formula is C15H10BrN. This compound exhibits a planar conjugated system, where the free rotation between the phenyl group and the quinoline ring is restricted. The bromine atom reduces the electron density on the ring and enables the molecule to participate in halogen bonding. In terms of biological function, quinoline compounds are generally known for possessing a variety of biological activities, including antibacterial, antifungal, antimalarial, anthelmintic, antispasmodic, cardiotonic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic effects.

Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-3,5,7-trihydroxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Quercetin, is a natural flavonol compound and one of the most common and widely studied flavonoids in nature, with the formula C15H10O7. Its structure features hydroxyl groups on the A ring (5 and 7) and C ring (3), and a catechol moiety (two adjacent hydroxyl groups) on the B ring (3 and 4 positions). Quercetin is abundant in various plant-based foods, including onions, apples, tea, berries, and red wine. In real-world biological activity, Quercetin exhibits outstanding pharmacological properties: it is a powerful antioxidant and free-radical scavenger, effective at inhibiting lipid peroxidation, and also possesses anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties; it also acts as an effective anti-inflammatory agent by inhibiting histamine release, modulating immune cell function, and suppressing various inflammation-related enzymes (such as cyclooxygenase and lipoxygenase), and has been shown to interact with G-quadruplex DNA and may modulate RNA-related pathways. Furthermore, Quercetin is studied for its anti-allergic, cardioprotective, antiviral, and potential anticancer properties, and is widely used and researched as a dietary supplement or a component in natural medicine.

RNA information: NA 4 (Drosha and adjacent 515) is an RNA fragment mimicking the Drosha processing site and its flanking region in pri-miR-515. Its core structural feature is the simultaneous inclusion of the Drosha processing site motif unique to pri-miR-515 and the adjacent Compound 1-binding motif, and the combination of these two motifs provides conditions for dual-site binding.; RNA 4 (Drosha and adjacent 515) is an RNA fragment mimicking the Drosha processing site and its flanking region in pri-miR-515. Its core structural feature is the simultaneous inclusion of the Drosha processing site motif unique to pri-miR-515 and the adjacent Compound 1-binding motif, and the combination of these two motifs provides conditions for dual-site binding.
Small_molecule_information: N-[3-[4-[[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[(2-amino-2-oxoethyl)-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]amino]methyl]triazol-1-yl]propyl]-4-[2,6-ditert-butyl-4-[5-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as Compound 1a (a peptoid derivative of Compound 1 formed by attaching Compound 1 to a peptoid linker that serves as a control to evaluate the impact of the peptoid scaffold on binding affinity), is a huge, modular molecule combining a well-known bis-benzimidazole DNA-binding unit with a 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol moiety, linked via a triazole-propylamine spacer to a long, flexible poly(glycinamide)-like chain; binding assays have shown that it binds to RNA 4 and compared with Compound 1, it only exerts a modest effect on the binding affinity, which confirms that the peptoid scaffold does not contribute significantly to RNA binding, and its structure suggests a dual mechanism or application: the bis-benzimidazole part confers potent nucleic acid binding capability, suitable for development as an anticancer agent, while the di-tert-butylphenol moiety is a recognized antioxidant or free radical scavenger pharmacophore, and in reality, such complex conjugates are explored as chemical tools or advanced drug delivery systems to achieve high affinity binding to biological macromolecules, possibly serving as gene delivery vectors or novel therapeutics.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[[5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoyl]amino]pentanedioic acidThis is a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline-peptide conjugate similar to 2-[[5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoyl]amino]butanedioic acid, maintaining the potentially cytotoxic benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core. The peptide chain consists of Arginine and Glutamic acid. As an acidic amino acid, Glutamic acid enhances hydrophilicity. The molecule is likely designed as a cell-penetrating or tumor-targeted agent. The Glutamic acid residue might be leveraged for transport via amino acid transporters or cleaved by tumor-specific enzymes to release the active drug, indicating potential applications in targeted cancer therapy.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: N-[[4-[6-[(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-ylhydrazinylidene)methyl]imidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-2-yl]phenyl]methylideneamino]-4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-amine, also referred to as Compound 2 (H2), is a complex bis-imine and hydrazone derivative. The core structure includes an imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine moiety, a phenyl group, and two 4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazole rings. Imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine is a fused bicyclic diazaheterocyclic system known for its fluorescent properties and diverse biological activities. The molecule contains two 4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazole (imidazolinium) groups, which are connected to the main chain via a hydrazone (hydrazinylidene) and an imine (methylideneamino) linkage, respectively. The imidazolinium structure is often used as a ligand or a biologically active unit. This complex poly-nitrogen aromatic structure recognizes GG internal loops in r(CGG)exp hairpins and binds r(CUG)exp, partially restoring normal reporter signal in DM1 cells, although it is less selective (binding CGG/CCG/A-site RNAs) and shows high-dose cytotoxicity, serving as a tool compound for FXTAS/DM1 therapeutic development, and suggesting potential applications as a fluorescent probe, a ligand for biomacromolecules, or a drug candidate with anticancer or antiviral properties.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (7S,9S)-7-[(2R,4S,5R,6S)-4-amino-5-hydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxy-6,9,11-trihydroxy-9-(2-hydroxyacetyl)-4-methoxy-8,10-dihydro-7H-tetracene-5,12-dione, also referred to as Epirubicin, is the chemical name for, or a very close analogue of, the anthracycline antibiotic Epirubicin, which is an anthracycline chemotherapeutic agent that intercalates into DNA, inhibiting topoisomerase II and causing DNA strand breaks; Epirubicin is a widely used chemotherapy drug in clinical practice for treating various cancers such as breast cancer and ovarian cancer, and its mechanism involves DNA intercalation and Topoisomerase II inhibition.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (4S,4aS,5aS,6S,12aR)-4-(dimethylamino)-1,6,10,11,12a-pentahydroxy-6-methyl-3,12-dioxo-4,4a,5,5a-tetrahydrotetracene-2-carboxamide, also referred to as nitrogen-heterocyclic aromatic compound, is the core structure or a derivative of a tetracycline antibiotic, which is a broad-spectrum antibiotic that binds to the 30S ribosomal subunit, blocking tRNA attachment and inhibiting protein synthesis by interacting with bacterial rRNA but not directly with mRNA or other RNA types; tetracycline has a rigid hydrophobic skeleton with four rings connected together, the side chains of C-10 phenol dione and C-1 to C-3 triketone formamide provide negative charge and hydrogen bond donors, this planar aromatic region can be stacked at the single-stranded/double-stranded junction of RNA, meanwhile, magnesium ions mediate the electrostatic bridging between its dimethylamino group and the phosphate skeleton, inducing conformational distortion at the active site and demonstrating a non-competitive inhibitory function for Class I intron splicing; tetracyclines are clinically important broad-spectrum antibiotics used in reality to treat various bacterial infections like pneumonia, acne, and Lyme disease, by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[11-[3-(dimethylamino)propylamino]-5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]ethyl-trimethylazanium diiodide, this compound is a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline derivative with long diamine and quaternary ammonium salt side chains. The presence of the quaternary ammonium salt typically confers higher polarity and DNA binding affinity, making it a potential potent anti-tumor agent or a fluorescent probe in biological research.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[5-(1,2,4-triazol-1-ylmethyl)-1H-1,2,4-triazol-3-yl]pyridine is a poly-nitrogen heterocyclic compound featuring two 1,2,4-triazole rings and one pyridine ring. One 1,2,4-triazole ring (connected to the pyridine ring) serves as the core, while the other 1,2,4-triazole ring is linked to the 5-position of the core triazole ring via a methylene bridge. The triazole ring is an important pharmacophore, widely present in antifungal drugs and antivirals. This molecule is rich in nitrogen atoms, granting it strong hydrogen bonding and coordination capabilities, suggesting potential biological activities such as antifungal, antiviral, or anticancer effects, or applications as a metal coordinating agent.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a conserved stem-bulge-loop hairpin at the 5′ end of all HIV-1 transcripts, existing as 29–59-nt fragments. Its structure features a double-stranded stem, a 3-base pyrimidine bulge (e.g., UCU), and a 6-nt loop. Functionally, it binds Tat’s arginine-rich segment, recruits P-TEFb, boosts RNA Pol II processivity (viral gene expression >100-fold), aids reverse transcription, and is a key therapeutic target for HIV infections.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-6-phenyl-N-[3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl]thieno[2,3-b]pyridine-2-carboxamide, also referred to as compound 4, is a fused heterocyclic amide based on the thieno[2,3-b]pyridine scaffold, and it is also a 3-aminothieno[2,3-b]pyridine-2-carboxamide derivative characterized by a phenyl substituent at the 6-position, a structural modification critical for its enhanced potency as a TAR RNA binder. This tricyclic backbone features an amino group at position 3 and a phenyl group at position 6. It is linked via an amide bond to a 3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl substituent. The molecule is highly lipophilic, thanks to the phenyl group and the crucial trifluoromethyl (CF3) group. The trifluoromethyl group is a classic substituent in medicinal chemistry, used to enhance metabolic stability and transmembrane permeability, while also acting as a strong electron-withdrawing group. This planar, highly lipophilic amidic heterocycle is a prime example of a small molecule drug, often investigated as a non-competitive or competitive inhibitor of signal pathway enzymes like tyrosine kinases, with broad applicability in anti-cancer, immunomodulatory, and anti-inflammatory fields; additionally, it specifically interacts with key nucleobases in the HIV-1 TAR RNA elementG21, U23, and G43demonstrating dual binding affinity for both the bulge region (U23) and the lower stem (G43), a unique interaction profile that establishes it as a promising lead molecule for developing antiviral therapeutics that target RNA-protein interactions.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(4-chlorophenyl)-7-(4-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl)chromen-2-one, also referred to as C20 (a non-planar coumarin analog with a piperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system carrying a 1 charge), is another cationic coumarin derivative. It retains the fluorescent coumarin property, the cationic 4-methylpiperazin-4-ium group, and the para-chlorophenyl group at C3, but lacks the fluorine atom at C5, and can recognize the single-G bulge of RNA1, with this binding driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics (confirmed by FP and SAR comparison), serving as a selective RNA ligand; the compounds cationic nature allows it to target negatively charged cellular components, while the coumarin scaffold provides potential as a fluorescent probe for bioimaging, and its biological activity is likely centered around enzyme inhibition or receptor modulation, such as anticoagulant effects or kinase inhibition.

RNA information: (CUG)12 is an RNA model of expanded pathogenic CUG repeats in myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1). Structurally, it forms a stable A-form hairpin with UU mismatches in its double-stranded region, stabilized by base-pairing and phosphate backbone interactions. Functionally, it high-affinity sequesters MBNL1 protein, disrupting alternative splicing, causing ribonuclear foci accumulation and pre-mRNA mis-splicing (e.g., insulin receptor exon 11 exclusion), via a toxic gain-of-function mechanism, making it a DM1 therapeutic target.
Small_molecule_information: 9-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)amino]butylamino]-N-methylacridine-2-carboxamide, also referred to as Acridine 2, is the representative monomeric ligand for this study, containing a 2-carboxamide group and combining two key pharmacophores: the acridine core and the diaminotriazine group, linked by a butylamine chain; it has modest activity, is cell-impermeable, with acridine derivatives being classic DNA intercalators, while the diaminotriazine structure is analogous to the active portion of folic acid antagonists, and this compound is designed as a dual-mechanism drug: the acridine intercalates into DNA and may inhibit topoisomerases, while the triazine component may act as a folate antagonist, consequently, it is studied for potential anticancer and antimalarial activity.

RNA information: r(CUG)2 is an RNA motif found in the expanded CUG repeat RNA (r(CUG)exp) that causes myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1). It forms a stable stem-loop structure with two CUG repeats and can specifically bind to muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein, leading to its sequestration and the resulting misregulation of alternative splicing. This RNA-protein interaction contributes to the molecular pathology of DM1, and small molecules targeting r(CUG)2 can disrupt the complex and restore splicing defects in disease models.
Small_molecule_information: (E,2S,11S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,11-bis[[(2S)-1-(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)pyrrolidine-2-carbonyl]amino]dodec-6-enediamide is a complex, symmetrical dimeric peptidic analog. Its structure is based on a C12 diene-diamide (dodec-6-enediamide, E-configured double bond) core, with the 2 and 11 positions linked to two N-(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)proline units, and the termini connected to (2S)-2-(3-aminopropylamino)-6-aminohexanoyl (a lysine derivative) groups. It incorporates the benzo[g]quinoline aromatic heterocycle and contains multiple chiral centers and amide bonds. This compound has an amphiphilic structure. The benzo[g]quinoline core confers stronger aromatic stacking and hydrophobic interaction capabilities, and the two free amino groups (on the side chain and terminus of the lysine derivative) likely make it cationic at physiological pH. In terms of biological function, peptidic analogs generally have the potential to act as receptor ligands or enzyme inhibitors; its cationic and hydrophobic nature may confer membrane permeability or the ability to bind negatively charged biomolecules (such as DNA/RNA), and benzo[g]quinoline compounds, like their quinoline isomers, typically possess diverse biological activities.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 4-aminobenzoic acid is commonly known as p-Aminobenzoic acid (PABA), an amino acid and carboxylic acid derivative, structured as a benzoic acid molecule with an amino group at position 4 of the benzene ring, molecular formula C7H7NO2, and 4-Aminobenzoic acid is an aromatic amino acid characterized by an amino group and a carboxyl group attached to a benzene ring, in terms of real-world activity, PABA plays a crucial role as a biochemical precursor in microorganisms and plants, it is an essential intermediate required by bacteria for the synthesis of folic acid, sulfonamide drugs (e.g., sulfamethoxazole) exert their potent antibacterial effect by structurally mimicking PABA and competitively inhibiting the key enzyme (dihydropteroate synthase) in the bacterial folic acid synthesis pathway, for humans, PABA is a non-essential nutrient, but its derivatives were once widely used in sunscreens for their effective UVB absorption properties, the figure indicates that 4-aminobenzoic acid has weak binding affinity and does not induce gene expression, suggesting that it may not be an active ligand for 108yjdF RNA.
