Small_molecule_information: 3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-7-[(2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidin-1-ium-4-yl)amino]chromen-2-one, also referred to as SMSM30, is a non-planar coumarin analog with a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge, which is based on the coumarin core with the 8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl group at C3, the C7 substituent is the (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidin-1-ium-4-yl)amino group, connected via an amine linker, the four methyl groups on the piperidine ring impart significant steric bulk and lipophilicity, while the quaternized nitrogen maintains a permanent positive charge, this tetramethylpiperidine structure is related to the TEMPO radical parent scaffold, suggesting potential use in anti-oxidative stress applications or as a mitochondria-targeting agent, since this type of bulky, lipophilic cation readily accumulates in mitochondria, and it recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand, which could be explored as a novel anti-inflammatory or anti-cancer agent.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[(7-chloroquinolin-4-yl)amino]-6-(diethylaminomethyl)phenol;dihydrochloride, also referred to as Quinacrine Di HCl, is a quinoline-phenol heterocyclic compound presented as the dihydrochloride salt, which is an antimalarial and antiprotozoal drug with DNA intercalation properties and has been repurposed for cancer and prion diseases due to its ability to inhibit protein aggregation and nucleic acid interactions, its structure featuring a 4-aminoquinoline backbone with a 7-chloro substitution is chemically analogous to classic antimalarial drugs like chloroquine, the presence of both a phenolic hydroxyl group and a diethylaminomethyl group on the phenyl ring provides multiple sites for interaction and protonation, quinolines are a broad class of compounds known for antimalarial, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, due to its salt form the molecule is highly protonated enhancing its water solubility and the functional groups suggest potential for metal ion chelation, in reality this compound is likely explored as a novel antimalarial candidate to combat drug resistance or possibly as an anticancer agent due to its potential to interfere with metal homeostasis or kinase activity.

RNA information: 5'CCG/3'GCC is a 1×1 CG internal-loop RNA motif of the 5'CNG/3'GNC family, with a short stem loop (5'CCG/3'GCC), no U·U mismatches, G-C/C-G pairing, high stability, and low "breathing effects". Pathogenically linked to FXTAS (via mutant FMR1 mRNA’s r(CCG) repeats), it acts as a disease-specific RNA switch, recruiting proteins/ligands (e.g., diguanidinobenzoates) to modulate splicing/RAN translation. It also serves as an experimental control for RNA-small molecule binding specificity.
Small_molecule_information: 8-(9-carboxy-4-formyl-1,3,6-trihydroxyphenazin-2-yl)-6-formyl-4,7,9-trihydroxyphenazine-1-carboxylic acid, also referred to as Dilomofungin, is an extremely complex dimeric molecule, consisting of two highly substituted phenazine units linked by a carbon-carbon bond, which is a spontaneous dimer of lomofungin formed in DMSO and structurally features a carboncarbon bond between two lomofungin monomers. The phenazine scaffold is a heterocyclic system made of two benzene rings fused with two nitrogen atoms, and many natural phenazine derivatives (like pyocyanin) are pigments produced by microorganisms with potent antibiotic and biological redox activities. This compound possesses multiple functional groups, including hydroxyl, carboxyl, and formyl groups, which lend the molecule multiple hydrogen bond donor/acceptor capabilities and high water solubility, and it exhibits higher potency than lomofungin in inhibiting MBNL1-CUG RNA binding in vitro, while also having strong binding affinity and the ability to partially rescue mis-splicing in DM1 cell models, though it has the unintended effect of stabilizing toxic CUG repeat RNA, leading to its accumulation in nuclear foci and reduced transcript turnover, which limits its therapeutic potential for myotonic dystrophy type 1. The phenazine rings establish it as a potent redox-active molecule capable of mediating electron transfer in biological systems. In reality, such structurally complex phenazine derivatives are often explored as novel antibiotics or anticancer agents, where the mechanism of action might involve DNA intercalation or the generation of reactive oxygen species to induce apoptosis. This molecule could also serve as a chelating agent or redox indicator in biochemical analysis.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: (2S)-2-[[4-[(2-amino-4-oxo-3H-pteridin-6-yl)methylamino]benzoyl]amino]pentanedioic acid is the chemical structure for Folic Acid (FA), also known as Vitamin B9 and also referred to as a non-azaaromatic B-vitamin (B9), which is structured with an oxidized pteridine ring, para-aminobenzoic acid (PABA), and a glutamic acid moietyunlike its reduced form tetrahydrofolate (THF), and it lacks planar azaaromatic features, binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis; folic acid is a vital B vitamin required for cell division, DNA synthesis, and red blood cell maturation, it must be biologically activated by reduction to tetrahydrofolate (THF) by the enzyme DHFR, with its core role being a dietary precursor that needs to be converted to THF to act as a one-carbon carrier for nucleotide synthesis and cell division, which is critical for fetal development and red blood cell production; clinically, the compound is widely used as a nutritional supplement, especially for pregnant women to prevent fetal neural tube defects, and for treating megaloblastic anemia caused by folate deficiency, and many anticancer drugs (e.g., methotrexate) are competitive inhibitors of this molecules activation pathway.

RNA information: pre-miR-21 is a stem-loop non-coding RNA precursor with a distinct structure, containing G28-A29 (Dicer-TRBP cleavage site) in the stem and single-stranded bulges/apical loops (small-molecule binding pockets). It processes into oncogenic mature miR-21; ligands like 4a or bPGN  bind specifically, inhibiting Dicer processing, upregulating tumor suppressors (PDCD4, PTEN), and suppressing cancer cell proliferation. It is a druggable target for cancers such as colorectal cancer.
Small_molecule_information: 6-pyrrolidin-1-yl-2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine, also referred to as 3b, is built upon the 2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine core, with a pyrrolidin-1-yl or piperidine group directly attached to the 6-position. The direct attachment of the nitrogen atom allows the ring to influence the electronic properties and basicity of the pyridine system more directly compared to a methylene-linked structure. This class of nitrogen-containing heterocyclic amines is widely studied as scaffolds for kinase inhibitors or GPCR ligands. The ring moiety enhances lipophilicity, which aids in cell penetration and bioavailability, and this compound exhibits good affinity for pre-miR-21 with preferential binding over duplex DNA or tRNA, although it lacks Dicer-mediated pre-miR-21 cleavage inhibition, serving to elucidate structure-activity relationships for miR-21 maturation inhibitors and representing an early-stage research molecule potentially for antitumor, anti-inflammatory, or nervous system applications.

RNA information: 5'CGG/3'GGC is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to fragile X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome (FXTAS), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it forms a hairpin with a 1×1 internal loop, closed by CG pairs, adopting non-canonical folding. Functionally, it contributes to FXTAS pathogenesis, likely by sequestering cellular proteins and disrupting normal splicing. As a disease-relevant RNA, it serves as a target for screening therapeutic small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: 2,3-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium, also referred to as Nitidine, is another methylated benzophenanthridine quaternary alkaloid, similar to 1,2-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium but with different methoxy substitution (2,3-positions), and as a benzophenanthridine alkaloid it also possesses antitumor and antimalarial activity, can inhibit topoisomerases and telomerase to disrupt DNA replication, and its fluorescent properties aid in cellular imaging studies; due to its planar, rigid, and positively charged structural features, its primary activity in practice is as a DNA intercalator and cytotoxic agent, exhibiting strong anti-tumor properties, minor changes in the substitution pattern (from 1,2- to 2,3-dimethoxy) are critical in drug design to modulate the molecules charge distribution and steric bulk, which can alter its interaction strength and selectivity for specific DNA sequences or Topoisomerase isozymes, and this makes it valuable in the structure-activity relationship studies of novel anticancer agents.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[5-methyl-11-(3-piperidin-1-ylpropylamino)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]-N-(3-piperidin-1-ylpropyl)acetamide iodide is a disubstituted, dicationic [1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline derivative with two 3-piperidin-1-ylpropylamine side chains. The dicationic charge and highly conjugated planar core classify it as a potent polycationic intercalator. These compounds are used in the development of DNA minor groove binders and bifunctional topoisomerase inhibitors, exhibiting high anticancer and broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity, particularly against drug-resistant strains.

Small_molecule_information: 7H-purine-2,6-diamine, also referred to as 2,6-diaminopurine (DAP), is a nitrogen-heterocyclic aromatic compound and a planar aromatic base with an additional amino group at the C2 position of adenine, a purine nucleobase where both positions 2 and 6 of the purine ring are substituted with amino groups, molecular formula C5H6N6. In terms of real-world activity, DAP is an important non-natural nucleic acid base with significant biological and pharmacological relevance; through its extended hydrogen bond donor, it forms a denser network with the riboswitch core pocket, and its complex lifetime lasts for several minutes, while it can also be incorporated into the DNA strand, where it forms a more stable three-hydrogen-bond pair with Thymine (T) than the natural two-hydrogen-bond A-T pair, a stronger pairing property that is highly useful in designing DNA oligonucleotide probes and PCR primers with enhanced stability. However, its overly tight combination with the riboswitch core pocket makes it difficult for it to exchange rapidly within the short transcriptional window, and as a purine analog, DAP and its nucleoside derivatives are also investigated for antiviral and anticancer activity, with their cytotoxicity mechanism generally related to interference with intracellular purine metabolism; physiologically, it is more like a calibration key used to calibrate the sensitivity of the system rather than a natural trigger that truly activates gene expression.

RNA information: 5'CNG/3'CNG refers to repetitive RNA sequences (N = any nucleotide), commonly located in 5'/3' UTRs. Structurally, it forms stable hairpin or G-quadruplex structures. Functionally, it regulates post-transcriptional gene expression. Pathogenically, expanded subtypes (e.g., CUG/CAG repeats) cause microsatellite expansion disorders like myotonic dystrophy type 1, sequestering RNA-binding proteins (e.g., MBNL1) to disrupt alternative splicing.
Small_molecule_information: 8-(9-carboxy-4-formyl-1,3,6-trihydroxyphenazin-2-yl)-6-formyl-4,7,9-trihydroxyphenazine-1-carboxylic acid, also referred to as Dilomofungin, is an extremely complex dimeric molecule, consisting of two highly substituted phenazine units linked by a carbon-carbon bond, which is a spontaneous dimer of lomofungin formed in DMSO and structurally features a carboncarbon bond between two lomofungin monomers. The phenazine scaffold is a heterocyclic system made of two benzene rings fused with two nitrogen atoms, and many natural phenazine derivatives (like pyocyanin) are pigments produced by microorganisms with potent antibiotic and biological redox activities. RNA information: The bacterial riboflavin (FMN) riboswitch is located in the 5' untranslated region (5' UTR) of mRNA and consists of an aptamer domain and an expression platform. It can specifically bind to FMN and regulate the expression of the downstream key riboflavin synthesis gene ribB through conformational changes. When FMN is sufficient, the riboswitch forms a terminator/sequestering loop to inhibit gene expression; when FMN is deficient, it switches to an antiterminator/anti-sequestering loop, allowing gene transcription and translation to achieve negative feedback regulation. This riboswitch exists exclusively in bacteria and has no homologous structure in mammals, making it a highly selective target for new antibacterial drugs (such as ribocil).
Small_molecule_information: (2S)-2-amino-N-[12,17-bis[[(2S)-2-amino-5-(diaminomethylideneamino)pentanoyl]amino]-4-pentacyclo[6.6.6.0{2,7}.0{9,14}.0{15,20}]icosa-2(7),3,5,9(14),10,12,15(20),16,18-nonaenyl]-5-(diaminomethylideneamino)pentanamide possesses a highly complex cage-like structure and multiple arginine-derived amidine groups. The pentacyclo[6.6.6.0{2,7}.0{9,14}.0{15,20}]icosane scaffold is often used to construct multivalent ligands. The molecule is a polyamidinated peptide/cage molecule conjugate, likely designed as a high-affinity nucleic acid or protein binding agent, for applications such as gene transfection or as a potent broad-spectrum antiviral/antibacterial agent. The complex structure suggests specialized use in nanomedicine or biomolecular interaction studies.

Small_molecule_information: (2S)-5-hydroxy-2-(3-hydroxy-4-methoxyphenyl)-7-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-[[(2R,3R,4R,5R,6S)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxymethyl]oxan-2-yl]oxy-2,3-dihydrochromen-4-one, also referred to as Hesperidin, is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects commonly found in citrus fruits, and is an extremely complex natural product flavonoid O-diglycoside with a dihydroflavone (flavanone) core substituted at the 7-position with a diglycoside unit (likely involving glucose and rhamnose); the numerous phenolic hydroxyl groups and the bulky glycoside moiety are the primary drivers of its activity, and in practice, such compounds are key functional components in herbal medicines, demonstrating potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging capabilities, while due to its glycosidic structure, it typically has high water solubility and specific oral bioavailability (via gut microbial hydrolysis), and it is widely studied for cardiovascular benefits as well as cardioprotective, antiviral, and hepatoprotective effects, making it a focal point in natural product research.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a conserved stem-bulge-loop hairpin at the 5′ end of all HIV-1 transcripts, existing as 29–59-nt fragments. Its structure features a double-stranded stem, a 3-base pyrimidine bulge (e.g., UCU), and a 6-nt loop. Functionally, it binds Tat’s arginine-rich segment, recruits P-TEFb, boosts RNA Pol II processivity (viral gene expression >100-fold), aids reverse transcription, and is a key therapeutic target for HIV infections.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[3-amino-4-(3-piperidin-1-ylpropoxy)phenyl]-5,7-dimethoxy-1H-quinolin-4-one, also referred to as Phenylquinolone 3 (a class of 2-phenylquinolone derivatives), differs from 2-[3-amino-4-(3-piperidin-1-ylpropoxy)phenyl]-5,7-dimethoxy-1-methylquinolin-4-one in that the N-methyl on the quinolinone ring is replaced by a hydrogen atom (1H-quinolin-4-one)N1R H5,7-R OMeR OMeX On 3R -N-N-HDNATat-TARN1

RNA information: r(GCGC): This model RNA is a GC-rich hairpin with a stem composed of repeating 5'-GC/3'-CG base pairs. Its key functional role in the study was to act as a stringent control for determining the selectivity of the investigated small molecules. Due to its high thermodynamic stability, the stem was shortened to 8 base pairs to make its melting temperature comparable to the longer AU-rich hairpins. Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-5,7-dihydroxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Luteolin (a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects that exhibits antioxidant activity and may influence gene expression), is a naturally occurring flavone compound (distinguished from flavonols like 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-3,5,7-trihydroxychromen-4-one by the lack of a hydroxyl group at C3), with the formula C15H10O6. Its structure features hydroxyl groups on the A ring (5 and 7) and a catechol moiety (3,4-dihydroxyphenyl) on the B ring. Luteolin is widely found in celery, parsley, broccoli, cabbage, and various medicinal herbs. In real-world biological activity, Luteolin demonstrates powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an important active component in traditional herbal medicine and modern supplements. It operates through multiple mechanisms, including free radical scavenging, inhibition of various inflammatory mediators, and modulation of immune cell function. Furthermore, it is extensively studied for its neuroprotective, anti-allergic, and potential anticancer properties, such as by inhibiting tumor cell proliferation and inducing apoptosis.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: benzene-1,2-diol, also referred to as the benzene derivative with two adjacent hydroxyl groups (commonly known as Catechol), is one of the simplest dihydric phenolic compounds, featuring two adjacent hydroxyl groups attached to the benzene ring, and this compound is structurally a benzene derivative with two adjacent hydroxyl (-OH) groups, belonging to dihydroxybenzenes, and has a planar, polar aromatic structure (not an azaaromatic, unlike the documents focus), Catechol is an extremely important chemical structure, widely present in natural products (such as catechins, dopamine, adrenaline), and possesses potent antioxidant and free radical scavenging capabilities, due to its two adjacent hydroxyl groups, catechol is an excellent bidentate ligand, capable of forming stable chelates with many metal ions (e.g., iron, vanadium, chromium), in reality, catechol and its derivatives are used as raw materials for synthesizing pharmaceuticals (such as catecholamine neurotransmitters), pesticides, fragrances, and preservatives, in medicine, it is a crucial structural unit in the Parkinsons disease treatment drug L-DOPA, functionally, no data links it to yjdF riboswitch binding or gene regulation (the documents key topics); generally, it acts as a metabolite or redox agent, but such roles arent mentioned in the given content. RNA information: The r(CUG)₁₀ sequence represents a pathogenic-length model for expanded CUG repeats and folds into a stable hairpin with regularly spaced U–U mismatches. Structurally, it provides multiple equivalent binding sites, enabling one ligand to associate with each trinucleotide repeat and producing cooperative binding. This repeat length captures essential features of toxic RNA found in myotonic dystrophy, including the ability to sequester MBNL1 and form nuclear foci. Functionally, r(CUG)₁₀ is the most relevant in vitro construct for evaluating selective small-molecule recognition, since its length allows the manifestation of cooperative interactions that mimic disease-relevant RNA–protein complexes.
Small_molecule_information: (Z,2R,11R)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,11-bis[[(2S)-2-[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)-propylamino]propanoyl]amino]dodec-6-enediamide, also referred to as DCC 10 (2012), is a structurally massive cis (Z) peptidomimetic dimer that incorporates an extended hydrocarbon bridge via pentenyl glycine residues, producing greater linker length and conformational flexibility which allows the benzo[g]quinoline groups to adopt more favorable orientations for engaging RNA repeats, and this structural extension enhances the ability of the compound to match the geometry of expanded repeat hairpins; functionally, it exhibits strong activity in cellular models, efficiently penetrating cells and rescuing functional phenotypes caused by toxic RNA, and it demonstrates that tuning linker length and flexibility can significantly improve recognition efficiency and biological performance, making it a promising scaffold for therapeutic exploration, while its Z-configuration of the core twelve-carbon chain forces a rigid U-shaped bend in the main chain, significantly altering its overall spatial conformation, and this conformational change is critical for the molecules biological activity as it determines the relative distance and orientation of the two active units (benzo[g]quinoline/lysine side chains) in space, and this specific bent conformation may make it more suitable for binding to DNA regions with a particular curvature or to oligomeric protein receptors, and the molecule maintains the powerful polycationic character and the DNA binding potential of the benzo[g]quinoline groups, thus this compound is designed as a crucial molecule for studying the conformation-activity relationship of large polycationic peptidomimetics, with potential applications as a highly specific anti-cancer agent or gene therapy vector.

Small_molecule_information: 4,11-bis(2-aminoethylamino)naphtho[2,3-f][1]benzothiole-5,10-dione, also referred to as a thiophene-fused analogue of 2a with similar side chains, is an organic compound featuring a complex polycyclic core of naphthoquinone and benzothiole, forming the naphtho[2,3-f][1]benzothiole-5,10-dione skeleton. RNA information: The NRQ is a 18-nucleotide RNA oligomer corresponding to the wild-type, guanine-rich sequence (5′-GGGAGGGGCGGGUCUGGG-3′) located in the 5′-UTR of the human NRAS proto-oncogene mRNA. This sequence possesses the intrinsic ability to fold into a stable, parallel RNA G-quadruplex structure, which has been previously established to act as a translational repressor by inhibiting the initiation of NRAS protein synthesis. In this study, it serves as the primary target structure for the novel MSQ derivatives. Its confirmed G-quadruplex formation, characterized by a specific CD spectrum with a positive peak at 262 nm that is enhanced in the presence of potassium ions, and its functional role make it essential for evaluating the binding affinity and specificity of potential ligands designed to stabilize this structure and further repress translation.
Small_molecule_information: N-([1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl)-N,N-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine, also referred to as the previously synthesized quindoline derivative that served as the control compound in the study, is a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl)-N,N-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine, a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline derivative with a diamine side chain; this control compound once exhibited very strong binding affinity for the NRAS RNA G-quadruplex (NRQ) but demonstrated a profound lack of selectivity as it also bound significantly to the NRQ mutant which cannot form a canonical G-quadruplex structure, and this non-selective binding profile, likely due to strong, non-specific electrostatic interactions from its cationic nature, underscores the major drawback of previous-generation G-quadruplex ligands, while this structural class is known for DNA/RNA binding and topoisomerase inhibition, with many such compounds developed as broad-spectrum anticancer agents and its real-world applications may involve their use as novel anti-tumor leads or fluorescent probes for cancer therapy or biological research.

Small_molecule_information: 8-(dimethylamino)-10-(5-hydroxypentyl)-7-methylbenzo[g]pteridine-2,4-dione, also referred to as BRX247, is an alcohol-containing pteridine derivative which is a synthetic analog of FMN (flavin mononucleotide) designed to optimize binding ability with the FMN riboswitch; its structure features the 8-(dimethylamino)-7-methylbenzo[g]pteridine-2,4-dione core, with a pentyl (five-carbon) side chain bearing a terminal hydroxyl group attached at the N10-position, and the structure of this compound is simplified by progressively removing the hydroxyl groups from the ribityl chain of natural FMN, ultimately retaining only an alkyl chain and eliminating the terminal phosphate group, a structural simplification that aims to enhance the molecules passive membrane permeability while reducing dependence on the phosphate group; the molecular formula of this compound is CHNO, its side chain is a fatty alcohol, and although it retains the ability to bind to the FMN riboswitch, its binding affinity is relatively weak, additionally exhibiting low activity in transcription termination assays, indicating limited capability in regulating gene expression; nevertheless, research on this compound provides an important structural basis and functional guidance for the subsequent development of more effective FMN analogs, and in terms of biological function, it may serve as a lipophilic precursor or analog of flavin coenzymes in metabolism.

RNA information: The expanded RNA repeat r(G₄C₂)ₑₓₚ, the genetic cause of c9ALS/FTD, dynamically folds between a G-quadruplex and a hairpin structure featuring 1x1 nucleotide GG internal loops. The hairpin form is the predominant and functionally relevant structure within cells, where it mediates disease pathogenesis by two key mechanisms: it sequesters RNA-binding proteins into nuclear foci, and it undergoes repeat-associated non-ATG (RAN) translation to generate neurotoxic dipeptide repeat proteins. This specific hairpin conformation, particularly its GG internal loops, is thus a critical therapeutic target for ameliorating toxicity, as its selective targeting inhibits both protein sequestration and RAN translation.
Small_molecule_information: 4-(2-aminoethoxy)-2-N,6-N-bis[4-(2-aminoethoxy)quinolin-2-yl]pyridine-2,6-dicarboxamide, also referred to as PDS cPDS, is a large, linear polyamine compound centered on a pyridine-2,6-dicarboxamide core, which links two substituted quinoline rings via amide bonds, with an additional substituted aminoethoxy group at the 4-position of the pyridine ring. The quinoline and pyridine moieties are nitrogen-containing heteroaromatics that contribute planarity and conjugation. The most significant feature is the presence of three 2-aminoethoxy substituents, introducing multiple primary amine groups that confer strong polycationic properties upon the molecule, making it highly protonated and positively charged at physiological pH. This multicationic structure and extended planar scaffold are common design elements for novel DNA/RNA binding agents, which interact with nucleic acids via electrostatic and intercalation mechanisms. Such compounds and their analogues are often explored for antiviral, antibacterial properties, and as novel non-viral gene transfection reagents or nucleic acid function inhibitors; additionally, PDS cPDS are potent and selective G-quadruplex-stabilizing small molecules that demonstrated excellent binding to the r(G4C2) G-quadruplex, however, they were ineffective at inhibiting RAN translation, providing key evidence that the G-quadruplex is not the therapeutically relevant structure for this disease mechanism.

Small_molecule_information: 4-benzylsulfanyl-6-fluorocinnoline, also referred to as CP6, is a compound based on the cinnoline scaffold, substituted with a benzylsulfanyl group at position 4 and a fluorine atom at position 6, with the formula C15H11FN2S, and CP6 is characterized by a naphthalene fused with a nitrogen-rich heterocycle, featuring a fluorine atom and a benzyl group attached via a thioether linkage, cinnoline derivatives are often explored as kinase inhibitors and antitumor agents, the sulfide group (S) is a common bioisostere in medicinal chemistry, this molecule is likely investigated as a potential tyrosine kinase inhibitor (e.g., targeting EGFR), aiming to disrupt growth factor receptor signaling pathways to inhibit cancer cell growth, or as a potential treatment for cardiovascular diseases, this design resulted in excellent specificity for the AA-mismatched RNA, a significant stabilizing effect on the duplex, and potent anti-aggregation activity, making it a top lead.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-benzoyl-9-methyl-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 5, which contains a methyl group on the chromene ring and showed reasonable binding to CAG repeat RNA as part of the initial screening and helped establish structure-activity relationships, is a complex polycyclic ketone compound derived from the fused chromeno[4,3-b]pyridine core. The molecule is substituted with three major aromatic groups: an amino group at position 3, a benzoyl group at position 2, and a phenyl group at position 4, alongside a methyl group at position 9. The benzoyl and phenyl moieties contribute to high lipophilicity and bulk, facilitating cell membrane permeation and interaction with hydrophobic binding pockets of targets. The amino and ketone groups provide sites for hydrogen bond donation and acceptance, crucial for specific non-covalent binding with biological targets. Chromene-based structures are the foundation for many natural products and synthetic drugs, often exhibiting broad biological activities such as anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Structurally, this compound is a candidate for development as a kinase inhibitor, aiming to exert anti-tumor or immunomodulatory effects by disrupting intracellular signaling pathways.

RNA information: The HIV-1 frameshift-stimulating stem-loop (FSS) RNA is a highly conserved structured element located within the gag-pol transcript that regulates production of the essential Gag-Pol polyprotein through a −1 ribosomal frameshift. It consists of a stable stem-loop positioned downstream of a slippery sequence that induces ribosomal pausing and promotes frameshifting in about 5–10% of translation events. This process allows translation to continue beyond the gag stop codon, producing Gag-Pol proteins such as protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase that are required for infectious virion assembly. Altering FSS activity disrupts the Gag to Gag-Pol ratio, substantially reducing viral replication and infectivity.
Small_molecule_information: N-[(E,2S,7S)-1,8-bis[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]carbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-7-[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)amino]-1,8-dioxooct-4-en-2-yl]-2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carboxamide, also referred to as Compound 1E (the parent, non-methylated scaffold in the series that serves as a baseline for comparison), is a complex, symmetrical peptidic analog that binds to the HIV-1 frameshift-stimulating RNA with moderate affinity and shows only limited selectivity versus DNA or tRNA, whose relatively low potency highlights the need for structural modification to achieve better performance and retains fluorescence properties that enable quantitative binding studies but lacks the conformational restriction and hydrophobic character conferred by N-methylation, which ultimately reduces its ability to penetrate cells efficiently and to exhibit antiviral activity; its structure is based on a C8 diene-diamide (oct-4-ene-diamide, E-configured double bond) central core, with the 2 and 7 positions linked to units containing (2S)-phenylalaninyl-3-aminopropylamine and (2S)-proline fragments, both termini are acylated by the 2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl group, and compared to N-[(E,2S,7S)-1,8-bis[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]-methylcarbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-7-[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)-methylamino]-1,8-dioxooct-4-en-2-yl]-2-ethyl-N-methylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carboxamide, the N-methyl substitution is removed (that is, there are no methyl groups on the phenylalanine amine and the two benzo[g]quinoline amide bonds), it incorporates the benzo[g]quinoline aromatic heterocycle and contains multiple chiral centers and amide bonds, adopts a non-planar, flexible conformation, the benzo[g]quinoline core confers stronger aromatic stacking and hydrophobic interaction capabilities, the 3-aminopropylamine group enhances hydrophilicity and cationic properties, and in terms of biological function, peptides and their analogs generally have the potential to act as receptor ligands, enzyme inhibitors, or signaling molecules, and benzo[g]quinoline compounds typically possess diverse biological activities.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-5,7-dihydroxy-3-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-[[(2R,3R,4R,5R,6S)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxymethyl]oxan-2-yl]oxychromen-4-one, also referred to as Rutin, is a naturally occurring flavonoid glycoside found in plants, known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasoprotective properties; Rutin is widely used as a dietary supplement and medicinal agent due to its potent antioxidant and free-radical scavenging activities, it stabilizes blood vessels, reduces capillary fragility, may protect against oxidative stress-related diseases, clinically it is known for its Vitamin P activity, primarily used to strengthen capillary walls, reduce capillary permeability, and treat conditions like varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and capillary fragility, it also exhibits anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties, and its poor water solubility limits bioavailability, but derivatives are explored for enhanced delivery.

RNA information: This RNA serves as a defined model for the pathogenic expanded repeat in FXTAS. It folds into a stable intramolecular hairpin structure characterized by repeating 1x1 nucleotide GG internal loops. These loops adopt a specific syn-anti conformation that forms three hydrogen bonds, contributing to the hairpin's significant thermodynamic stability (ΔG°₃₇ ≈ -5.6 kcal/mol). This structured hairpin acts as a high-affinity binding platform, effectively sequestering proteins like DGCR8 and Sam68. The primary function of this RNA in the study is to demonstrate that its stable self-structure poses a major energetic barrier for duplex formation with complementary oligonucleotides, a key finding that highlights the challenge of targeting it with antisense approaches.
Small_molecule_information: N-(3-azidopropyl)-4-[3-[6-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as Compound 2 (Ht-N3, a bis-benzimidazole derivative with Hoechst 33258 core, 2-(piperidin-1-yl)ethoxy side chain, azide group, and 4-fluorophenylethyl tail replacing FMNs ribose-phosphate), is an azide-containing bis-benzimidazole probe without the tert-butyl groups (compared to N-(3-azidopropyl)-4-[2,6-ditert-butyl-4-[6-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide), its core activity remains the same: strong DNA binding, and it additionally binds 5CGG/3GGC and pre-miR-210, disrupts r(CGG)-DGCR8, restores SMN2/Bcl-x splicing, inhibits tumor growth, has low cytotoxicity while tRNA reduces its selectivity, serving as a probe/lead for FXTAS, CGG-repeat disorders, and miR-210-driven cancers; its real-world activity is as a Click Chemistry probe for nucleic acid binding studies and labeling, which enables researchers to map the interaction site of the bis-benzimidazole unit in a biological context using the high specificity of the azide group.

Small_molecule_information: 3-N,3-N,7-N,7-N-tetramethyl-4-nitro-3H-phenothiazine-3,7-diamine, also referred to as the compound with a tricyclic phenothiazine core where a sulfur atom bridges two benzene rings, bearing 3,7-tetramethyldiamino groups and a 4-nitro substituent and exhibiting aromatic, electron-deficient, and polar features, is a nitro-substituted phenothiazine derivative, structurally similar to Methylene Blue; functionally, no links to RNA targets (e.g., pre-miR-21, r(GC)) or related biological activities (e.g., RAN translation inhibition) are reported, leaving its specific roles uncharacterized in the given context, and it is used in reality as a redox indicator, biological stain, antimalarial drug, and a potential therapeutic for inhibiting Tau protein aggregation in Alzheimers disease.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 7-(1,4-diazepan-4-ium-1-yl)-3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)chromen-2-one, also referred to as SMSM55, is a non-planar coumarin derivative where the substituent at position 7 is the 1,4-diazepan-4-ium-1-yl group, carrying a 1 charge and possessing a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system; this is a seven-membered heterocycle containing two nitrogen atoms, with one of the nitrogens bearing a positive charge, the flexibility of the seven-membered ring aids in accommodating various binding pockets, and the two nitrogen atoms provide additional sites for hydrogen bond donation or acceptance, the quaternary ammonium positive charge is a key feature for improving water solubility and electrostatic binding, the combination of the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine group and the coumarin core suggests its potential as a specific kinase inhibitor or receptor ligand, and it can recognize the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, thus serving as a selective RNA ligand, these molecules are often studied as candidates for antipsychotic drugs or anti-cancer agents, given the broad activity profile of the diazepane structure.

RNA information: NRQ mutant is a G-base mutant of NRQ that prevents the formation of the G4 structure.
Small_molecule_information: 5-methyl-3-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-N-(2-pyrrolidin-1-ylethyl)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-amine iodide, also referred to as the N5-methylated analogue of 4b-2, is the quaternary ammonium salt based on the benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core. It is designed as a potent cytotoxic agent, likely acting as a DNA intercalator, and is investigated for its structure-activity relationship in antitumor drug development; this derivative is one of the strongest binders in its series for the NRQ, exhibits strong anti-proliferative activity in A375, yet has poor selectivity due to its binding to mutant and hairpin oligomers.

Small_molecule_information: 4-methylbenzenesulfonate;(2Z)-3-methyl-2-[(1-methylquinolin-1-ium-4-yl)methylidene]-1,3-benzothiazole, also referred to as Thiazole Orange, is an organic salt containing quinolinium and benzothiazole heterocyclic rings that intercalates into DNA and RNA, commonly used in flow cytometry and molecular biology for detecting nucleic acids; its structure is based on the quinoline and benzothiazole parent skeletons: a benzene ring is fused with a pyridine ring to form the quinoline skeleton, and a benzene ring is fused with a thiazole ring to form the benzothiazole skeleton, with these two parent nuclei connected by a methine group. In terms of biological function, this class of compounds typically functions as dyes, photosensitizers, or exhibits specific biological enzyme inhibitory activities (Benzothiazoles are important in therapeutic chemistry).
